  Q7 — Cross-source aggregation for E5b (final E5b question)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Background. Same pattern as E1b Q7, E3 Q6, E5 Q7 — classical Parashari canonical default + 1 commentary variant per technique with astrologer-profile toggle.                                                                                                                                                                             For E5b, the four methods and their canonical sources:                                                                                                             

  ┌────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │       Method       │                      Canonical default                       │              Commentary variant              │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Pindayu            │ BPHS Ch.72 (5-harana, locked Q1)                             │ Phaladeepika Ch.7 (3-harana variant)         │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Amshayu            │ BPHS Ch.73 (same 5-harana, locked Q2)                        │ Phaladeepika Ch.7 (2-harana variant)         │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Nisargayu          │ BPHS Ch.74 (standard)                                        │ Varaha Mihira Brihat Jataka commentary       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Jaimini Ayur-dasha │ Jaimini Upadesha Sutras Book 2 + BPHS (thresholds locked Q3) │ Rangacharya Kalpalatha commentary extensions │
  └────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — Canonical defaults only, no astrologer toggles

  Single-source per method. No variant selectability. Simplest.

  Option B — Canonical default + 1 commentary variant per method, astrologer toggle

  Matches E1b Q7 / E3 Q6 / E5 Q7 convention. Each method ships canonical + alternate with astrologer-profile toggle via F2 astrologer_source_preference.

  Option C — Full F8 aggregation (all variants as sibling rules with confidence indicator)

  Higher complexity; matches master design §1.3 Strategy D.

  Option D — Per-method curation (pick best for each)

  Pindayu: BPHS only (Phaladeepika 3-harana too aggressive departure)
  Amshayu: BPHS only
  Nisargayu: BPHS + Varaha Mihira toggle
  Jaimini: JUS + Rangacharya toggle

  Per-method decision rather than uniform.

  Consistency with prior PRDs

  E1b Q7 = Option B (BPHS + Rangacharya variant per Jaimini dasai)
  E3 Q6 = Option B (JUS + Rangacharya variant per Jaimini technique)
  E5 Q7 = Option B (Tajaka Neelakanthi + Varshatantra variant per Tajaka technique)

  Extending Option B to E5b gives uniform astrologer experience across Jaimini (E1b/E3) + Tajaka (E5) + Longevity (E5b) tradition layers.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option B (canonical + Phaladeepika/Rangacharya toggles)
  - Parashara's Light: Option A (BPHS only, no variants)
  - B.V. Raman: Option A primary (BPHS canonical)
  - K.N. Rao: Option B (canonical + commentary variants per method)
  - Astrosage: Option A

  Q7 — Cross-source aggregation for E5b:

  - A) Canonical defaults only, no toggles — simplest; matches PL + Astrosage + Raman.
  - B) Canonical default + 1 commentary variant per method, astrologer toggle — matches E1b Q7 / E3 Q6 / E5 Q7 convention + JH + K.N. Rao. Uniform consistency across
   Jaimini/Tajaka/Longevity tradition layers. ← my recommendation.
  - C) Full F8 aggregation — maximum tooling; higher complexity.
  - D) Per-method curation — mixed approach per method.
  - E) Other.

● E7 Extended Vargas + SBC + Upagrahas — Pass 1 kick-off                                                                                                             

  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Astrologer workbench has a Vargas tab letting them inspect any divisional chart D1-D144. Specialized views: Sarvathobhadra Chakra (SBC) as a 9×9 grid for            muhurtham/prasna/name-selection queries (Lakshmi naming her newborn tests 4 candidate names against SBC grid). Upagrahas panel showing 8 sub-planets with Kerala vs   North Indian school toggle. 5 Sphuta sensitive points (Beeja/Kshetra/Deha/Mrityu/Pranapada) for deeper analysis. Per GAP_CLOSURE extension, Sphutas added to E7     scope (they were flagged as absent).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E7 inserts after natal + panchangam)

  1. Natal chart + panchangam computed
  2. E7 Extended Vargas: Compute 5 new divisional charts beyond existing 16:
    - D72 Dwadashottari (composite D9 × D8)
    - D84 Chaturashittiyamsa
    - D100 Shatamsa
    - D108 Ashtottari Amsa (D12 × D9)
    - D144 Nadi Amsa Extended
  3. E7 Sarvathobhadra Chakra (SBC): Construct 9×9 grid with 28 natchathirams (including Abhijit per DECISIONS 3.7 SBC exception), 12 rasis, 8 weekdays + directions,
   tithi categories, vowels
  4. E7 Upagrahas: Compute 8 sub-planets (Dhuma, Vyatipata, Parivesha, Chapa/Indrachapa, Upaketu, Kala, Yamakantaka, Ardhaprahara) — two school variants available   
  5. E7 Sphutas (GAP_CLOSURE): Compute 5 sensitive points (Beeja, Kshetra, Deha, Mrityu, Pranapada)
  6. Output: structured_positions for extended vargas + upagrahas + sphutas; structured grid for SBC; queryable via multiple APIs

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Extended Varga division algorithm source

  Background. Josi already has 16 standard vargas (D1-D60). E7 adds 5 more: D72, D84, D100, D108, D144. Each needs a deterministic division algorithm — how the 30°  
  of each rasi is subdivided into N parts and mapped to target rasi.

  Classical sources

  Variant A — BPHS Ch.6 + Uttara Kalamrita synthesis (standard)

  - D72 Dwadashottari: BPHS Ch.6 brief + UK Ch.2 detailed. Uses D9 × D8 composite formula: rasi divided into 12 parts; each part maps via specific table.
  - D84 Chaturashittiyamsa: UK Ch.2 + Muhurta Chintamani. 30°/84 = 0°21'25.7" per division; specific rasi-mapping table.
  - D100 Shatamsa: UK Ch.2. 30°/100 = 0°18' per division.
  - D108 Ashtottari Amsa: D12 × D9 composite (12 × 9 = 108). Two-step derivation.
  - D144 Nadi Amsa Extended: Extends Nadi practice (standard Nadi = D150). 30°/144 = 0°12'30" per division.

  Variant B — Kerala Parahita Siddhantha variants

  Kerala tradition uses slightly different division algorithms for some vargas (specifically D84 and D108). Rangacharya commentary notes the divergence.

  Variant C — Modern synthesis (BPHS + UK + Phaladeepika)

  Hybrid — each varga picks the most-cited classical source per varga.

  Variant D — Research to verify exact classical citations per varga

  Deep dive into UK Ch.2 + BPHS Ch.6 primary sources before locking.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (BPHS + UK synthesis) — widely used
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A primary (subset of extended vargas)
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Variant A where extended vargas printed

  Practical impact

  Extended vargas are specialized use-case tools (not daily-reading level). Astrologers requesting them typically cross-check against JH as reference. Matching      
  Variant A ensures cross-verification works.

  Q1 — Extended Varga division algorithm source:

  - A) BPHS Ch.6 + Uttara Kalamrita Ch.2 synthesis (Variant A) — standard modern implementation; matches JH + PL + Astrosage. Same for both user types. ← my
  recommendation.
  - B) Kerala Parahita Siddhantha variants (Variant B) — Malayalam-tradition alternatives for D84/D108.
  - C) Variant A default + astrologer toggle to Kerala (Variant B) — per uniform variant convention.
  - D) Research — verify exact classical citations per varga before locking.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

  Shatamsa, D108 Ashtottari Amsa, D144 Nadi Amsa Extended). Matches JH + PL + Astrosage. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q2 — Sarvathobhadra Chakra (SBC) layout source                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  Background. SBC is a 9×9 grid encoding 28 natchathirams (including Abhijit per DECISIONS 3.7 SBC exception), 12 rasis, 8 weekdays + directions, 8 compass letters,   vowels, tithi categories. Used for muhurtham (auspicious-timing), prasna (horary), and evaluating names/letters (e.g., Lakshmi naming her newborn — story in         PRODUCT_STORIES.md).                                                                                                                                               

  Classical sources with SBC layouts

  Source A — BPHS Ch.86 standard

  BPHS's dedicated Sarvathobhadra chapter. Widely adopted grid layout. Matches modern JH / Astrosage rendering.

  Source B — Prasna Marga (Kerala) layout

  Prasna Marga Ch.12 includes SBC with slightly different letter-cell mapping for name-testing use. Used in Malayalam panchangams.

  Source C — Muhurta Chintamani integration

  Muhurta Chintamani Ch.7 integrates SBC with muhurtham selection. Slight reorientation emphasizing muhurtham cells.

  Source D — BPHS default + Prasna Marga for horary queries

  Use BPHS Ch.86 grid for general viewing; switch to Prasna Marga letter-mapping for name-testing queries (E10 Prasna integration).

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.86 standard only

  Single source. Matches JH + Astrosage + most modern software.

  Option B — Prasna Marga Kerala for Malayalam region users + BPHS default everywhere else

  Regional auto-adapt per DECISIONS 2.1 panchangam convention. Malayalam users get Prasna Marga layout; others get BPHS.

  Option C — BPHS default + astrologer toggle to Prasna Marga/Muhurta Chintamani

  Uniform variant convention (matches E1b Q7 / E3 Q6 / E5 Q7 / E5b Q7).

  Option D — Hybrid: BPHS for grid viewing; Prasna Marga for name-testing queries; Muhurta Chintamani for muhurtham queries

  Source picked based on query context, not astrologer choice.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.86 is the canonical source. Prasna Marga/Muhurta Chintamani integrations are downstream applications, not different grids.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Source A (BPHS Ch.86 standard)
  - Parashara's Light: Source A
  - Astrosage: Source A
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Source A
  - Malayalam panchangams: Source A or slight variant

  Q2 — SBC layout source:

  - A) BPHS Ch.86 standard only — matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Tamil Vakya. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Regional auto-adapt — Malayalam region users get Prasna Marga variant; others BPHS.
  - C) BPHS default + astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - D) Query-context hybrid — BPHS viewing + Prasna Marga names + Muhurta Chintamani muhurtham.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

  Q3 — Upagrahas Kerala vs North Indian school default                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  Background. Josi already has Gulika and Mandi. E7 adds 8 more upagrahas (sub-planets computed from Sooriyan and day/night fractions). Two schools compute these      with different formulas — not just different names.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  The 8 upagrahas

  Dhuma · Vyatipata (Pata) · Parivesha · Chapa (Indrachapa) · Upaketu · Kala · Yamakantaka · Ardhaprahara

  Classical source variants

  North Indian school

  Computes 8 upagrahas with formulas from BPHS Ch.5 + Jataka Parijata. Uses day-duration and night-duration fractions specifically. Standard in North Indian
  Parashari software + Tamil Vakya panchangams.

  Kerala school

  Kerala Parahita Siddhantha computes 6 upagrahas (Dhuma, Vyatipata, Parivesha, Chapa, Upaketu + one variant). Different day/night fraction formulas per Kerala      
  Prasna Marga + Kalaprakasika commentaries.

  Options

  Option A — North Indian school default + Kerala school as astrologer toggle

  Matches uniform astrologer-toggle convention. North Indian is wider adoption (JH + PL + Astrosage + Tamil Vakya all default North Indian). Kerala users toggle via 
  profile.

  Option B — Kerala school default + North Indian toggle

  Kerala-first. Matches Malayalam panchangam + Prasna Marga user base. Divergent from JH/PL/Astrosage default.

  Option C — Regional auto-adapt

  Malayalam region users → Kerala school default; others → North Indian default. Astrologer can toggle regardless.

  Option D — Both always computed; UI surfaces per region with astrologer override

  All 8 North Indian + 6 Kerala computed. UI shows region-appropriate by default. Astrologer sees both panels side-by-side.

  Option E — Compute both always; astrologer explicitly selects; B2C fixed to North Indian

  Per consistency with other locked decisions that split B2C (single path) vs astrologer (configurable).

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: North Indian default + Kerala toggle
  - Parashara's Light: North Indian only
  - Astrosage: North Indian only
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: North Indian school
  - Malayalam panchangams (Kolla Varsham) : Kerala school

  Q3 — Upagrahas school default:

  - A) North Indian default + Kerala astrologer toggle — matches JH + uniform variant convention. Same pattern as other E-PRD decisions. ← my recommendation.        
  - B) Kerala default + North Indian toggle — Kerala-first.
  - C) Regional auto-adapt — Malayalam users get Kerala; others North Indian.
  - D) Both computed, both surfaced — no default; astrologer picks.
  - E) Compute both; B2C North Indian fixed; astrologer picks — split by user type.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ option A

  Q4 — 5 Sphutas classical source + formulas (GAP_CLOSURE extension)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
  Background. Per GAP_CLOSURE Section 1.B + 1.K, E7 gained 5 Sphuta sensitive points (flagged as absent from original E7 despite Maitreya-parity requirement). These   are computed points (Arabic-Parts-style) used for specific life-area analysis:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  The 5 Sphutas

  ┌──────────────────────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │          Sphuta          │  Sanskrit meaning   │                             Classical usage                              │
  ├──────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Beeja Sphuta (बीज)        │ "Seed point"        │ Male progeny / fertility (father's contribution)                         │
  ├──────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kshetra Sphuta (क्षेत्र)    │ "Field point"       │ Female progeny / maternal contribution                                   │
  ├──────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Deha Sphuta (देह)         │ "Body point"        │ Physical body / health indicator                                         │
  ├──────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Mrityu Sphuta (मृत्यु)      │ "Death point"       │ Mortality window indicator (astrologer-gated per E5b ethical convention) │
  ├──────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Pranapada Sphuta (प्राणपद) │ "Life-breath point" │ Vitality / prana window                                                  │
  └──────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Classical source variants (formulas differ slightly)

  Variant A — BPHS Ch.79-80 + Uttara Kalamrita Ch.2 synthesis (standard)

  ┌───────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Sphuta   │                               BPHS/UK formula                                │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Beeja     │ Sooriyan_long + Sukkiran_long + Guru_long                                    │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kshetra   │ Chandran_long + Sevvai_long + Guru_long                                      │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Deha      │ Chandran_long + Sukkiran_long + Guru_long                                    │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Mrityu    │ Lagna_long + Chandran_long − Sooriyan_long + 180° (with specific adjustment) │
  ├───────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Pranapada │ (Sooriyan_long at sunrise + ghatis elapsed × 15°) + specific offset          │
  └───────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Widely-cited modern standard. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage.

  Variant B — Phaladeepika Ch.27 variant

  Slightly different Pranapada formula (adjusts for local sunrise conventions). Minor differences in Mrityu Sphuta (uses different base point).

  Variant C — Kerala Prashnashastra variant

  Kerala tradition adjusts Mrityu and Pranapada formulas. Used in Malayalam panchangams.

  Variant D — Canonical (Variant A) + astrologer toggle

  Per uniform variant convention (E1b Q7 / E3 Q6 / E5 Q7 / E5b Q7 / E7 Q3).

  Display/ethical considerations

  - Mrityu Sphuta: per E5b ethical gating precedent, astrologer-workbench-only; B2C AI chat declines Mrityu Sphuta queries.
  - Beeja/Kshetra: progeny analysis — generally astrologer-only due to fertility sensitivities.
  - Deha/Pranapada: health indicators — soft gating recommended (share with context; decline specific predictions).

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (BPHS + UK synthesis)
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A (subset — not all 5)
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Variant A
  - Kerala panchangams: Variant C

  Q4 — 5 Sphutas classical source:

  - A) BPHS Ch.79-80 + Uttara Kalamrita Ch.2 synthesis (Variant A) — modern standard; matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Tamil Vakya. Same for both user types (with      
  E5b-style ethical gating for Mrityu). ← my recommendation.
  - B) Phaladeepika Ch.27 variant — minor formula differences.
  - C) Kerala Prashnashastra variant — Malayalam tradition.
  - D) Variant A default + Phaladeepika/Kerala toggles — uniform variant convention.
  - E) Other.

  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q5 — SBC query API scope                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Background. Sarvathobhadra Chakra is queryable for multiple classical use-cases. What queries does E7 expose via API?                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  5 classical SBC query types

  Query type 1 — Name-testing (varna fitness)

  Given a candidate name/letter (e.g., "Aarav" → first syllable "A"), score fitness against user's natal Chandran natchathiram + current moment. Used for naming     
  children (PRODUCT_STORIES.md example), renaming businesses, mantra selection.

  Classical basis: Prasna Marga Ch.12 + Muhurta Chintamani.

  Query type 2 — Natchathiram compatibility

  Given 2 natchathirams (e.g., user's natal + partner's natal, or user's natal + current moment's natchathiram), check SBC grid alignment. Used for marriage matching
   cross-verification (complements E25 Ashtakoota Milan from GAP_CLOSURE).

  Query type 3 — Weekday fitness for activity

  Given a future date + activity category (travel, marriage, business launch), score SBC-alignment for natchathiram+weekday+tithi on that date.

  Classical basis: Muhurta Chintamani Ch.7.

  Query type 4 — Travel/direction auspiciousness

  Given a direction of travel + date, check SBC grid for Dishashoola + directional-auspiciousness.

  Classical basis: Muhurta Chintamani + Prasna Marga.

  Query type 5 — Muhurta selection (date-range search)

  Given activity category + date range (e.g., "best wedding muhurta in June-July 2027"), iterate over date range scoring SBC fitness; return top 10 moments.

  Classical basis: full muhurta selection system; computationally heavy.

  Scope options

  Option A — Full query API (all 5 types)

  Comprehensive classical coverage. Astrologer workbench has dedicated SBC query UI with all 5 query builders. Significant engineering scope.

  Option B — Basic subset (types 1, 2, 3 — name-testing + natchathiram compat + weekday)

  Ships most-used queries; defers travel/direction (type 4) and muhurta search (type 5) to E7-enhancement PRD.

  Option C — Astrologer-only rich query + B2C basic name-testing only

  B2C: only name-testing query (widely-useful consumer feature). Astrologer workbench: all 5 query types.

  Option D — Type 1 (name-testing) MVP only + remaining 4 deferred

  Tight MVP. Name-testing is high-value consumer feature; others defer.

  Option E — Types 1, 2, 3, 4 shipped; type 5 muhurta search deferred

  Full name/compat/weekday/direction querying. Defer expensive muhurta range search.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Types 1, 2, 3, 4 shipped; type 5 has basic date-range search
  - Parashara's Light: Types 1, 3 shipped
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams (printed): Types 1, 3, 4 visible in print

  Q5 — SBC query API scope:

  - A) Full 5 query types — comprehensive; biggest scope.
  - B) Basic 3 (name + compat + weekday) — most-used; deferred rest.
  - C) Astrologer-full + B2C name-testing only — split by user type.
  - D) Name-testing MVP only — tight scope.
  - E) Types 1-4 shipped; type 5 muhurta search deferred — all interactive queries; defer expensive range search. ← my recommendation (matches JH scope; excludes    
  only the expensive range-search feature).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Brewed for 31s
❯ option E                                                                                                                                                                       
● E7 Q5 locked: Option E — SBC query API ships types 1-4: (1) name-testing / varna fitness, (2) natchathiram compatibility, (3) weekday fitness for activity, (4)    
  travel/direction auspiciousness. Type 5 muhurta date-range search deferred to E7-enhancement PRD. Matches JH scope. Same for both user types.

  Q6 — Cross-source aggregation for E7 (final E7 question)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
  Background. Same uniform convention as earlier PRDs (E1b Q7 / E3 Q6 / E5 Q7 / E5b Q7). Canonical default + astrologer-profile toggle where classical variants        exist.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  E7 technique-by-technique variant mapping

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                Technique                 │ Canonical default (locked) │            Potential commentary variant            │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Extended Vargas (D72/D84/D100/D108/D144) │ BPHS Ch.6 + UK Ch.2 (Q1)   │ Kerala Parahita variant for D84/D108               │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sarvathobhadra Chakra                    │ BPHS Ch.86 (Q2)            │ No variant — classical grid is deterministic       │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Upagrahas                                │ North Indian school (Q3)   │ Kerala school (already has toggle per Q3) ✓        │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 5 Sphutas                                │ BPHS + UK synthesis (Q4)   │ Phaladeepika Ch.27 / Kerala Prashnashastra variant │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — Canonical defaults only across remaining techniques (skip adding more toggles)

  Q3 Upagrahas toggle already exists. Don't add more toggles for Extended Vargas or Sphutas. Simpler scope.

  Option B — Extend variant-toggle pattern to all E7 techniques (uniform convention)

  Add Kerala Parahita variant toggle to Extended Vargas (D84/D108); add Phaladeepika variant toggle to Sphutas. Uniform with E1b/E3/E5/E5b/E7-Upagrahas pattern.     

  Option C — Selective addition (Vargas toggle yes; Sphuta toggle no)

  Add Kerala variant to Vargas (since D84/D108 have real classical divergence) but skip Sphuta variant (Phaladeepika differences are minor).

  Option D — Selective addition (Sphuta toggle yes; Vargas toggle no)

  Add Phaladeepika/Kerala variant to Sphutas (ethical-sensitive Mrityu Sphuta deserves astrologer configurability) but skip Vargas variant.

  Uniformity consideration

  E1b Q7, E3 Q6, E5 Q7, E5b Q7 all locked "canonical + commentary variant toggle per technique." E7 Q3 already has Kerala Upagraha toggle. Option B extends
  uniformity.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option B (all variants available as toggles)
  - Parashara's Light: Option A (canonical only)
  - Astrosage: Option A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Option B (variant-rich)

  Q6 — Cross-source aggregation for E7:

  - A) Canonical defaults only, no additional toggles (Q3 Upagrahas toggle stands alone) — matches PL + Astrosage. Simpler.
  - B) Extend variant-toggle pattern to all E7 techniques (Kerala variant for Vargas, Phaladeepika variant for Sphutas) — uniform convention with E1b/E3/E5/E5b.     
  Matches JH + SJVC. ← my recommendation (consistency).
  - C) Vargas toggle yes, Sphuta toggle no — selective.
  - D) Sphuta toggle yes, Vargas toggle no — selective.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

● E8 Western Depth — Pass 1 kick-off

  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Western-leaning users (Western/diaspora astrologers + serious modern Western practitioners) get a dedicated Western Depth tab in the astrologer workbench. Arabic  
  Parts panel (50 Lots, sect-aware per Dorotheus/Paulus/Valens), Fixed Stars (60 ranked from Ptolemy→Brady with classical interpretations), Harmonic Charts
  (H5/H7/H9/H10/H11/H12/H16 toggles), Eclipse catalog (NASA 5-millennium), Uranian midpoints + 8 hypothetical planets. B2C Western-leaning users get AI chat answers
  citing Part of Fortune, Regulus conjunctions, etc. — but framed in Western tradition-isolated language.

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E8 inserts as separate tradition module)

  1. Natal chart computed (E8 uses tropical zodiac by default — distinct from Vedic sidereal)
  2. Western-appropriate house system applied (Placidus default for Western per DECISIONS 1.3; Whole Sign for Hellenistic)
  3. E8 Arabic Parts engine: compute 50 Lots with sect-aware formulas (day-born vs night-born uses different formula sets)
  4. E8 Fixed Stars engine: Swiss Ephemeris swe.fixstar for 60 stars, epoch-adjusted positions, conjunction detection within 1° orb
  5. E8 Harmonic Charts engine: 7 key harmonics (H5/H7/H9/H10/H11/H12/H16)
  6. E8 Eclipse engine: NASA 5-millennium canon, 200yr past + 50yr future, Saros-cycle identification
  7. E8 Uranian engine: 78 midpoints (pairwise half-sums of 10 planets + 3 angles) + 8 hypothetical planets via Witte ephemeris
  8. Output: strict tradition isolation — E8 outputs never merge into Parashari yoga rules or Vedic aggregations

  What's already locked from DECISIONS

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Zodiac default for E8 Western computations

  Background. Western astrology has TWO zodiac conventions:

  - Tropical zodiac — tied to spring equinox (sidereal longitude − ayanamsa offset). Standard in modern Western astrology (Ptolemy-influenced).
  - Sidereal Western (Fagan-Bradley) — fixed-star-aligned sidereal zodiac. Used by Western sidereal practitioners (Cyril Fagan, Donald Bradley).

  Classical Hellenistic (Dorotheus, Valens, Paulus) used tropical. Modern psychological Western (Liz Greene, Steven Forrest) also tropical. Only Western sidereal    
  minority (Fagan tradition) uses sidereal Western.

  Zodiac options

  Option A — Tropical default

  E8 uses tropical zodiac (no ayanamsa). All 5 Western modules (Arabic Parts, Fixed Stars, Harmonics, Eclipses, Uranian) computed in tropical. Matches Hellenistic + 
  modern Western mainstream.

  Option B — Sidereal Western (Fagan-Bradley) default

  E8 uses Fagan-Bradley ayanamsa. Positions align with sidereal fixed stars. Matches minority Western sidereal school.

  Option C — Tropical default + sidereal Fagan-Bradley astrologer toggle

  Pragmatic: tropical for majority + toggle for Western sidereal practitioners. Per uniform variant convention.

  Option D — Astrologer picks at chart creation

  No default; user always selects. Avoids hidden assumption.

  Option E — Hybrid: Arabic Parts + Harmonics tropical; Fixed Stars sidereal (classically must be sidereal)

  Per-technique zodiac selection based on classical usage:
  - Arabic Parts, Harmonics, Uranian midpoints: tropical (Hellenistic/modern Western tradition)
  - Fixed Stars: inherently sidereal (stars don't move tropically)
  - Eclipses: astronomical events (both zodiacs report same dates, different longitudes)

  Classical consensus

  - Hellenistic (Dorotheus, Valens, Paulus): tropical
  - Modern Western mainstream: tropical
  - Fagan-Bradley sidereal school: sidereal Western
  - Fixed Stars inherent: sidereal (stars themselves don't shift with equinox precession)

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire (Western): tropical default + sidereal toggle per chart
  - Kepler: tropical default
  - Astro.com: tropical default
  - Ernst Wilhelm tools: both supported

  Q1 — Zodiac default for E8 Western modules:

  - A) Tropical default — matches Hellenistic + modern Western mainstream. Same for both user types.
  - B) Sidereal Fagan-Bradley default — minority Western sidereal school.
  - C) Tropical default + Fagan-Bradley astrologer toggle — pragmatic; uniform variant convention.
  - D) Astrologer picks at chart creation — no default.
  - E) Per-technique hybrid — tropical for Parts/Harmonics/Uranian; sidereal for Fixed Stars (classically required); eclipses both. ← my recommendation (classically 
  accurate per technique).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Worked for 57s

※ recap: Reviewing Parashari PRDs (10 of ~30 done, Pass 1 astrologer review). Currently locking E8 Western Depth decisions; just asked Q1 on zodiac default for        Western modules. Next: you answer Q1 (A/B/C/D/E).
                                                                                                                                                                     ❯ difference between A and D                                                                                                                                         
                             
● Difference between Option A (Tropical default) and Option D (Astrologer picks at chart creation)                                                                   

  Option A — Tropical default                                                                                                                                           
  Astrologer creates Western chart                                                                                                                                       → System SILENTLY uses tropical zodiac (no prompt, no question)                                                                                                      → All Arabic Parts, Fixed Stars, Harmonics, Eclipses, Uranian computed in tropical                                                                                   → If astrologer wants sidereal, they must explicitly find and toggle a setting                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  Characteristics:
  - Zero friction — astrologer doesn't make a decision each time
  - Hidden assumption — chart doesn't document "which zodiac was used" unless astrologer checks
  - Default matches majority practice (modern Western + Hellenistic = tropical)
  - Risk: Western sidereal astrologer might accidentally use wrong zodiac for first chart

  Option D — Astrologer picks at chart creation

  Astrologer creates Western chart
    → System PROMPTS: "Tropical or Sidereal (Fagan-Bradley)?"
    → Astrologer must select
    → Selection stored with chart; visible in chart metadata
    → All Western modules use selected zodiac

  Characteristics:
  - Explicit decision — astrologer actively selects each chart's zodiac
  - Documented choice — chart records which zodiac was used; auditable
  - Slight friction — extra step at chart creation (~2 seconds)
  - No wrong-default risk — astrologer never accidentally runs in wrong mode
  - Better for mixed practices — astrologers who see both tropical + sidereal clients never confuse

  Side-by-side comparison

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┐
  │                   Factor                   │            Option A             │            Option D            │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Default assumption                         │ Tropical (silent)               │ No default (explicit prompt)   │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Astrologer friction per chart              │ None                            │ +1 decision step               │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Chart metadata records zodiac?             │ Only if astrologer checks       │ Always explicit                │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Risk of "wrong zodiac" accidents           │ Exists for sidereal users       │ Zero                           │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ UX for tropical-only astrologers           │ Fastest                         │ Slightly slower                │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ UX for mixed tropical/sidereal astrologers │ Friction when switching         │ Uniform; always explicit       │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Classical-fidelity signal                  │ Hidden default                  │ Visible choice per chart       │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Matches modern software                    │ Solar Fire / Kepler / Astro.com │ Less common; power-user option │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────┘

  Example scenario

  Astrologer who practices BOTH tropical (modern Western clients) AND sidereal (Fagan-Bradley clients):

  - Option A: Must remember to toggle every time they switch client types. Easy to forget → wrong chart → wrong reading.
  - Option D: System asks every time; never forgets.

  Astrologer who only practices modern Western (95% of Western astrologers):

  - Option A: Fastest — tropical always; no prompts.
  - Option D: Slight friction — answers "tropical" every time; extra click.

  Option C — compromise

  Tropical default + Fagan-Bradley astrologer toggle (per uniform variant convention). Combines A's fast UX with D's ability to override — but the override is at    
  astrologer profile level, not per-chart.

  ---
  Still waiting on Q1 pick — A / B / C / D / E / F?

✻ Sautéed for 57s

● E8 Q1 locked: Option E — Per-technique hybrid zodiac. Arabic Parts / Harmonics / Uranian use tropical (Hellenistic + modern Western tradition). Fixed Stars        
  inherently sidereal (stars don't shift with equinox precession). Eclipses are astronomical events (same dates either zodiac). Most classically accurate per
  technique. Matches Ernst Wilhelm / Solar Fire power-user convention.                                                                                                  
  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q2 — House system default for E8 Western charts                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Background. DECISIONS 1.3 locked Vedic house systems (Whole Sign B2C default + 6-system astrologer shortlist). E8 Western needs its own default — Western astrology   has different conventions.                                                                                                                                        

  Western house system landscape

  ┌───────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      
  │    System     │                                      Used by                                      │                         Notes                         │      
  ├───────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ Placidus      │ Modern Western mainstream (~75%)                                                  │ Default in Solar Fire, Kepler, Astro.com              │      
  ├───────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ Koch          │ German Western tradition                                                          │ Popular in German-speaking markets                    │      
  ├───────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ Porphyry      │ Hellenistic revival                                                               │ Oldest mathematically-divided house system (3rd c CE) │      
  ├───────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ Whole Sign    │ Hellenistic revival + traditional Hellenistic astrologers (Chris Brennan lineage) │ Simple; classically ancient                           │      
  ├───────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ Equal House   │ Some modern Western; Fagan-Bradley sidereal Western                               │ Simple; Ptolemaic                                     │      
  ├───────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ Regiomontanus │ Medieval horary traditions                                                        │ Rarely used for natal                                 │      
  ├───────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤      
  │ Campanus      │ Medieval                                                                          │ Rarely modern                                         │      
  └───────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      

  Options

  Option A — Placidus default (matches modern Western mainstream)

  Most Western software defaults Placidus. Hellenistic revival astrologers prefer Whole Sign but are minority.

  Option B — Whole Sign default (matches Hellenistic revival)

  If E8 targets classical Hellenistic Western practice, Whole Sign is more authentic (Dorotheus, Valens, Paulus all used Whole Sign). Modern psychological Western + 
  tropical-sidereal hybrids prefer Placidus.

  Option C — Placidus default + astrologer-profile toggle to Whole Sign / Porphyry / Koch / Equal

  Uniform variant convention. B2C Western chart = Placidus; astrologer picks per profile.

  Option D — Astrologer picks at chart creation (like Q1 Option D)

  No default; astrologer selects each time.

  Option E — Hellenistic-centric (Whole Sign default) + modern toggle to Placidus

  Classical-fidelity-first. Matches Chris Brennan Hellenistic revival tradition. Modern Western practitioners toggle to Placidus.

  Option F — Tradition-based split

  - If astrologer selects "Hellenistic tradition" on profile: Whole Sign default
  - If "modern psychological Western": Placidus default
  - If "Fagan-Bradley sidereal": Equal House default

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Placidus default
  - Kepler: Placidus default
  - Astro.com: Placidus default + toggle
  - Chris Brennan's tools: Whole Sign default
  - Ernst Wilhelm: Placidus with Whole Sign toggle

  Relationship to DECISIONS 1.3

  Vedic default = Whole Sign. Using same default for Western would be "house-system-uniformity" across traditions but miss Western mainstream convention.

  Classical consensus

  - Hellenistic (classical Western): Whole Sign
  - Modern Western mainstream: Placidus
  - The split: Generational + methodological, not purely classical

  Q2 — E8 Western house system default:

  - A) Placidus default — matches modern Western mainstream + Solar Fire + Kepler + Astro.com. Same for both user types.
  - B) Whole Sign default — matches Hellenistic classical + Chris Brennan revival + consistent with Vedic DECISIONS 1.3.
  - C) Placidus default + astrologer-profile toggle to Whole Sign / Porphyry / Koch / Equal. ← my recommendation (modern Western default with classical flexibility).
  - D) Astrologer picks at chart creation — no default.
  - E) Whole Sign default + astrologer toggle to Placidus — Hellenistic-centric.
  - F) Tradition-based split — Hellenistic profile → Whole Sign; modern → Placidus; sidereal → Equal.
  - G) Other.

  Which?

  Q3 — Arabic Parts sect-aware formulas classical source                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Background. Arabic Parts (Hellenistic Lots) have sect-aware formulas — chart born during day (Sooriyan above horizon) uses one formula; born at night uses a         different formula. Example — Lot of Fortune:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  - Day formula: Lagna + Chandran − Sooriyan
  - Night formula: Lagna + Sooriyan − Chandran (Moon/Sun reversed)

  Classical sources differ slightly on specific formulas across the 50 Lots. Multiple Hellenistic authorities.

  Classical sources

  Source A — Dorotheus (1st c CE)

  Pentateuch of Astrology. Earliest recorded Lot formulas. Project Hindsight + Chris Brennan treat as foundational.

  Source B — Valens (Vettius Valens, 2nd c CE)

  Anthology — most comprehensive Lot derivations. Slight variations from Dorotheus for some Lots.

  Source C — Paulus Alexandrinus (4th c CE)

  Introductory Matters. Most detailed single-source Lot treatment in Hellenistic era. Widely cited by modern Hellenistic revivalists.

  Source D — Al-Biruni (Arabic era, 11th c CE)

  Synthesized Hellenistic Lots into Arabic tradition. Added derived Lots not in Hellenistic originals. Some formula differences from Hellenistic source.

  Source E — Modern synthesis (Project Hindsight / Chris Brennan / Robert Hand)

  Cross-references all Hellenistic sources; picks best-attested formula per Lot. Most defensible scholarly position; used in Hellenistic revival community.

  7 Hermetic Lots (core — all sources agree)

  Fortune · Spirit · Eros · Necessity · Courage · Victory · Nemesis. These 7 are consistent across Dorotheus/Valens/Paulus.

  The formula variance occurs in the 43 additional Lots (Lot of Marriage, Lot of Death, Lot of Children, Lot of Father, Lot of Mother, etc.) — where
  Dorotheus/Valens/Paulus/Al-Biruni disagree on ~5-10% of specific formulas.

  Options

  Option A — Dorotheus primary (earliest classical)

  Use Dorotheus formulas for all 50 Lots. Earliest attestation. Missing formulas (Dorotheus-silent Lots) filled from Valens.

  Option B — Paulus Alexandrinus primary (most detailed)

  Most single-source coverage; Paulus has formulas for 40+ of the 50 Lots.

  Option C — Modern synthesis (Chris Brennan/Project Hindsight)

  Cross-reference all Hellenistic sources; pick best-attested per Lot. Scholarly consensus baseline.

  Option D — Al-Biruni synthesis (Arabic Parts tradition proper)

  Follow Al-Biruni's 11th c Arabic synthesis — which is WHY they're called "Arabic Parts" in Western tradition. Matches medieval Western + Arabic tradition lineage. 

  Option E — Dorotheus default + astrologer-profile toggle to Paulus/Al-Biruni/Modern-synthesis

  Uniform variant convention.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Modern synthesis (Option C)
  - Kepler: Modern synthesis
  - Astro.com: Al-Biruni primary (Option D)
  - Chris Brennan's tools: Modern synthesis per Project Hindsight
  - Ernst Wilhelm: Dorotheus + Paulus synthesis

  Q3 — Arabic Parts classical source:

  - A) Dorotheus primary — earliest classical; Valens fills gaps.
  - B) Paulus Alexandrinus primary — most single-source coverage.
  - C) Modern synthesis (Chris Brennan / Project Hindsight) — cross-references all sources; scholarly consensus. Matches Solar Fire + Kepler + Hellenistic revival   
  mainstream. ← my recommendation.
  - D) Al-Biruni (Arabic Parts proper) — medieval Arabic tradition; matches Astro.com.
  - E) Modern synthesis default + astrologer-profile toggle to Dorotheus / Paulus / Al-Biruni. Uniform variant convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Baked for 33s

❯ option C                                                                                                                                                                      
● E8 Q3 locked: Option C — Modern synthesis (Chris Brennan / Project Hindsight) for Arabic Parts. Cross-references Dorotheus / Valens / Paulus for best-attested     
  formula per Lot. Matches Solar Fire + Kepler + Hellenistic revival mainstream. Same for both user types.

  Q4 — Fixed Stars list selection (60 ranked)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
  Background. E8 PRD §2.1 specifies "60 ranked fixed stars" from Ptolemy → Brady tradition. Which specific 60?                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4 Royal Stars (universally included, Persian "Watchers of the Heavens")                                                                                            

  1. Regulus (α Leo) — royal/leadership
  2. Spica (α Vir) — gifts/blessing
  3. Antares (α Sco) — obsession/extremes
  4. Aldebaran (α Tau) — honor through strife

  Classical core (10 widely-cited beyond Royal Stars)

  Algol (β Per), Sirius (α CMa), Vega (α Lyr), Altair (α Aql), Deneb (α Cyg), Fomalhaut (α PsA), Betelgeuse (α Ori), Rigel (β Ori), Capella (α Aur), Arcturus (α     
  Boo).

  Extended set (remaining 46+ stars to pick from)

  Pollux, Castor, Procyon, Alphard, Bellatrix, Alnilam, Alnilak, Alcyone (Pleiades), Denebola (β Leo), Alphecca (α CrB), Menkar (α Cet), Achernar (α Eri), Canopus (α
   Car), Zubenelgenubi (α Lib), Zubeneschamali (β Lib), Hamal (α Ari), Schedar (α Cas), Algenib (γ Peg), Mirach (β And), Mirfak (α Per), Dubhe (α UMa), Alioth (ε    
  UMa), Alcor (80 UMa), Mizar (ζ UMa), Alphard (α Hya), Ras Alhague (α Oph), Sadalmelik (α Aqr), Sadalsuud (β Aqr), Markab (α Peg), Scheat (β Peg), Sualocin (α Del),
   Enif (ε Peg), Alnath (β Tau), Menkalinan (β Aur), Albireo (β Cyg), Achird (η Cas), Deneb Algedi (δ Cap), Sadachbia (γ Aqr), Alnitak (ζ Ori), Deneb Kaitos (β Cet),
   Kochab (β UMi), Polaris (α UMi), Adhafera (ζ Leo), Regulus variants, Zosma (δ Leo), Kochab, Algorab (δ Crv).

  Source catalogs

  Source A — Ptolemy's Tetrabiblos (2nd c CE)

  Classical Greek/Hellenistic canon. ~60-80 stars with specific meanings.

  Source B — Bernadette Brady's Book of Fixed Stars (modern)

  Expanded Ptolemaic interpretations with modern astrological framework. Widely cited in modern Western practice.

  Source C — Vivian Robson's The Fixed Stars and Constellations in Astrology (20th c)

  Most extensive catalog (~100 stars), synthesizing Hellenistic + Arabic + medieval traditions. Used by Bernadette Brady + modern practitioners.

  Source D — Modern Western astronomy + astrology synthesis

  Solar Fire / Kepler ship ~60-80 stars from combined Brady + Robson + classical sources. Most widely-adopted modern set.

  Options

  Option A — Brady's primary 60 (modern standard)

  Bernadette Brady's Book of Fixed Stars highlights 60 key fixed stars with modern interpretations. Matches most modern Western astrology software selection.        

  Option B — Ptolemy's classical 60

  Stricter Hellenistic canon per Tetrabiblos. Hellenistic revival purists prefer.

  Option C — Robson's extended 60 (most research-comprehensive)

  Vivian Robson selected "most astrologically useful" 60 from ~100 catalog. Synthesizes Arabic + Hellenistic + medieval.

  Option D — Modern synthesis (Brady + Robson + classical) curated 60

  Cross-references all sources; picks 60 most-cited + most-useful. Matches Solar Fire / Kepler selection.

  Option E — Tiered 60 — 4 Royal Stars as "primary" + 16 "core" + 40 "extended" with astrologer filter

  Layered display — B2C sees only 4 Royal Stars + conjunctions; astrologer sees all 60 with filter (primary/core/extended).

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Option D (modern synthesis ~60 stars)
  - Kepler: Option D
  - Bernadette Brady's tools: Option A
  - Ernst Wilhelm: Option D

  Q4 — Fixed Stars list selection:

  - A) Brady's primary 60 — modern standard.
  - B) Ptolemy's classical 60 — Hellenistic purist.
  - C) Robson's extended 60 — research-comprehensive.
  - D) Modern synthesis curated 60 — matches Solar Fire + Kepler. ← my recommendation.
  - E) Tiered 60 — 4 Royal + 16 core + 40 extended with astrologer filter. Split by user type (B2C sees Royal; astrologer sees all).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

  Q5 — Harmonic Charts scope                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Background. E8 PRD §2.1 specifies 7 key harmonics: H5 · H7 · H9 · H10 · H11 · H12 · H16. Each harmonic transforms the natal chart by multiplying all planet          longitudes by N and taking modulo 360°, revealing latent patterns.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
  The 7 PRD-specified harmonics (Addey tradition)

  ┌────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Harmonic  │       Traditional interpretation       │                                   Vedic parallel (if any)                                    │
  ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ H5 (5th)   │ Quintile patterns — talent, creativity │ —                                                                                            │
  ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ H7 (7th)   │ Septile patterns — spiritual, mystical │ —                                                                                            │
  ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ H9 (9th)   │ Novile — marriage                      │ Parallel to Vedic Navamsa (D9) — philosophically same: "how inner marriage/dharma manifests" │
  ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ H10 (10th) │ Decile — career fulfillment            │ Parallel to Vedic Dasamsa (D10)                                                              │
  ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ H11 (11th) │ Elftile — friendship/group dynamics    │ —                                                                                            │
  ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ H12 (12th) │ Undecile — karmic / hidden patterns    │ Parallel to Vedic Dwadashamsa (D12)                                                          │
  ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ H16 (16th) │ Sixteenth harmonic — tests, crises     │ —                                                                                            │
  └────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Classical source weighting

  - John Addey (1958–1983): Harmonics in Astrology, Selected Writings — founder of modern harmonic theory. Proposed 7+ harmonics as primary.
  - Michael Harding (Working with Astrology): extended harmonic analysis
  - Charles Harvey (Anima Mundi): harmonic synthesis
  - Richard Tarnas (Cosmos and Psyche): harmonic interpretation framework

  Scope options

  Option A — 7 PRD-specified harmonics (H5/H7/H9/H10/H11/H12/H16)

  Matches Addey mainstream + PRD specification. Most widely-used.

  Option B — Expanded set (add H2/H3/H4/H6/H8 for completeness)

  Include all low-order harmonics. But H2=conjunction doubled, H3=trine tripled, H4=square, H6=sextile, H8=semi-square — these are ALREADY present in standard natal 
  aspects. Redundant.

  Option C — Minimal 3-4 core (H5/H7/H9 only — most-popular harmonics)

  Tightest MVP. H5 (talent), H7 (spiritual), H9 (marriage — Vedic-adjacent) are the 3 most-cited.

  Option D — Tiered: 7 primary + research tier available (H13/H14/H17/H18+)

  Astrologer toggles optional research-tier harmonics beyond the 7 primary.

  Option E — Per-user-type split: B2C H9+H10 only (marriage + career most asked); Astrologer all 7

  Consumer simplicity + astrologer depth.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Options A + optional research tier (matches Option D)
  - Kepler: Option A (7 primary)
  - Astro.com: Option A
  - Ernst Wilhelm: Option A + astrologer toggle

  Practical note

  H9 specifically is interesting — it's philosophically PARALLEL to Vedic Navamsa but computationally different (H9 = tropical-longitude × 9 mod 360°; Navamsa =     
  sidereal division-9 table). Cross-tradition users see convergent meaning across very different computations.

  Q5 — Harmonic Charts scope:

  - A) 7 PRD-specified harmonics (H5/H7/H9/H10/H11/H12/H16) — Addey mainstream. Matches Kepler + Astro.com. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Expanded set (H2/H3/H4/H6/H8 added) — redundant with standard aspects.
  - C) Minimal 3-4 core (H5/H7/H9 only) — tightest MVP.
  - D) Tiered: 7 primary + astrologer research tier (H13/H14/H17/H18+) — matches Solar Fire + Ernst Wilhelm.
  - E) Per-user-type split — B2C H9+H10 only; Astrologer all 7.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Sautéed for 33s

● E8 Q5 locked: Option A — 7 PRD-specified harmonics (H5 talent, H7 spiritual, H9 marriage, H10 career, H11 friendship, H12 karmic, H16 tests). Matches Addey

  Q6 — Eclipse catalog horizon                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  Background. E8 PRD §2.1 specifies "NASA Five Millennium Canon dataset (local static file, Saros-indexed) covering 200y past + 50y future." Question: is this         horizon right, or adjust?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  Horizon options

  Option A — PRD current: 200y past + 50y future (250-year window)

  Pros: Covers native's birth-year eclipses + ancestors' lifetimes + near-future. Matches Solar Fire + Astro.com eclipse browser defaults.
  Cons: Asymmetric — "only 50 years of future eclipses" may feel arbitrary.

  Option B — Symmetric 100y past + 100y future (200-year window)

  Pros: Symmetric; covers native's lifetime + ancestry + descendants' generation.
  Cons: Less ancestral lookup depth.

  Option C — Extended 500y past + 100y future

  Pros: Historical eclipse research (astrocartography + mundane astrology use).
  Cons: Larger dataset; mostly unused by typical astrologer.

  Option D — Full NASA 5-millennium canon (3000 BCE – 3000 CE)

  Pros: Comprehensive; matches NASA data availability.
  Cons: Very large file; mostly unused; storage + perf cost.

  Option E — Astrologer-configurable per profile

  Default 200y past + 50y future; astrologer can extend to 500/1000/5000 years per their research needs.

  Option F — Native-lifetime-adaptive

  Automatically scoped: (native's birth year − 100) to (native's age + 50 years). Eclipse catalog dynamically sized per chart.

  Typical usage

  Most astrologers use eclipse-natal-conjunction checks for:
  - Past eclipses near birth (prenatal/natal eclipses in ±6 months of birth — eclipse-before-birth theory)
  - Past eclipses conjunct natal points (last 10-20 years — trigger events)
  - Future eclipses approaching natal points (next 5-10 years — predictive)

  Few astrologers check 200+ years back or 50+ years forward regularly.

  NASA catalog data size

  - 5-millennium full: ~15 MB JSON
  - 250-year window: ~1 MB
  - 200-year window: ~0.8 MB

  Storage trivial at all scopes.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Configurable; default 200y past + 100y future
  - Kepler: 300y past + 100y future
  - Astro.com: 200y past + 50y future (matches PRD)
  - Bernadette Brady's eclipse tools: full 5-millennium for eclipse research

  Q6 — Eclipse catalog horizon:

  - A) PRD current 200y past + 50y future — matches Astro.com default. Same for both user types.
  - B) Symmetric 100y past + 100y future — symmetric scope.
  - C) Extended 500y past + 100y future — historical research depth.
  - D) Full NASA 5-millennium canon — comprehensive but over-scoped.
  - E) Default 200y past + 50y future + astrologer toggle to extend per profile. ← my recommendation (PRD default + research-astrologer flexibility).
  - F) Native-lifetime-adaptive — dynamic scope per chart.
  - G) Other.

  Which?
   option E

● E8 Q6 locked: Option E — Default 200y past + 50y future Eclipse catalog + astrologer-profile toggle to extend (500y / 1000y / full 5-millennium). PRD default for  
  B2C + Option A practice; astrologer research flexibility. Matches Solar Fire + Astro.com convention with research extensibility.

  ---
  Q7 — Uranian (Hamburg School) scope (final E8 question)

  Background. Uranian astrology = 20th-century Western methodology from Alfred Witte + Hamburg School (~1920s). NOT classical Parashari, NOT classical Hellenistic — 
  specific to Uranian/Hamburg tradition. Two distinct components:

  Component 1 — Uranian midpoints + planetary pictures

  - 78 midpoints (half-sums of pairwise combinations of 10 planets + 3 angles = 13 elements × 12/2 = 78 midpoints)
  - Plus reflection points (2B − A) and sum points (A + B)
  - Planetary pictures: natal planet within 2° orb of a midpoint → interpretive configuration

  Widely used beyond strict Uranian — many modern Western astrologers use midpoints for fine analysis even without hypothetical planets.

  Component 2 — 8 Uranian hypothetical planets

  Cupido · Hades · Zeus · Kronos · Apollon · Admetos · Vulkanus · Poseidon

  These don't physically exist astronomically. Witte's "hypothetical bodies" at fictional orbital positions; positions derived from Witte's published ephemeris      
  tables. Strictly Hamburg School / Uranian practitioners use them.

  Scope options

  Option A — Full Uranian scope

  Both components: 78 midpoints + planetary pictures + 8 hypothetical planets + orbital positions. Comprehensive Uranian coverage.

  Option B — Midpoints only

  78 midpoints + planetary pictures. Skip 8 hypothetical planets (reject as non-astronomical / non-classical).

  Pros: Classical/astronomical fidelity. Cons: Uranian practitioners expect hypotheticals.

  Option C — 8 hypothetical planets only

  Skip midpoints (shared with mainstream Western). Just ship Uranian-specific hypotheticals.

  Option D — Tiered: midpoints primary + 8 hypotheticals astrologer-only

  B2C + astrologer default: 78 midpoints + planetary pictures. Astrologer toggle exposes 8 hypothetical planets for Uranian practitioners.

  Option E — Astrologer-configurable scope

  Default: midpoints only. Astrologer profile toggle: "include Uranian hypotheticals" enables the 8 planets.

  Classical / astronomical authenticity concern

  - Midpoints: astronomical; defensible across all Western traditions. No authenticity concern.
  - 8 hypothetical planets: NOT astronomical. Purely Witte's theoretical construct. Some Western astrologers reject them entirely.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Full Uranian (midpoints + 8 hypotheticals)
  - Kepler: Midpoints only (no 8 hypotheticals by default)
  - Astro.com: Full Uranian available
  - Ernst Wilhelm: Midpoints + 8 hypotheticals via astrologer preference
  - Strict-classical Hellenistic astrologers: Reject 8 hypotheticals

  Q7 — Uranian scope:

  - A) Full Uranian (78 midpoints + 8 hypothetical planets). Comprehensive; matches Solar Fire + Astro.com.
  - B) Midpoints only — skip 8 hypotheticals (classical/astronomical fidelity).
  - C) 8 hypothetical planets only — skip midpoints.
  - D) Tiered: midpoints primary + 8 hypotheticals astrologer-only toggle — matches Kepler + Ernst Wilhelm approach. ← my recommendation (midpoints are
  astrologically useful; hypotheticals are niche + non-astronomical; clean split).
  - E) Astrologer-configurable — default midpoints, optional Uranian-hypotheticals toggle.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Worked for 33s

❯ option D

● E8 Q7 locked: Option D — Tiered Uranian scope. Default: 78 midpoints + planetary pictures (astronomically defensible; useful across Western traditions).
  Astrologer-profile toggle exposes 8 Uranian hypothetical planets (Cupido, Hades, Zeus, Kronos, Apollon, Admetos, Vulkanus, Poseidon) for Hamburg School
  practitioners. Matches Kepler + Ernst Wilhelm convention. Clean split between astronomically-grounded midpoints + Uranian-specific hypotheticals.

● E8b Asteroids, Centaurs, Planetoids — Pass 1 kick-off                                                                                                              

  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Modern Western astrologers and Chiron-aware practitioners get asteroid/centaur/TNO positions in their chart. Workbench Asteroids sub-panel (under Western Depth tab   from E8) with toggles per body. Modern Western astrologers enable Ceres/Juno/Vesta/Chiron by default; classical Hellenistic astrologers leave them off; Vedic       astrologers see them only if they explicitly enable Western Depth tab. Strictly isolated from Vedic per E8 convention.                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Step B — Calculation pipeline (E8b sits inside E8 Western Depth)                                                                                                   

  1. Natal chart computed (tropical zodiac per E8 Q1 per-technique hybrid)
  2. E8b major asteroids (5): Swiss Eph swe.calc_ut(jd, swe.CERES/PALLAS/JUNO/VESTA/ASTRAEA, SEFLG_SWIEPH | SEFLG_SPEED)
  3. E8b Chiron: swe.calc_ut(jd, swe.CHIRON, ...)
  4. E8b Eris: swe.calc_ut(jd, swe.ERIS, ...)
  5. E8b additional centaurs (4): Pholus via SE_PHOLUS; Nessus/Chariklo/Hylonome via extended asteroid files (swe.calc_ut(jd, 10000 + mpc_number, ...))
  6. E8b TNOs (4, behind feature flag): Sedna, Haumea, Makemake, Orcus via extended ephemeris
  7. Output: structured_positions with body_type: {major_asteroid, centaur, dwarf_planet, tno}, plus retrograde/speed flags
  8. Integration with E8's aspect engine at minor-body orb conventions (2-3°)

  Remaining E8b-specific questions (~5)

  - Q1 — Body inclusion scope (5 major asteroids + Chiron + Eris + 4 centaurs + 4 TNOs = 15, vs subset)
  - Q2 — TNO feature-flag default (behind flag vs always-on for astrologer)
  - Q3 — Orb conventions for minor bodies (2-3° per PRD — confirm)
  - Q4 — Interpretation layer scope (classical-significance strings per body vs tradition-specific)
  - Q5 — Astrologer-profile defaults per practice-type (Hellenistic purist: exclude; Modern Western: enable Big Four + Chiron; Vedic: hidden)

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Body inclusion scope for E8b

  Background. E8b PRD proposes 15-20 minor bodies. Different tiers have different classical acceptance levels.

  Classical/modern acceptance tiers

  Tier 1 — Universally accepted in modern Western (5 bodies)

  Chiron — "wounded healer" archetype (Reinhart 1989); universal in modern Western.
  Ceres — nurturing/grief mother archetype (Demetra George 1986).
  Pallas — strategic wisdom/justice (Eleanor Bach 1973).
  Juno — partnership/marriage commitment.
  Vesta — sacred devotion/focus.

  These 5 are the "Big Five" modern Western standards.

  Tier 2 — Widely accepted (2 bodies)

  Eris — discord/boundary-outer generational (Richard Tarnas).
  Astraea — optional; behind flag in PRD.

  Tier 3 — Growing literature (4 centaurs)

  Pholus — catalyst/small-cause-big-effect (1992 discovery).
  Nessus — generational abuse/cycle-break (1993).
  Chariklo — healer-goddess/sanctuary (1997).
  Hylonome — grief/loss (1995).

  Centaur literature smaller; ~10-20% of modern Western astrologers use them.

  Tier 4 — Research-only (4 TNOs)

  Sedna (2003), Haumea (2004), Makemake (2005), Orcus (2004). Very long orbits (~250-12,000 years); generational-only. Fringe but actively researched.

  Scope options

  Option A — Tier 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 all included (15-20 bodies)

  Comprehensive. PRD scope. All astrologers can toggle any subset.

  Option B — Tier 1 + 2 only (Big Five + Eris) = 6 bodies

  Tighter MVP. Skip centaurs and TNOs. Matches Astro.com + mainstream modern Western software defaults.

  Option C — Tier 1 only (Big Five) = 5 bodies

  Minimal — just the universally-accepted 5.

  Option D — Tier 1 + 2 + 3 (Big Five + Eris + 4 centaurs) = 10 bodies

  Skip TNOs. Matches Solar Fire + Kepler defaults.

  Option E — All tiers behind feature flags (user/astrologer enables each tier)

  Tier 1: always on. Tier 2: default on. Tier 3: astrologer toggle. Tier 4: research flag.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Tier 1 + 2 + 3 (10 bodies) default; Tier 4 optional
  - Kepler: Tier 1 + 2 + 3
  - Astro.com: Tier 1 + 2 (6 bodies); centaurs + TNOs behind setting
  - Demetra George tools: Tier 1 + 2 + 3

  Q1 — E8b body inclusion scope:

  - A) All tiers (15 bodies) — comprehensive; matches PRD.
  - B) Tier 1 + 2 (6 bodies) — Big Five + Eris; tight scope. Matches Astro.com.
  - C) Tier 1 only (5 bodies) — Big Five minimum.
  - D) Tier 1 + 2 + 3 (10 bodies) — + 4 centaurs. Matches Solar Fire + Kepler.
  - E) Tiered feature flags — Tier 1 always on; Tier 2 default on; Tier 3 astrologer toggle; Tier 4 research flag. ← my recommendation (preserves all 15 in PRD but  
  user-friendly defaults).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

  Preserves PRD's 15-body scope with user-friendly defaults. Matches Solar Fire + Kepler base + astro.com progressive disclosure pattern.

  ---
  Q2 — Orb conventions for minor bodies

  Background. Minor bodies (asteroids, centaurs, TNOs) are physically small and astrologically "narrow" in influence. Classical convention applies TIGHTER orbs than 
  major planets. PRD §2.1 suggests 2-3°.

  Orb options

  Option A — PRD convention (2° asteroids-to-majors, 1° asteroid-to-asteroid)

  - Asteroids → major planets: 2° conjunction orb
  - Asteroid → asteroid: 1° (very tight)
  - Centaurs → major planets: 3° (slightly wider)
  - TNOs → major planets: 3° (generational bodies)

  Matches PRD spec; most widely-used modern Western convention.

  Option B — Uniform 3° for all minor-body aspects

  Simpler; slightly more generous. Less classically-defensible for asteroid-to-asteroid (which is unusually wide at 3°).

  Option C — Uniform 1° (tight classical)

  All minor-body aspects require 1° conjunction. Research-grade precision; misses many cultural/interpretive moments.

  Option D — Brady-convention (fixed 2° for minor bodies + major planets both)

  Bernadette Brady's Fixed Stars also use 1° orb; extending 2° uniformly for minor bodies is Solar Fire default.

  Option E — Astrologer-configurable orb per body-class

  Profile setting: asteroids 1-3°, centaurs 1-4°, TNOs 1-4°. Research-astrologer customization.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Option A (2° asteroid-major, 1° asteroid-asteroid, 3° centaurs)
  - Kepler: Option A
  - Astro.com: Option D (uniform 2°)
  - Ernst Wilhelm: Option E (configurable)

  Q2 — E8b orb conventions:

  - A) PRD convention — 2° asteroid-major, 1° asteroid-asteroid, 3° centaurs, 3° TNOs. Matches Solar Fire + Kepler. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Uniform 3° for all minor-body aspects — simpler.
  - C) Uniform 1° classical — research-grade precision.
  - D) Brady uniform 2° — matches Astro.com.
  - E) Astrologer-configurable orb — per-profile customization.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ A

  standard. Same for both user types.
