  Q3 — Interpretation layer scope per body                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
  Background. Like DECISIONS 1.9/1.10 (BPHS Ch.6 purpose metadata per varga), Josi can attach interpretive strings per minor body. The question: how deep/rich?                                                                                                                                                                             Proposed canonical interpretations per body                                                                                                                        

  ┌──────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┐
  │   Body   │           Canonical archetype           │   Primary authority   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Chiron   │ Wounded healer / rainbow bridge         │ Melanie Reinhart 1989 │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Ceres    │ Nurturing / grief / mother              │ Demetra George 1986   │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Pallas   │ Strategic wisdom / justice              │ Eleanor Bach 1973     │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Juno     │ Partnership / marriage commitment       │ Demetra George        │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Vesta    │ Sacred devotion / focus                 │ Demetra George        │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Eris     │ Discord / outer-boundary / generational │ Richard Tarnas        │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Astraea  │ Innocence restored / optional           │ Modern consensus      │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Pholus   │ Catalyst / small-cause-big-effect       │ Reinhart              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Nessus   │ Generational abuse / cycle-break        │ Zane Stein            │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Chariklo │ Healer-goddess / sanctuary              │ Reinhart              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Hylonome │ Grief / loss                            │ Zane Stein            │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Sedna    │ Exile / isolation / transformation      │ Henry Seltzer         │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Haumea   │ Fertility / regeneration                │ Mark Andrew Holmes    │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Makemake │ Creative rebellion                      │ Richard Brown         │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
  │ Orcus    │ Oaths / underworld transformation       │ Emerging consensus    │
  └──────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┘

  Scope options

  Option A — Ship canonical interpretive strings per body (single authority per)

  One archetype string + primary-authority citation per body. Simple; defensible; matches Solar Fire + Kepler.

  Option B — Multi-authority variants per body

  Each body has 2-3 authority-specific interpretations (e.g., Chiron per Reinhart + Clow + Tarnas). Astrologer picks. Richer; heavier.

  Option C — Minimal — single archetype keyword per body

  Just 1 keyword per body (Chiron = "wounded healer"). No citations; no longer text. Tight scope.

  Option D — No interpretation strings; astrologer provides framing

  E8b ships positions only; astrologer owns interpretation. Matches classical Vedic position (Josi doesn't interpret — astrologer does).

  Option E — Tiered like Q1

  Tier 1 bodies (Big Five + Chiron): full canonical interpretations (Option A).
  Tier 2 (Eris + Astraea): archetype keyword (Option C).
  Tier 3 (centaurs): minimal keyword.
  Tier 4 (TNOs): no interpretation (astrologer provides).

  Option F — Astrologer-expandable via community contributions

  Josi ships Option A canonical + community contribution workflow (like E4b Q6 authoring model). Astrologers submit alternate interpretations with classical
  citations.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Option A (canonical single authority)
  - Kepler: Option A
  - Astro.com: Option B (multi-authority for popular bodies)
  - Ernst Wilhelm: Option D (positions only; interpretation via his paid content)

  Q3 — E8b interpretation layer scope:

  - A) Canonical interpretive strings per body (single authority) — matches Solar Fire + Kepler. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Multi-authority variants per body — richer; heavier.
  - C) Minimal keyword per body — tight scope.
  - D) No interpretation strings — positions only, astrologer provides framing.
  - E) Tiered interpretations — full for Tier 1, minimal for Tier 3, none for Tier 4.
  - F) Canonical + community-contributable variants — Option A + extension workflow.
  - G) Other.

  Which?

                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q4 — Astrologer-profile practice-type defaults for E8b body visibility                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Background. Astrologers have different practice traditions. When an astrologer creates their Josi profile, their practice type should sensibly default E8b body      visibility.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
  Astrologer practice types

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                   Practice type                    │                                 Typical E8b preference                                 │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Hellenistic purist (Chris Brennan lineage)         │ Rejects asteroids/modern bodies. Only 7 classical planets + Lunar Nodes                │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Modern Western mainstream                          │ Big Five asteroids + Chiron enabled (Tier 1 = PRD default)                             │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Modern Western + centaurs                          │ Tier 1 + 2 + 3 enabled (Chiron + centaurs are common)                                  │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Uranian / Hamburg School                           │ Variable — some use asteroids, some don't; always use Uranian hypotheticals from E8 Q7 │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Vedic-only (Parashari / Jaimini)                   │ E8b hidden entirely; doesn't display Western Depth tab                                 │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Modern psychological (Liz Greene / Steven Forrest) │ Tier 1 + Eris; centaurs optional                                                       │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Cross-tradition generalist                         │ Uses minimums from each tradition; Tier 1 enabled                                      │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Profile-default options

  Option A — No practice-type profile defaults; manual toggle per astrologer

  Every astrologer manually enables/disables each body tier. Maximum flexibility; friction at setup.

  Option B — Preset practice-type profiles with pre-configured E8b toggles

  Astrologer picks practice type at profile setup:
  - Hellenistic purist → all tiers off
  - Modern Western → Tier 1 on; Tier 2 on; Tier 3 off; Tier 4 off
  - Modern Western + centaurs → Tier 1/2/3 on; Tier 4 off
  - Vedic-only → Western Depth tab hidden; no E8b
  - Modern psychological → Tier 1/2 on; Tier 3 off; Tier 4 off

  Option C — Single global default (modern Western) + manual per-body override

  Default: Tier 1 + Tier 2 on (modern Western). Astrologer can toggle individual bodies.

  Option D — Hide E8b by default for Vedic-region users; show for all others

  Geographic/language-based default:
  - Tamil / Telugu / Malayalam / Kannada / Hindi region profile → E8b hidden
  - English / European / Western-region profile → E8b visible with Tier 1 + 2 on

  Option E — Preset profiles + per-body override (B + C combined)

  Preset profile sets initial defaults; astrologer can toggle specific bodies post-preset.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire: Option E (practice-type preset + individual overrides)
  - Kepler: Option C (single global default; manual override)
  - Astro.com: Option E (account preferences; per-body toggle)
  - Ernst Wilhelm: Option E (detailed per-body configuration)

  Q4 — Astrologer-profile practice-type defaults:

  - A) No defaults; manual toggle — maximum flexibility; friction at setup.
  - B) Preset practice-type profiles — Hellenistic/Modern/Vedic/etc. sets E8b toggles at profile creation.
  - C) Single global default (Modern Western) + manual per-body override.
  - D) Geographic/language-based default — Vedic regions hide E8b; Western regions show.
  - E) Preset profiles + per-body override — matches Solar Fire + Astro.com convention. ← my recommendation (practice-type sensible defaults + astrologer granular   
  control).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ option E

   / Modern Western+centaurs / Vedic-only / Modern psychological / Uranian / Cross-tradition). Each preset sets E8b tier-visibility defaults. Astrologer can override   individual bodies post-preset. Matches Solar Fire + Astro.com + Ernst Wilhelm convention.

● E9 KP System — Pass 1 kick-off

  Step A — User story (context only)

  User asks AI chat: "Should I take this job?" AI routes to KP horary — "This is a yes/no question; KP is designed for exactly this. Pick a number 1-249." User picks
   137. AI casts KP horary chart; analyzes cuspal sub-lord of 10th house (career); returns "10th cusp sub-lord signifies 6th+10th — classically favorable for new    
  role." Astrologer workbench has dedicated KP panel — sub-lord table for planets+cusps, 5-level significator map, real-time Ruling Planets, 249-number horary form. 

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E9 requires KP-specific bindings)

  1. Natal chart computed
  2. Critical KP bindings:
    - Ayanamsa: SIDM_KRISHNAMURTI (KP-specific; NOT Lahiri per DECISIONS 1.2 shortlist)
    - House system: Placidus (required by KP sub-lord math — DECISIONS 1.3 flagged this KP-bound pair)
  3. E9 sub-lord computation:
    - Zodiac split: 27 natchathirams × 9 sub-divisions per natchathiram = 243 sub-divisions
    - Each sub proportional to Vimshottari dasa lord periods: Ketu 7/120, Sukkiran 20/120, Sooriyan 6/120, Chandran 10/120, Sevvai 7/120, Rahu 18/120, Guru 16/120,  
  Sani 19/120, Budhan 17/120 — nakshatra span of 13°20' divided proportionally
    - Each planet's longitude maps to exactly one sub-lord
    - Cuspal sub-lords: sub-lord of each of 12 house cusps
  4. E9 multi-level significators (5 levels per KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4):
    - Level 1: Planets IN the house
    - Level 2: Planets in natchathiram of Level 1 planets
    - Level 3: Lord of the house
    - Level 4: Planets in natchathiram of the Level 3 lord
    - Level 5: Planets conjunct the house lord or its natchathiram lord
  5. E9 Ruling Planets (at query moment): day lord + ascendant sign lord + ascendant natchathiram lord + Moon sign lord + Moon natchathiram lord (+ some variants    
  include ascendant sub-lord)
  6. E9 249-number horary: lookup table maps each number 1-249 to specific zodiacal longitude (from Mesham 0°00' at #1 to Meenam 29°41' at #249)
  7. Output: kp_analysis shape (proposed new F7 shape)

  What's already locked from DECISIONS

  Remaining E9-specific questions (~6)

  - Q1 — KP ayanamsa + house system binding strictness (auto-force Krishnamurti + Placidus when KP engine invoked, or allow astrologer override?)
  - Q2 — Significator hierarchy source (KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4 canonical — confirm 5-level)

  - Q5 — Cross-tradition display (KP in astrologer workbench alongside Parashari — visible simultaneously, or gated via profile practice-type like E8b Q4?)
  - Q6 — Single-source canon (KP Reader Vols 1-6 only, no variant toggle — single-source system)

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — KP ayanamsa + house system binding strictness

  Background. KP is mathematically built on:
  - Krishnamurti ayanamsa (SIDM_KRISHNAMURTI = 5, different value from Lahiri by ~5 arc-minutes at J2000)
  - Placidus house system (cuspal sub-lord math depends on Placidus-specific cusp computation)

  Using KP with Lahiri ayanamsa OR with Whole Sign houses produces mathematically wrong results. KS Krishnamurti himself is explicit: "KP requires Krishnamurti      
  ayanamsa + Placidus houses; no substitutions."

  Binding options

  Option A — Auto-force when KP engine active (PRD current)

  When astrologer enables KP engine (E9) on a chart, Josi:
  - Auto-switches ayanamsa to Krishnamurti (regardless of astrologer's global Lahiri default)
  - Auto-switches house system to Placidus (regardless of Whole Sign default)
  - UI shows banner: "KP active — using Krishnamurti ayanamsa + Placidus houses per KS Krishnamurti requirement"

  Classical fidelity; matches KS Krishnamurti's explicit instruction; prevents astrologer error.

  Option B — Astrologer explicit confirmation on KP activation

  When astrologer activates KP: modal dialog "KP requires Krishnamurti ayanamsa + Placidus houses. Auto-switch? [Yes / No — use current settings]". Astrologer can   
  override but is warned.

  Option C — Allow astrologer override without warning

  Astrologer can use KP with any ayanamsa + any house system. Classically incorrect but maximum flexibility.

  Option D — Force + separate KP chart (don't modify natal chart settings)

  Natal chart keeps its Lahiri + Whole Sign. KP engine generates a SEPARATE KP chart with Krishnamurti + Placidus specifically for KP analysis. User sees both —     
  natal Parashari + separate KP chart.

  Option E — Force binding + silent error for manual override attempts

  Astrologer cannot override. KP engine requires exact bindings. Attempts to override throw validation error.

  Classical position

  KS Krishnamurti explicitly states KP requires its specific ayanamsa + Placidus houses. Any substitution produces mathematically incorrect sub-lords and invalid    
  horary results. All KP practitioners follow this.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option D (separate KP chart with auto-forced bindings)
  - Parashara's Light: Option A (auto-force when KP mode activated)
  - KPStar (dedicated KP software): Option E (strict force; no override)
  - Astrosage KP module: Option A

  Q1 — KP ayanamsa + house system binding:

  - A) Auto-force when KP engine active (ayanamsa + house system auto-switch with banner notification). Classical fidelity + prevents astrologer error.
  - B) Astrologer explicit confirmation — dialog confirmation before switching.
  - C) Allow override without warning — astrologer freedom; risks incorrect results.
  - D) Separate KP chart, don't modify natal — natal stays Lahiri+Whole Sign; KP generates its own chart. Matches JH convention. ← my recommendation (cleanest       
  separation; astrologer sees both Parashari natal + KP chart explicitly).
  - E) Strict force with no override — validation error on override attempts.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Crunched for 1m 0s

❯ option D

● E9 Q1 locked: Option D — Separate KP chart, don't modify natal. Natal chart stays with Lahiri ayanamsa + Whole Sign house system per DECISIONS. KP engine generates
   its own chart with Krishnamurti ayanamsa + Placidus houses. Astrologer workbench shows both side-by-side (Parashari natal + KP chart). Cleanest separation.       
  Matches JH convention.

  ---
  Q2 — Significator hierarchy source

  Background. KP significator system assigns 5 levels of "significators" per house (planets that represent or trigger matters of that house). KS Krishnamurti's KP   
  Reader Vol.2 Ch.4 defines the canonical 5-level hierarchy:

  KP 5-level significator hierarchy (KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4)

  For each house 1-12:

  - Level 1 (Strongest): Planets occupying the natchathiram of the occupants of the house
    - Natchathiram-lords of planets IN the house
  - Level 2: Occupants of the house themselves
  - Level 3: Planets in the natchathiram of the lord of the house
  - Level 4: Lord of the house
  - Level 5 (Weakest): Planets conjoined with Rahu/Ketu or planets aspecting the house lord/occupants

  Wait — I actually gave a different 5-level order earlier. Let me reconcile. Per KS Krishnamurti Vol.2:

  1. Planets in the nakshatra of planets posited in the house — STRONGEST (natchathiram lord's significator)
  2. Planets posited in the house — next
  3. Planets in the nakshatra of the lord of the house
  4. Lord of the house — weaker
  5. Planets conjoined with any of the above OR planets conjunct Rahu/Ketu — weakest/modifier

  The order matters because KP uses strongest → weakest priority when predicting events. This is the canonical order from KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4.

  Options

  Option A — KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4 canonical 5-level (Krishnamurti primary source)

  Strict single-source — KS Krishnamurti's own text. No variant. Matches all KP software.

  Option B — Modified 5-level with Rahu/Ketu agenda as separate level

  Some modern KP astrologers elevate "Rahu/Ketu effect" as its own level. KP Reader Vol.2 keeps it in Level 5.

  Option C — 7-level extended (modern KP synthesis)

  Add 2 more levels: planets in sub-lord's natchathiram + planets aspecting sub-lord. Extended research-tier usage.

  Option D — KP Reader canonical + modern commentary annotations

  Level structure stays Option A canonical; UI shows level + commentary notes from modern KP practitioners (K. Hariharan, K.N. Rao's KP treatment, etc.).

  Classical source

  KP is single-source canon — KS Krishnamurti himself wrote Vols 1-6. There's no competing classical authority to adjudicate. KS Krishnamurti's son and students     
  preserved the system. KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4 is THE source.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora KP module: Option A (Vol.2 Ch.4 canonical)
  - KPStar: Option A (strict canonical)
  - Parashara's Light KP: Option A
  - Astrosage KP: Option A

  Q2 — Significator hierarchy source:

  - A) KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4 canonical 5-level — strict single-source (KS Krishnamurti's own text). Matches all KP software. Same for both user types. ← my
  recommendation (KP is single-source canon — no variants justified).
  - B) Modified 5-level (Rahu/Ketu as separate level) — modern KP synthesis.
  - C) 7-level extended — research-tier modern expansion.
  - D) Canonical + modern commentary annotations — level structure + secondary notes.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ explain A

● Option A — KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4 canonical 5-level significator hierarchy (detailed explanation)

  Background. In KP, to predict whether an event related to a house will occur, you identify which planets "represent" (signify) that house. KS Krishnamurti
  specifies 5 levels of significators in strict priority order — strongest to weakest. A planet appearing at a higher level is a MORE RELIABLE event-predictor than  
  one at a lower level.

  The 5 levels (exact order from KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4)

  For each house H (1-12), the 5 levels are identified in this priority:

  Level 1 (Strongest) — Planets in the natchathiram of the planets occupying house H

  Step: Find planets physically placed in house H. For each such planet, find WHICH planet rules its natchathiram. Those natchathiram-lord planets are Level 1       
  significators.

  Classical logic: The planets occupying a house "carry" the house's influence; but the PLANET WHOSE NATCHATHIRAM they're in is the "deeper signifier" — because     
  natchathiram-lord is considered the "source" of a planet's event-producing power in KP.

  Example: If Sukkiran is in 7th house, and Sukkiran is in Poosam natchathiram (ruled by Sani), then Sani is a Level 1 significator of 7th house.

  Level 2 — Planets occupying house H themselves

  Step: Direct occupants of house H.

  Example: If Sukkiran is in 7th house, Sukkiran is a Level 2 significator of 7th house.

  Level 3 — Planets in the natchathiram of the lord of house H

  Step: Find the rasi-lord of house H (the planet ruling the rasi on the cusp of H). For that lord, find which planet rules its natchathiram. That natchathiram-lord 
  is a Level 3 significator.

  Example: If 7th cusp is Thulam (ruled by Sukkiran), and Sukkiran is in Poosam natchathiram (ruled by Sani), then Sani is Level 3 for 7th house.

  (Note: Sani appears at BOTH Level 1 AND Level 3 in this example — multi-level appearance STRENGTHENS significator status.)

  Level 4 — Lord of house H itself

  Step: Rasi-lord of the cusp of house H.

  Example: 7th cusp is Thulam → Sukkiran is Level 4 for 7th house.

  Level 5 (Weakest) — Modifier planets

  Step: Planets that are:
  - Conjoined with any Level 1-4 significator (conjunction adds significator status)
  - Conjunct Rahu or Ketu (nodes act as "amplifiers" — planets with Rahu/Ketu take on the nodes' significations)
  - Aspecting the house cusp OR house lord

  These are "secondary" — they add influence but are not primary predictors.

  Why the ordering matters for prediction

  When KP predicts "will event X occur?" for a house:

  1. Find the sub-lord of the house cusp (the "cuspal sub-lord")
  2. Check if that sub-lord is a significator of THAT house — if yes, event likely occurs during the sub-lord's dasa/transit
  3. PRIORITY: Level 1 significators are STRONGEST predictors — events trigger during their periods most reliably
  4. Level 4-5 significators are weaker — events may trigger but with less reliability

  Classical rule: A planet appearing at MULTIPLE levels (e.g., Level 1 AND Level 3 simultaneously) is a STRONG significator — its dasa period reliably produces the  
  house event.

  Complete worked example

  Chart: Lagna Mesham. 7th cusp = Thulam (ruled by Sukkiran). Sukkiran placed in 7th house in Poosam natchathiram (ruled by Sani). Guru placed in 7th house too, in  
  Rohini natchathiram (ruled by Chandran).

  5-level significators of 7th house (marriage/partner):

  - Level 1: Sani (natchathiram-lord of Sukkiran in 7th) + Chandran (natchathiram-lord of Guru in 7th)
  - Level 2: Sukkiran + Guru (occupants)
  - Level 3: Sani (natchathiram-lord of Sukkiran who is 7th lord)
    - Note: Sani appears at Level 1 AND Level 3 → stronger
  - Level 4: Sukkiran (7th cusp lord)
    - Note: Sukkiran appears at Level 2 AND Level 4 → stronger
  - Level 5: Any planets conjunct Sani/Chandran/Sukkiran/Guru + Rahu/Ketu-conjuncts

  Prediction: If cuspal sub-lord of 7th cusp is Sani, and Sani is a Level 1 + Level 3 significator (double-appearance) → marriage highly likely during Sani's        
  upcoming dasa periods. If cuspal sub-lord is Guru (Level 2 only) → less reliable predictor.

  Single-source canon (why Option A is defensible)

  Unlike Parashari (which has BPHS vs Saravali vs Phaladeepika variants), KP has no classical disagreement because KS Krishnamurti wrote every KP rule himself       
  (1971-1983). His 6-volume KP Reader IS the canon. No commentator has "revised" the 5-level hierarchy — it's accepted as KS Krishnamurti's own specification.       

  This is why Option A ("KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4 canonical 5-level") is classically defensible without a variant toggle — there's nothing to vary against.

  Summary

  Option A = ship exactly the 5-level hierarchy from KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4. Single source. No commentary variants. Matches all KP software. Astrologers cross-checking
   against JH/KPStar/Parashara's Light get matching significators.

  ---
  Still waiting on your pick — Q2 A / B / C / D / E?

✻ Worked for 36s

❯ A

● E9 Q2 locked: Option A — KP Reader Vol.2 Ch.4 canonical 5-level significator hierarchy. Strict single-source from KS Krishnamurti's own text. Levels: (1)
  natchathiram-lords of house occupants, (2) house occupants, (3) natchathiram-lord of house-cusp lord, (4) house-cusp lord, (5) modifier planets (conjunct Level 1-4   or Rahu/Ketu). Same for both user types.                                                                                                                             
  ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q3 — Ruling Planets computation variant

  Background. Ruling Planets (RP) = a SET of 4-5 planets derivable at any moment (not birth-specific). Used in KP horary + event-timing: events manifest when        
  transiting planets + RPs + significators align.

  Classical RP elements (KS Krishnamurti Vol.3)

  At any query moment, compute:

  1. Day lord (vaara lord) — Sunday=Sooriyan, Monday=Chandran, Tuesday=Sevvai, Wednesday=Budhan, Thursday=Guru, Friday=Sukkiran, Saturday=Sani
  2. Ascendant sign lord — rasi-ruler of the rising sign at query moment
  3. Ascendant natchathiram lord — natchathiram-ruler of the ascendant degree
  4. Moon sign lord — rasi-ruler of Chandran's rasi at query moment
  5. Moon natchathiram lord — natchathiram-ruler of Chandran's position

  KS Krishnamurti's standard 5-element RP.

  Variants

  Variant A — 5-element standard (KP Reader Vol.3 canonical)

  Day lord + Asc sign lord + Asc natchathiram lord + Moon sign lord + Moon natchathiram lord. 5 elements.

  Variant B — 6-element with Ascendant sub-lord added

  Add the sub-lord of the ascendant degree as 6th element. Some modern KP schools use this (Hariharan lineage).

  Variant C — 7-element (adds Moon sub-lord)

  6-element variant + Moon's sub-lord. Research-tier KP practice.

  Variant D — Vol.3 base + Rahu/Ketu modifier

  When Rahu or Ketu occupies ascendant sign OR Moon sign, elevate them to RP set (treated as "stronger than usual"). Modern KP synthesis.

  Variant E — 5-element default + astrologer toggle to 6/7-element

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Classical fidelity

  KS Krishnamurti's 5-element RP is canonical. The 6-element (add sub-lord) variant is a modern extension by K. Hariharan and other post-Krishnamurti KP teachers.   
  Not in original KP Reader text.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora KP module: 5-element canonical (Variant A)
  - KPStar: 5-element + 6-element toggle (Variant E)
  - Parashara's Light KP: 5-element canonical
  - Astrosage KP: 5-element

  Q3 — Ruling Planets variant:

  - A) 5-element standard (KP Reader Vol.3 canonical) — strict single-source. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation (KP
  single-source canon).
  - B) 6-element with ascendant sub-lord — Hariharan modern extension.
  - C) 7-element with ascendant + Moon sub-lords — research-tier.
  - D) 5-element + Rahu/Ketu modifier — modern synthesis.
  - E) 5-element default + astrologer toggle to 6/7-element — matches KPStar. Per uniform variant convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E9 Q3 locked: Option A — 5-element standard Ruling Planets (KP Reader Vol.3 canonical): day lord + ascendant sign lord + ascendant natchathiram lord + Moon sign   
  lord + Moon natchathiram lord. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q4 — Sub-level depth                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  Background. KP uses nested natchathiram subdivisions. Original KP Reader prescribes up to 5 levels (Sub → Sub-Sub → Sub-Sub-Sub → 4th-level → 5th-level). Each       level further subdivides by Vimshottari dasa lord proportions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  Levels

  ┌───────┬───────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ Level │       Name        │                    Span                     │                 Usage                 │
  ├───────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │   1   │ Natchathiram lord │ ~13°20' per natchathiram (27 across zodiac) │ Base significator Level               │
  ├───────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │   2   │ Sub-lord          │ ~1°-3° per sub (243 across zodiac = 27 × 9) │ Primary cuspal sub-lord for KP horary │
  ├───────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │   3   │ Sub-sub-lord      │ ~6'-24' per sub-sub (2,187 = 243 × 9)       │ Fine-grained event timing             │
  ├───────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │   4   │ 4th-level         │ ~30''-160'' per (19,683 = 2,187 × 9)        │ Research-tier precision               │
  ├───────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │   5   │ 5th-level         │ ~3''-17'' per (177,147 = 19,683 × 9)        │ Academic/research only                │
  └───────┴───────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────┘

  Scope options

  Option A — Ship 5 levels (PRD current + KP Reader canonical)

  Full KP Reader specification. KPStar supports 5 levels. Matches PRD §2.1.

  Option B — Ship 3 levels MVP (Sub-Sub-Sub-lord)

  Tighter. Most KP astrologers use up to Sub-Sub-Sub-lord for horary (3 levels). 4th/5th levels rarely used even by specialists.

  Option C — Ship 2 levels MVP (Sub-lord + Sub-sub-lord)

  Minimum useful — sub-lord + one deeper level for horary analysis.

  Option D — Astrologer-configurable depth

  Astrologer profile picks depth (2/3/4/5). Default = 3.

  Practical usage

  - Level 2 (Sub-lord): Universally used — cuspal sub-lord is primary KP prediction tool
  - Level 3 (Sub-sub-lord): Commonly used for precise event timing within sub-lord period
  - Level 4 (4th-level): Rarely used (~5% of KP practitioners for specific research)
  - Level 5 (5th-level): Academic/research-only (~1% usage)

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora KP: 5 levels
  - KPStar: 5 levels
  - Parashara's Light KP: 3 levels (Sub → Sub-Sub → Sub-Sub-Sub)
  - Astrosage KP: 3 levels

  Trade-off

  5 levels = comprehensive coverage but marginal value. 3 levels = covers 95% of real KP practice. Going 5 adds precision for research but typical usage doesn't     
  benefit.

  Q4 — KP sub-level depth:

  - A) 5 levels (PRD current) — full KP Reader canonical. Matches KPStar + JH.
  - B) 3 levels MVP — covers 95% real practice. Matches PL + Astrosage.
  - C) 2 levels MVP — minimum useful.
  - D) Astrologer-configurable — default 3; astrologer can enable 4/5.
  - E) 3 levels default + astrologer toggle to 5 — B2C simpler; research-astrologer depth. ← my recommendation (matches real usage; keeps deep research available).  
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E9 Q4 locked: Option A — 5 levels full KP Reader canonical depth (Natchathiram lord → Sub-lord → Sub-sub-lord → 4th-level → 5th-level). Matches KPStar + JH. Full  
  coverage including research-tier precision. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q5 — KP cross-tradition display / activation in astrologer workbench                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  Background. Per E9 Q1 lock (separate KP chart, don't modify natal), astrologer sees both Parashari natal + KP chart. But HOW is KP activated?                                                                                                                                                                                             Activation options                                                                                                                                                 

  Option A — KP always-visible on astrologer workbench

  Every astrologer sees "KP" as a tab alongside Parashari/Jaimini/Tajaka tabs. KP chart computed for every chart. Always accessible.

  Pros: No friction; always available. Cons: Compute cost per chart; UI clutter for non-KP astrologers.

  Option B — KP hidden by default; astrologer enables via practice-type profile

  Per E8b Q4 practice-type convention. Astrologer selects practice type at profile:
  - Vedic-Parashari only → KP tab hidden
  - Vedic-KP practitioner → KP tab primary + Parashari cross-reference
  - Mixed practice → Both visible
  - Western → both Vedic + KP hidden

  Matches E8b pattern.

  Option C — KP auto-detected by ayanamsa choice

  If astrologer picks Krishnamurti ayanamsa globally → KP tab appears. If astrologer uses Lahiri default → KP tab hidden.

  Pros: Simple signal. Cons: Breaks Q1 (separate KP chart convention); Lahiri default users who occasionally want KP can't access without switching ayanamsa.        

  Option D — Per-chart activation

  Astrologer explicitly activates "KP analysis" per chart. KP chart computed on demand.

  Pros: Lazy compute; no ambient KP overhead. Cons: Friction per chart.

  Option E — Practice-type preset + per-chart override

  Combination of B + D. Practice-type sets default KP visibility; astrologer can override per chart.

  Integration with prior E-PRD conventions

  - E8b Q4 locked practice-type presets for asteroid tier defaults. Same pattern extends naturally to E9.
  - E8 Q2 locked Placidus default for Western; same practice-type structure.

  Practice-type convention is becoming Josi's pattern for cross-tradition display.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option A (KP always-visible tab)
  - Parashara's Light: Option B (practice-type setting)
  - KPStar: KP-only (no Parashari); standalone
  - Astrosage KP module: Option B (enable KP in account settings)

  Q5 — KP cross-tradition activation:

  - A) KP always-visible on astrologer workbench — matches JH; maximum accessibility.
  - B) Practice-type profile preset — astrologer picks "Vedic-KP practitioner" / "Mixed" / "Parashari-only" / etc.; preset sets KP visibility. Matches E8b Q4        
  pattern. ← my recommendation (consistency with E8b convention).
  - C) Auto-detect by ayanamsa choice — breaks Q1 separation.
  - D) Per-chart activation — lazy compute; friction per chart.
  - E) Practice-type preset + per-chart override — B + D combined; maximum flexibility.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ B                                                                                                                                                                      
● E9 Q5 locked: Option B — Practice-type profile preset. Astrologer selects practice type at profile creation:                                                       
  - Vedic-Parashari only → KP tab hidden
  - Vedic-KP practitioner → KP tab primary + Parashari cross-reference                                                                                                 - Mixed Vedic practice → Both tabs visible equally                                                                                                                 
  - Western → both Vedic + KP hidden (Western Depth tab visible per E8/E8b)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  Matches E8b Q4 convention. Consistent cross-tradition display pattern across Josi.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q6 — Single-source canon confirmation (final E9 question)

  Background. KP is mathematically single-source — KS Krishnamurti wrote every rule himself in KP Reader Vols 1-6 (1971-1983). Unlike Parashari (BPHS + Saravali +   
  Phaladeepika variants) or Jaimini (JUS + Rangacharya commentary), KP has NO classical disagreement to adjudicate.

  Question: ship strict canon only, OR include modern post-Krishnamurti extensions?

  Modern KP teachers and their extensions

  After KS Krishnamurti's death (1972), his son K. Hariharan and student-teachers continued expanding KP:

  - K. Hariharan — extended RP to 6-element (adds ascendant sub-lord); introduced "time-flash" horary variant
  - Kanakkumar Bosale — Maharashtrian KP school; introduces KP-adapted muhurta techniques
  - P.V.R. Narasimha Rao (JH developer) — integrates KP into Vedic framework; uses strict KS Krishnamurti canon
  - KP Institute (Chennai) — preserves strict canon; rejects modern extensions as "non-Krishnamurti"

  Options

  Option A — Strict KP Reader Vols 1-6 only (KS Krishnamurti's own text)

  Single-source canon. No modern extensions. Matches KP Institute + most KP practitioners + JH + PL + KPStar. Classically defensible.

  Option B — KP Reader + Hariharan extensions (widely adopted)

  Include Hariharan's 6-element RP as option + Hariharan's time-flash horary variant. Modernized KP widely used in South India.

  Option C — KP Reader + multiple modern teachers (Hariharan + Bosale + regional)

  Full modern KP synthesis. More inclusive.

  Option D — KP Reader default + modern extensions astrologer toggle

  Matches uniform variant convention (E1b/E3/E5/E5b/E7). Default canonical; astrologer toggles Hariharan extensions.

  Classical fidelity argument

  KP's defining characteristic is single-authority canon. Extensions dilute this. Most KP practitioners cross-check against JH which ships strict canon only.        
  Shipping modern extensions risks producing different horary results than cross-reference software.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Strict canon (Option A)
  - KPStar: Strict canon
  - Parashara's Light KP: Strict canon
  - Astrosage KP: Strict canon primary + optional Hariharan notes
  - KPastrosagefast (Hariharan-school tool): Hariharan extensions primary

  Q6 — KP canon scope:

  - A) Strict KP Reader Vols 1-6 only — single-source canonical. Matches JH + KPStar + PL + Astrosage. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation (KP's defining  
  feature is single-authority canon; protecting that).
  - B) KP Reader + Hariharan extensions — modernized KP.
  - C) KP Reader + multiple modern teachers — full synthesis.
  - D) KP Reader default + modern extensions astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention applied.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

✻ Cogitated for 30s

❯ A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E9 Q6 locked: Option A — Strict KP Reader Vols 1-6 only. Single-source canonical (KS Krishnamurti's own text). No modern extensions shipped. Matches JH + KPStar + 
  PL + Astrosage. KP's defining feature is single-authority canon — preserving that. Same for both user types.

      110 +    "kp_chart": {  # Separate KP chart per Q1

      125 +    "ruling_planets": {  # 5-element per Q3 at specified moment                                                                                           
      126 +        "day_lord": graha,                                                                                                                                
      127 +        "asc_sign_lord": graha,                                                                                                                           
      128 +        "asc_natchathiram_lord": graha,                                                                                                                   
      129 +        "moon_sign_lord": graha,                                                                                                                          
      130 +        "moon_natchathiram_lord": graha,                                                                                                                  
      131 +        "computed_at": datetime                                                                                                                           
      132 +    },                                                                                                                                                    
      133 +    "sub_depth_levels": 5,  # per Q4                                                                                                                      
      134 +    "source": "kp_reader_vols_1_6"  # strict canon                                                                                                        
      135 +}                                                                                                                                                         
      136 +```

● E10 Prasna/Horary — Pass 1 kick-off                                                                                                                                

  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  User asks AI chat: "I lost my wallet — will I find it?" AI: "This is a classic Prasna question. Casting a chart for this moment." Runs BOTH Kerala Prasna (Arudha    of Prasna Lagna, Navamsa yes/no, planetary hour, omen rules) AND KP Horary (249-number or auto-method). Returns synthesis: "Both methods agree — findable within     3-5 days; most likely near recent-travel or public-place context." Astrologer workbench has Prasna console — single query form (question + moment + location +       optional category) → dual-method results side-by-side with per-method reasoning trace.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E10 orchestrates existing engines)

  1. User/astrologer submits PrasnaQuery(question_text, moment_utc, location, intent_category)
  2. Intent categorization (AI-assisted if category not provided; maps to house number per E9 query map)
  3. Prasna Lagna cast at query moment + location (sidereal Lahiri for Kerala; Krishnamurti for KP)
  4. E10 Kerala Prasna analysis:
    - Arudha of Prasna Lagna (Prasna Marga Ch.2 formula)
    - Vimshottari MD/AD/PD at query moment (reuses E1a)
    - Navamsa yes/no of query-house lord (Prasna Marga Ch.8)
    - Omen analysis (rule-based; ~80 omens from Prasna Marga)
    - Planetary hour fitness (Horai per DECISIONS 3.11)
  5. E10 KP Horary delegation:
    - Delegate to E9's KP horary mechanics (249-number or auto)
    - Cuspal sub-lord of query house analysis
    - Ruling Planets at query moment (from E9)
  6. E10 Synthesis:
    - Both methods' results + confidence
    - AI chat generates narrative synthesis
  7. Output: horary_analysis shape with both method results, per-method confidence, overall synthesis, full reasoning trace

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Method routing (Kerala vs KP)

  Background. Kerala Prasna and KP Horary are two distinct classical methods. E10 PRD §1 says: "Kerala Prasna excels at omens, lost articles, elemental questions    
  (health, travel). KP excels at yes/no binary outcomes with confidence (marriage, career, litigation)."

  Routing options

  Option A — Always run BOTH methods; return dual results

  Every Prasna query triggers BOTH Kerala Prasna analysis AND KP Horary. User sees both results. AI synthesis presents both perspectives.

  Pros: No routing decision; classical rigor; user can compare. Cons: 2× compute; may confuse B2C with conflicting answers.

  Option B — Route by intent category

  Based on question category, use best-matching method:
  - Elemental questions (lost articles, health, travel, omens): Kerala Prasna
  - Binary outcomes (marriage, career, litigation, yes/no): KP Horary
  - Mixed categories: Both methods

  Option C — Astrologer picks default method at profile; auto-uses for every query

  Astrologer's practice-type preset (E9 Q5) determines default:
  - Vedic-Parashari → Kerala Prasna default
  - Vedic-KP → KP default
  - Mixed → Both

  Option D — Astrologer picks per-query

  Per-query method selector in UI. No auto-routing.

  Option E — Hybrid — route by category; astrologer can override

  Option B default + astrologer per-query override.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora Prasna module: Option A (both methods)
  - KPStar (KP-only): KP only
  - Kerala-specific tools: Kerala only
  - Astrosage Prasna: Option B (category-routed)

  Q1 — Method routing:

  - A) Always run BOTH Kerala + KP — classical rigor; dual results; matches JH. ← my recommendation (PRD intent: "Ultra AI mode surfaces both").
  - B) Route by intent category — automatic category mapping.
  - C) Astrologer practice-type preset default — per profile.
  - D) Astrologer picks per-query — no auto-routing.
  - E) Hybrid — category-routed + override — best of B+D.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Brewed for 51s

● E10 Q1 locked: Option A — Always run BOTH Kerala Prasna AND KP Horary methods for every query. Classical rigor; dual results with per-method reasoning trace.      
  Matches JH Prasna module + PRD intent "Ultra AI mode surfaces both." Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q2 — Arudha of Prasna Lagna formula                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Background. Kerala Prasna's key primitive: Arudha of Prasna Lagna (ALPL) — a "reflection point" of the query's rising chart. Per Prasna Marga Ch.2:                                                                                                                                                                                       Prasna Lagna = rising rasi at query moment                                                                                                                         
  ALPL computation:
    1. Find Prasna Lagna's lord L
    2. Count positions from Prasna Lagna's rasi to L's rasi (inclusive count)
    3. Project same count forward from L's rasi
    4. Landed rasi = Arudha of Prasna Lagna

  Plus exception rules for degenerate cases.

  Relation to E3 Q2 Arudha lock

  E3 Q2 locked Jaimini Upadesha Sutras 1.2.39-45 canonical 2-exception rule for natal Arudha computation:
  - If Arudha = H itself → project to 10th from L
  - If Arudha = 7th from H → project to 10th from L

  Question: does Arudha of Prasna Lagna use the same JUS 1.2.39-45 rule, or does Prasna Marga Ch.2 specify a different Arudha formulation for horary context?        

  Classical variant

  Variant A — Same JUS 1.2.39-45 rule (E3 Q2 inheritance)

  Use natal Arudha computation method identically. Same 2-exception rule. Prasna Lagna treated same as natal Lagna.

  Pros: Consistency with E3; single classical rule across all Arudha computations. Cons: Doesn't honor any Prasna-specific adjustments.

  Variant B — Prasna Marga Ch.2 canonical (horary-specific formulation)

  Prasna Marga may specify distinct exception rules for horary context. Check specific text.

  Variant C — JUS rule + Prasna Marga additional refinements

  JUS base + Prasna Marga commentary overlays where Prasna-specific subtleties matter.

  Variant D — Research Prasna Marga Ch.2 before locking

  Verify whether Prasna Marga's Arudha formulation materially differs from JUS.

  Classical position

  Scholarship on Prasna Marga (17th c Kerala text) suggests Arudha formulation EXPLICITLY references and follows Jaimini Upadesha Sutras. Prasna Marga's Arudha rule 
  = JUS Arudha rule applied to horary context. No material divergence.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora Prasna: Variant A (JUS rule inherited)
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Kerala Prasna tools: Variant A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC Prasna: Variant A

  Q2 — Arudha of Prasna Lagna formula:

  - A) Same JUS 1.2.39-45 rule (E3 Q2 inheritance) — consistent with natal Arudha computation; Prasna Marga 17th c follows JUS foundation. Matches JH + PL + Kerala  
  tools + Rath. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation (consistency + classically accurate).
  - B) Prasna Marga Ch.2 canonical (if distinct) — horary-specific variant.
  - C) JUS base + Prasna Marga refinements — synthesis.
  - D) Research Prasna Marga Ch.2 before locking.
  - E) Other.

  Which?A 
   A

● E10 Q2 locked: Option A — Same JUS 1.2.39-45 rule (E3 Q2 inheritance) for Arudha of Prasna Lagna. Consistent with natal Arudha computation; Prasna Marga (17th c   
  Kerala) explicitly follows JUS foundation. Matches JH + PL + Kerala tools + Rath. Same for both user types.

  ---
  Q3 — Omen rule scope

  Background. Prasna Marga includes Nimitta Shastra — ~80 classical omen rules that modulate the reading based on what's observed at query moment. Examples:

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┐
  │                 Omen                  │  Classical interpretation   │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Temple bell / mangalic sound heard    │ Strong benefic              │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Crow crosses path                     │ Malefic (obstacle)          │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Cow mooing                            │ Benefic (fertility/wealth)  │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Dog barking at querent                │ Malefic                     │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Specific animals per direction        │ Directional interpretations │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sudden wind/rain                      │ Disturbance indicators      │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Laughing/crying nearby                │ Mood modifier               │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Color of first-seen item              │ Deva omens                  │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Person's profession first encountered │ Karmic indicator            │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sweet sounds (music/mantra)           │ Benefic                     │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Harsh sounds (quarrel/argument)       │ Malefic                     │
  └───────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┘

  Scope options

  Option A — Ship all ~80 classical omens from Prasna Marga

  Comprehensive coverage. Full Nimitta Shastra implementation. Astrologer (or user via AI chat) selects which omens apply at query moment; each has
  classical-interpretation annotation.

  Option B — Subset (~30 most commonly-cited)

  Tighter scope. The 30 most frequently-used omens in Kerala practice ship MVP; remaining 50 deferred.

  Option C — Minimal (~15 core omens)

  Only the universally-taught omens (temple bell, crow, cow, dog, sudden wind/rain, etc.). Loss of fine-grained interpretation.

  Option D — Astrologer-configurable enabled list

  Astrologer profile picks which omens to apply. Default = standard 30.

  Option E — Tiered: 15 core always-available + 65 extended astrologer-toggle

  Matches other tiered decisions (E8b Q1 tiered bodies, E7 progressive disclosure).

  Practical UX consideration

  Omens are manually reported by querent (or astrologer) — not auto-detected from ambient data. So "more omens" = richer vocabulary; "fewer omens" = simpler UX.     

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora Prasna: ~40 omens implemented
  - KPStar: No omen module (KP-only)
  - Parashara's Light Prasna: ~30 omens
  - Kerala-specific tools: ~60-80 omens (most comprehensive)

  Q3 — Omen rule scope:

  - A) All ~80 classical omens — full Nimitta Shastra from Prasna Marga. Matches Kerala tools.
  - B) Subset ~30 most-cited — tight scope. Matches PL + JH approach.
  - C) Minimal ~15 core — universally-taught omens only.
  - D) Astrologer-configurable list — default 30; astrologer can add/remove.
  - E) Tiered: 15 core always-available + 65 extended astrologer-toggle — matches tiered decisions convention. ← my recommendation (progressive disclosure;
  extensible).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

  Q4 — Navamsa yes/no rule

  Background. Prasna Marga Ch.8 describes a specific Navamsa (D9) rule for yes/no horary answers. Used as one input to Kerala Prasna synthesis:

  For query Q:
    1. Determine query-house H (e.g., 7 for marriage, 10 for career)
    2. Find H's lord L
    3. Check L's Navamsa position at query moment:
       - Own sign / exaltation / friend's sign / benefic aspect → YES
       - Enemy sign / debilitation / malefic aspect → NO
       - Neutral → MAYBE / conditional
    4. Emit yes/no/maybe verdict with reasoning

  Classical source variants

  Variant A — Prasna Marga Ch.8 canonical rule

  Strict single-source application. Standard Kerala Prasna convention.

  Variant B — Prasna Marga + KP synthesis

  Use Prasna Marga yes/no rule + KP's cuspal sub-lord analysis for the same house → combined verdict. Modern Parashari-KP synthesis.

  Variant C — Astrologer toggle between Prasna Marga Ch.8 + variants

  Per uniform variant convention (E1b Q7 / E3 Q6 / E5 Q7 / E5b Q7 / E7 Q6). Prasna Marga default + Kerala-specific or KP-synthesis variants.

  Variant D — Dignity-based synthesis (modern simplified)

  Use Level-1 + Level-4 dignities in Navamsa → weighted verdict. Skip specific classical aspects; simpler.

  Classical source

  Prasna Marga Ch.8 is the primary Kerala Prasna source. No widely-competing classical authority. KP has its own yes/no method (cuspal sub-lord of house — already in
   Q1 Option A both-methods-always). So E10 Q4 specifically addresses Kerala Prasna's Navamsa yes/no rule.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora Prasna: Variant A (PM Ch.8 canonical)
  - Parashara's Light Prasna: Variant A
  - Kerala Prasna tools: Variant A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC Prasna: Variant A

  Q4 — Navamsa yes/no rule:

  - A) Prasna Marga Ch.8 canonical — strict single-source. Matches JH + PL + Kerala tools + Rath. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) PM Ch.8 + KP cuspal sub-lord synthesis — combined verdict from both methods per house.
  - C) PM default + astrologer variant toggle — per uniform variant convention.
  - D) Dignity-based simplified — non-classical.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ A

● E10 Q4 locked: Option A — Prasna Marga Ch.8 canonical Navamsa yes/no rule. Lord of query-house in Navamsa dignity evaluation → yes / no / maybe verdict. Matches JH
   + PL + Kerala tools + Sanjay Rath. Same for both user types.

  ---
  Q5 — AI synthesis strictness (dual-method presentation)

  Background. Q1 locked "always run both Kerala + KP methods." Q5 decides HOW the dual results are presented to user (B2C AI chat) vs astrologer (workbench).        

  Presentation options

  Option A — Raw dual display (astrologer interprets)

  Both methods' results shown side-by-side with classical reasoning trace. No AI synthesis. Astrologer reads both, synthesizes manually. B2C AI chat presents both   
  without unified narrative.

  Pros: Classical rigor; no AI spin. Cons: B2C users see "two verdicts" with potential conflict.

  Option B — Narrative synthesis (AI weaves both into unified answer)

  AI chat generates unified narrative: "Both Kerala Prasna and KP methods agree this is findable. Kerala notes [specific reasoning]; KP confirms [sub-lord
  reasoning]. Combined verdict: yes, within 3-5 days." Astrologer sees raw dual results + optional AI synthesis panel.

  Pros: B2C-friendly; coherent answer. Cons: AI interpretation risk; may smooth over genuine divergences.

  Option C — Structured comparison (tabular agreement flags)

  Side-by-side table: Kerala column + KP column + Agreement column (AGREE / PARTIAL / DIVERGE per sub-question). B2C sees compact agreement view; astrologer sees    
  full trace.

  Pros: Explicit agreement handling. Cons: More structured than narrative; less conversational.

  Option D — Confidence-weighted primary (AI picks one as verdict)

  Each method emits confidence. AI picks higher-confidence method as primary verdict; mentions other as secondary.

  Pros: Single clear answer. Cons: Hides dual-method rigor; opaque to user.

  Option E — Dual display for astrologer + narrative synthesis for B2C

  Split by user type. Astrologer workbench: raw dual display (Option A). B2C AI chat: narrative synthesis (Option B).

  Classical position

  Classical practice has astrologer synthesize mentally. No AI equivalent. Option B or E best for B2C; Option A for astrologer rigor.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option A (astrologer-only; no B2C layer)
  - Parashara's Light: Option A
  - Kerala-specific tools: Option A
  - AI-integrated modern tools (Astro-Vision AI, Astrosage Cloud): Option B (narrative)

  Q5 — AI synthesis strictness:

  - A) Raw dual display, no synthesis — classical rigor; astrologer-only expectation.
  - B) Narrative synthesis (AI unified answer) — B2C-friendly; astrologer sees synthesis + raw.
  - C) Structured comparison with agreement flags — tabular; explicit divergences visible.
  - D) Confidence-weighted primary — AI picks one method as verdict.
  - E) Dual display for astrologer + narrative synthesis for B2C — split by user type. ← my recommendation (astrologer gets classical rigor; B2C gets coherent       
  answer; matches two-user-type pattern throughout Josi).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ E                                                                                                                                                                      
● E10 Q5 locked: Option E — Dual display for astrologer + narrative synthesis for B2C. Astrologer workbench sees raw dual-method results with per-method reasoning   
  trace (classical rigor). B2C AI chat generates unified narrative synthesis weaving both Kerala + KP methods with agreement flag. Split-by-user-type matches Josi's
  two-user-type pattern throughout.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q6 — Cross-source aggregation for E10 (final E10 question)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Background. Per uniform convention (E1b Q7 / E3 Q6 / E5 Q7 / E5b Q7 / E7 Q6) + E9 Q6 single-source exception, E10 has two classical-source branches:                                                                                                                                                                                    
  E10 source structure

  ┌───────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │      Branch       │                 Canonical source                 │                             Variants                             │
  ├───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kerala Prasna     │ Prasna Marga (17th c, Kerala)                    │ Hora Deepika / Prasna Tantra / Swarodaya — commentary extensions │
  ├───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ KP Horary         │ KP Reader Vol.3 (locked single-source per E9 Q6) │ None (single-source)                                             │
  ├───────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Unified synthesis │ Q1 locked both-methods-always                    │ Synthesis logic is engine concern                                │
  └───────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — Strict single-source per branch (Prasna Marga + KP Reader only)

  No commentary variants shipped. Matches E9 Q6 convention. Simplest.

  Option B — Kerala Prasna default + variant astrologer toggle (Hora Deepika / Prasna Tantra / Swarodaya)

  Per uniform variant convention. Classical Kerala commentary texts:
  - Hora Deepika — general Kerala jyotish reference
  - Prasna Tantra — specific horary techniques
  - Swarodaya (Nadi-related) — breath/hora-based omens

  Astrologer-profile toggle exposes commentary variants.

  Option C — Full F8 aggregation with confidence

  Maximum tooling.

  Option D — Prasna Marga + KP strict single-source (Option A)

  Matches E9's single-source approach.

  Practical consideration

  Kerala Prasna has a rich commentary tradition BUT is less variance-heavy than Parashari (E1b/E3) or Tajaka (E5). Prasna Marga is the dominant single-source text — 
  commentary texts mostly elaborate without contradicting.

  Consistency with prior PRDs

  - E1b / E3 / E5 / E5b / E7: Option B uniform variant convention
  - E9 KP: Option A single-source (canon's defining feature)
  - E10: Kerala Prasna is closer to Prasna Marga as "single-source text" than to BPHS-with-multiple-commentaries

  Q6 — Cross-source aggregation for E10:

  - A) Strict single-source per branch (Prasna Marga canonical + KP Reader canonical from E9) — matches E9 Q6 single-source approach. Simplest. ← my recommendation  
  (Kerala Prasna is effectively single-source like KP; don't over-complicate with rarely-used commentary variants).
  - B) Prasna Marga default + Hora Deepika/Prasna Tantra/Swarodaya variant astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - C) Full F8 aggregation with confidence — maximum tooling.
  - D) Per-technique curation — picks best source per technique.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ A

  commentary variant toggles for MVP. Matches E9 Q6 single-source approach. Both branches treat their canonical text as definitive. Same for both user types.

      138 +    "query_id": str,                                                                                                                                      
      139 +    "question_text": str,                                                                                                                                 
      140 +    "moment_utc": datetime,                                                                                                                               
      141 +    "location": {lat: float, lon: float},                                                                                                                 
      142 +    "intent_category": str,  # marriage/career/lost_article/health/etc.                                                                                   
      143 +    "query_house": int,      # Mapped per E9 query map                                                                                                    
      144 +    "kerala_prasna": {                                                                                                                                    
      145 +        "prasna_lagna": rasi,                                                                                                                             
      146 +        "arudha_of_prasna_lagna": rasi,  # JUS rule

      148 +        "navamsa_yes_no": Literal["yes", "no", "maybe"],                                                                                                  
      149 +        "planetary_hour_lord": graha,                                                                                                                     
      150 +        "omens_active": list[OmenResult],  # From user-reported + 15 core + astrologer-enabled extended                                                   
      151 +        "verdict": str,                                                                                                                                   
      152 +        "confidence": float,                                                                                                                              
      153 +        "reasoning_trace": str                                                                                                                            
      154 +    },                                                                                                                                                    
      155 +    "kp_horary": {                                                                                                                                        
      156 +        "horary_chart": ... ,  # From E9 (Krishnamurti ayanamsa + Placidus)                                                                               
      157 +        "cuspal_sub_lord_analysis": {house: str},                                                                                                         
      158 +        "ruling_planets": ... ,  # From E9                                                                                                                
      159 +        "verdict": str,                                                                                                                                   
      160 +        "confidence": float,                                                                                                                              
      161 +        "reasoning_trace": str                                                                                                                            
      162 +    },                                                                                                                                                    
      163 +    "synthesis": {                                                                                                                                        
      164 +        "agreement_level": Literal["AGREE", "PARTIAL", "DIVERGE"],                                                                                        
      165 +        "astrologer_view": "dual_raw",  # Q5 astrologer UX                                                                                                
      166 +        "b2c_narrative": str,           # Q5 B2C AI-generated synthesis                                                                                   
      167 +        "combined_verdict": str,

● E19 Shadbalam Engine — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE new PRD)                                                                                                       
                             
  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Astrologer workbench shows Shadbalam panel — each graha's 6-component strength breakdown (Sthana/Dik/Kala/Chesta/Naisargika/Drik in rupas + percentage + pass/fail   badge per DECISIONS 1.7). Budhan has dual computation (benefic + malefic per DECISIONS 1.7). Ishta-Kashta Phala derivation available. E4a yoga strength formula      (Source D synthesis, locked E4a Q2) consumes this engine.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 Step B — Calculation pipeline (E19 adds comprehensive strength after natal chart)                                                                                  

  1. Natal chart computed with Panchadha Maitri (E23 — parallel or bundled) — friendship matrix required for Sthana Bala sub-components
  2. E19 Shadbalam 6-component computation per BPHS Ch.27-29:
    - Sthana Bala (positional) — 5 sub-components
    - Dik Bala (directional) — graha-specific kendra
    - Kala Bala (temporal) — 7+ sub-components including Paksha Bala with Budhan dual-classification (DECISIONS 1.7)
    - Chesta Bala (motional) — retrograde/combust/speed
    - Naisargika Bala (natural) — fixed ranking
    - Drik Bala (aspectual) — uses V2 graded + special-aspect promotion (E4a Q5 inheritance)
  3. Rupa sum per graha + percentage-of-threshold
  4. Ishta-Kashta Phala derivation (astrologer-only secondary view)
  5. Output feeds E4a yoga strength + E20 Bhava Bala + E27 Maraka-Badhaka etc.

  Remaining E19-specific questions (~7)

  - Q1 — Sthana Bala sub-components (5 classical BPHS vs extended variants)
  - Q2 — Saptavargaja Bala varga set (BPHS 7-varga standard; which 7?)

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Sthana Bala sub-components

  Background. Sthana Bala (positional strength, BPHS Ch.27 v.3-30) has 5 classical sub-components summing to a per-graha value.

  Classical 5 sub-components

  ┌──────────────────────────┬────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │           Sub            │  Sanskrit  │                                         What it measures                                         │
  ├──────────────────────────┼────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Uccha Bala               │ उच्च बल     │ Exaltation — graha's distance from its debilitation point (0 at debility, max at exaltation)     │
  ├──────────────────────────┼────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Saptavargaja Bala        │ सप्तवर्गज बल │ Divisional strength across 7 vargas (D1/D2/D3/D7/D9/D12/D30)                                     │
  ├──────────────────────────┼────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Oja-Yugma Rasi Amsa Bala │ ओज-युग्म बल  │ Odd/even sign placement (some grahas prefer male signs, some female)                             │
  ├──────────────────────────┼────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kendra Bala              │ केन्द्र बल    │ Angular strength (kendra/panaphara/apoklima gives progressively less strength)                   │
  ├──────────────────────────┼────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Drekkana Bala            │ द्रेक्काण बल   │ Decanate strength (1st/2nd/3rd drekkana of rasi has different weighting for male/female planets) │
  └──────────────────────────┴────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — 5 classical sub-components (BPHS Ch.27 canonical)

  Standard Parashari implementation. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman's published Shadbala tables.

  Option B — Extended 7 sub-components (include Bhava Bala contribution + Karaka Bala)

  Some commentators add 2 more. Modern synthesis.

  Option C — BPHS default + astrologer toggle to extended

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Option D — Research BPHS variants + commentary treatments

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.27 v.3-30 unambiguously lists 5 Sthana Bala sub-components. Later commentaries (Phaladeepika, Saravali) don't add more. 5-component is THE classical canon.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: 5-component canonical
  - Parashara's Light: 5-component canonical
  - B.V. Raman published Shadbala tables: 5-component
  - Astrosage: 5-component
  - K.N. Rao treatment: 5-component
