❯ Awhat is residential strenth of all planets                                                                                                                                                                      
● E19 Q1 locked: Option A — 5 classical Sthana Bala sub-components (BPHS Ch.27 canonical): Uccha + Saptavargaja + Oja-Yugma + Kendra + Drekkana. Same for both user  
  types.
                                                                                                                                                                       "Residential strength" of all grahas — explanation
                                                                                                                                                                       "Residential strength" in Parashari typically refers to Sthana Bala (स्थान बल = positional/residential strength) — how strong each graha is based on WHERE it resides                                                                                                                                                                       in the chart. This is Component 1 of the 6-component Shadbalam.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  Each graha's "natural residence" reference points (classical)

  ┌──────────┬────────────────────┬───────────────────────┬─────────────────────┬─────────────────┐
  │  Graha   │ Exaltation (uccha) │ Debilitation (neecha) │      Own sign       │   Mooltrikona   │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Sooriyan │ Mesham 10°         │ Thulam 10°            │ Simmam              │ Simmam 0°-20°   │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Chandran │ Rishabam 3°        │ Viruchigam 3°         │ Kadagam             │ Rishabam 3°-30° │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Sevvai   │ Magaram 28°        │ Kadagam 28°           │ Mesham + Viruchigam │ Mesham 0°-12°   │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Budhan   │ Kanni 15°          │ Meenam 15°            │ Mithunam + Kanni    │ Kanni 15°-20°   │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Guru     │ Kadagam 5°         │ Magaram 5°            │ Dhanusu + Meenam    │ Dhanusu 0°-10°  │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Sukkiran │ Meenam 27°         │ Kanni 27°             │ Rishabam + Thulam   │ Thulam 0°-15°   │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
  │ Sani     │ Thulam 20°         │ Mesham 20°            │ Magaram + Kumbham   │ Kumbham 0°-20°  │
  └──────────┴────────────────────┴───────────────────────┴─────────────────────┴─────────────────┘

  Uccha Bala formula (BPHS Ch.27 v.5-9 — the "residential strength at altitude")

  uccha_bala (virupas) = 60 × |planet_long − debility_long| / 180°

  - At exact exaltation degree: 60 virupas (1 full rupa) — MAX residential strength
  - At exact debilitation degree: 0 virupas — MIN residential strength
  - Linear interpolation between

  Sthana Bala breakdown (5 sub-components)

  For any graha in any chart, residential strength = sum of:

  1. Uccha Bala (0-60 virupas) — exaltation-distance
  2. Saptavargaja Bala (0-45 virupas typically) — dignity across 7 vargas (D1/D2/D3/D7/D9/D12/D30)
  3. Oja-Yugma Rasi Amsa Bala (0-15 virupas) — odd/even sign preference:
    - Male grahas (Sooriyan, Sevvai, Guru) prefer odd signs (Mesham, Mithunam, Simmam, Thulam, Dhanusu, Kumbham)
    - Female grahas (Chandran, Sukkiran) prefer even signs (Rishabam, Kadagam, Kanni, Viruchigam, Magaram, Meenam)
    - Budhan, Sani variable by D1 vs D9
  4. Kendra Bala (0-60 virupas) — 60 in kendra (1/4/7/10), 30 in panaphara (2/5/8/11), 15 in apoklima (3/6/9/12)
  5. Drekkana Bala (0-15 virupas) — 1st/2nd/3rd decanate preferences by graha gender

  Per-graha sample residential strength values (typical ranges)

  Illustrative Sthana Bala totals for each graha across common chart placements:

  ┌─────────────────────────┬──────────────┬──────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┬──────────┬────────┐
  │        Placement        │   Sooriyan   │ Chandran │ Sevvai │ Budhan │  Guru  │ Sukkiran │  Sani  │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼──────────┼────────┤
  │ Own sign + kendra       │ ~120 virupas │   ~120   │  ~125  │  ~120  │  ~120  │   ~125   │  ~120  │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼──────────┼────────┤
  │ Own sign + panaphara    │     ~90      │   ~90    │  ~95   │  ~90   │  ~90   │   ~95    │  ~90   │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼──────────┼────────┤
  │ Exaltation + kendra     │     ~180     │   ~180   │  ~195  │  ~180  │  ~180  │   ~195   │  ~180  │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼──────────┼────────┤
  │ Neutral + kendra        │     ~90      │   ~90    │  ~100  │  ~90   │  ~90   │   ~100   │  ~90   │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼──────────┼────────┤
  │ Debilitation + apoklima │    ~15-30    │  ~15-30  │ ~15-30 │ ~15-30 │ ~15-30 │  ~15-30  │ ~15-30 │
  └─────────────────────────┴──────────────┴──────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┴──────────┴────────┘

  Minimum threshold per graha (for Shadbalam "passing", already locked in DECISIONS 1.7)

  ┌──────────┬───────────────────┬─────────────────────┐
  │  Graha   │ Threshold (rupas) │ Threshold (virupas) │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Sooriyan │        6.5        │         390         │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Chandran │        6.0        │         360         │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Sevvai   │        5.0        │         300         │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Budhan   │        7.0        │         420         │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Guru     │        6.5        │         390         │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Sukkiran │        5.5        │         330         │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Sani     │        5.0        │         300         │
  └──────────┴───────────────────┴─────────────────────┘

  Note: these thresholds are for total Shadbalam (all 6 components), not just Sthana Bala. Sthana Bala alone contributes ~1/6 of the threshold typically.

  Panchadha Maitri (5-fold friendship) — feeds into Saptavargaja Bala

  Residential strength is modulated by the 5-fold friendship between the graha and the sign-lord of where it sits. Natural + Temporal + Composite friendship
  determines dignity within Saptavargaja Bala calculation. This is why E19 depends on E23 Panchadha Maitri (next GAP_CLOSURE PRD in dependency order).

❯ ok                                                                                                                                                                      
● E19 Q2 — Saptavargaja Bala varga set                                                                                                                               

  Background. Saptavargaja Bala (सप्तवर्गज बल) = "strength across 7 vargas" — component of Sthana Bala. For each graha, dignity                                        
  (exaltation/own/friend/neutral/enemy/debility) is evaluated in 7 divisional charts and weighted. But WHICH 7 vargas?
                                                                                                                                                                       ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) · esc to interrupt                                                                                                     Classical BPHS Ch.27 v.10-17 canonical 7                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  1. D1 Rashi                                                                                                                                                        
  2. D2 Hora
  3. D3 Drekkana
  4. D7 Saptamsa
  5. D9 Navamsa
  6. D12 Dwadashamsa
  7. D30 Trimsamsa

  Classical weighting per varga

  Each varga contributes proportional virupas based on graha's dignity:
  - Exaltation / own sign / mooltrikona: max weight
  - Friend's sign: ~60% weight
  - Neutral sign: ~30% weight
  - Enemy sign: ~15% weight
  - Debilitation: 0 weight

  Sum across 7 vargas → Saptavargaja Bala component.

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.27 canonical 7 (D1/D2/D3/D7/D9/D12/D30)

  Standard Parashari implementation. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + K.N. Rao.

  Option B — Alternative 7 (D1/D3/D4/D7/D9/D10/D12)

  Some commentaries substitute D2 Hora with D4 Chaturthamsa, and D30 Trimsamsa with D10 Dasamsa. Argued by modern career-focused astrologers.

  Option C — Extended Dashavarga (10 vargas)

  Add D10 + D16 + D60 to the 7 for richer strength assessment. Non-classical (not canonical Saptavarga); some modern schools use.

  Option D — Shodashavarga (full 16 vargas)

  All 16 divisional charts per DECISIONS 1.9/1.10 metadata. Most comprehensive; not classical for Shadbala specifically.

  Option E — BPHS default + astrologer toggle between Saptavarga / Dashavarga / Shodashavarga

  Uniform variant convention.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.27 explicitly lists the Saptavarga (D1/D2/D3/D7/D9/D12/D30). This is THE classical canon. Modern software uniformly uses this set.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option A (canonical Saptavarga)
  - Parashara's Light: Option A
  - Astrosage: Option A
  - Raman's published Shadbala: Option A
  - K.N. Rao treatment: Option A

  Q2 — Saptavargaja Bala varga set:

  - A) BPHS Ch.27 canonical 7 (D1/D2/D3/D7/D9/D12/D30) — matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Alternative 7 (D1/D3/D4/D7/D9/D10/D12) — modern career-focused variant.
  - C) Extended Dashavarga (10) — adds D10/D16/D60.
  - D) Full Shodashavarga (16) — most comprehensive; non-classical for Shadbala.
  - E) BPHS default + astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

  Background. Kala Bala (काल बल = temporal strength) is the MOST complex of the 6 Shadbalam components.   ┌───────────────────┬───────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    
  │        Sub        │ Sanskrit  │                                                      What it measures                                                       │    
  ├───────────────────┼───────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤    
  │ Nata-Unnata Bala  │ नत-उन्नत   │ Diurnal/nocturnal — some grahas stronger at day, some at night                                                              │

  ├───────────────────┼───────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

  │                   │           │ classification)                                                                                                             │

  │ Tribhaga Bala     │ त्रिभाग बल   │ 3-part division of day/night                                                                                                │

  ├───────────────────┼───────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤    
  │ Ayana Bala        │ अयन बल    │ Declination-based strength (Uttarayana/Dakshinayana + sign-specific)                                                        │    
  ├───────────────────┼───────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤    
  │ Yuddha Bala       │ युद्ध बल    │ Planetary war — when 2 non-luminaries within 1°, the "loser" loses rupas to the "winner"                                    │  
  └───────────────────┴───────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    

  Options

  Option A — BPHS 9 sub-components canonical

  Full classical coverage. Matches all modern Parashari software. Matches PRD locked components in DECISIONS 1.7.

  Option B — Reduced 7 (drop Yuddha + Ayana)

  Simpler; some modern Parashari implementations reduce scope. Loses Yuddha Bala (planetary war) which is classically significant.

  Option C — Extended with modern additions

  Add refinements like seasonal bala variants, hora-duration precision adjustments. Non-classical.

  Option D — BPHS default + astrologer toggle

  Uniform variant convention.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.27 is unambiguous on 9 Kala Bala sub-components. All modern Parashari software + Raman + K.N. Rao use the 9-component set. Yuddha Bala is particularly     
  important when 2 planets are in close conjunction (Sevvai-Sani, Guru-Sukkiran, etc.).

  Q4 — Yuddha Bala (planetary war) rules                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Background. Yuddha Bala (युद्ध बल) = "war strength" — computed when two non-luminaries (any planet EXCEPT Sooriyan and Chandran) are within 1° conjunction in          sidereal longitude. Classical rule: the "winner" gains rupas; the "loser" loses rupas.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Question: how is the winner determined?

  Variant A — Declination-based (BPHS Ch.27 v.48-52 primary)

  Winner: graha with greater northern declination (celestial latitude above ecliptic)
  Loser: graha with lesser declination or southern declination

  Classical rationale: northern declination = "higher in sky" = "victor" in classical imagery.

  Variant B — Brightness-based (some commentaries)

  Winner: graha with greater apparent brightness (magnitude)
  Loser: dimmer graha

  Used by some Rangacharya commentary interpretations.

  Variant C — Motion-speed-based (rare)

  Winner: graha with greater daily motion at the conjunction moment
  Loser: slower graha

  Rare; appears in some Kerala commentaries.

  Variant D — Classical declination (A) + commentary variant toggle

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Variant E — Hybrid — Declination primary; brightness tie-break

  When declination differs clearly → use A. When declinations within 0.1° → fall back to brightness. Nuanced.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.27 v.48-52 explicitly specifies declination-based determination. All modern Parashari software use Variant A. Yuddha Bala activates rarely (~1-2% of charts
   have two non-luminaries within 1°) but is classically significant.

  Worked example

  If Sevvai is at Thulam 15°12' with declination +8°30' and Sani at Thulam 15°45' with declination −3°15':
  - Both non-luminaries within 33' (< 1°) → Yuddha active
  - Sevvai has northern declination; Sani southern
  - Sevvai wins (Variant A) → gains virupas from Sani
  - Amount transferred: proportional to dignity + orb per BPHS formula

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (declination-based)
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Raman published: Variant A
  - K.N. Rao: Variant A

  Q4 — Yuddha Bala winner determination:

  - A) Declination-based (BPHS Ch.27 v.48-52 canonical) — matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Brightness-based — some commentaries.
  - C) Motion-speed-based — rare.
  - D) Classical declination + commentary variant toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - E) Hybrid — declination primary + brightness tie-break — nuanced.
  - F) Other.

  Which?
 option A

● E19 Q4 locked: Option A — Declination-based Yuddha Bala (BPHS Ch.27 v.48-52 canonical). Winner = graha with greater northern declination. Matches all modern
  software + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both user types.

  Q5 — Chesta Bala variant                                                                                                                                           
  
  Background. Chesta Bala (चेष्टा बल) = motional strength per BPHS Ch.27 v.23-30. Measures graha's "effort" based on retrograde status + speed + angular separation f from
   Sooriyan. Classical computation is complex.

  Classical Chesta Bala computation approach

  For outer planets (Sevvai, Guru, Sani):
  - Retrograde grahas get 60 virupas (max)
  - Direct grahas: Chesta = f(graha's angular distance from Sooriyan, graha's own speed relative to mean)

  For inner planets (Budhan, Sukkiran):
  - Based on synodic position relative to Sooriyan
  - Uses classical 8-position or 9-position scheme (Udita/Asta/Vakra/Atichara cycles)

  For Sooriyan and Chandran:
  - Ambiguity — some sources say Chesta Bala = same as Ayana Bala (Sun) or Paksha Bala (Moon); some treat separately

  Computation method variants

  Variant A — BPHS Ch.27 v.23-30 canonical (standard 9-step formula)

  Full BPHS methodology. Uses specific angular-distance-from-Sooriyan tables for inner planets + retrograde scaling for outer. Sun/Moon use Ayana/Paksha.

  Pros: Classical fidelity; matches all modern Parashari software. Cons: Complex implementation.

  Variant B — Modified with combustion penalty

  BPHS primary + combustion penalty for planets within 6° of Sun (inner planet interaction). Some Phaladeepika readings.

  Variant C — Sanjay Rath synthesis

  SJVC Jaimini-inflected Chesta computation with modifications for retrograde classification timing. Modern variant.

  Variant D — Simplified retrograde-only

  Full 60 virupas if retrograde; 0 if direct. Simplification — loses classical nuance.

  Variant E — Astrologer-configurable

  Per-profile selection of variant.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.27 v.23-30 is canonical. Most software follows standard 9-step formula. Modifications are post-classical.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (BPHS canonical)
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Raman published: Variant A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC tools: Variant C

  Q5 — Chesta Bala variant:

  - A) BPHS Ch.27 v.23-30 canonical — full 9-step formula. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) BPHS + combustion penalty — Phaladeepika reading.
  - C) Sanjay Rath SJVC synthesis — modern variant.
  - D) Simplified retrograde-only — loses classical nuance.
  - E) BPHS default + astrologer variant toggle — uniform convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?
 option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E19 Q5 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.27 v.23-30 canonical Chesta Bala. Standard 9-step formula using synodic position for inner planets (Budhan, Sukkiran); retrograde
   + speed for outer planets (Sevvai, Guru, Sani); Sooriyan uses Ayana Bala; Chandran uses Paksha Bala. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Same for both user
  types.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q6 — Ishta-Kashta Phala computation formula                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Background. Ishta-Kashta Phala (इष्ट-कष्ट फल) = "beneficial-harmful result" computed from Shadbalam components. Astrologer-only data per DECISIONS 1.7. Classical      derivation per BPHS Ch.29 uses Uccha Bala + Chesta Bala.                                                                                                           

  Classical formula variants

  Variant A — BPHS Ch.29 canonical (sqrt product)

  Ishta Phala  = sqrt(Uccha Bala × Chesta Bala)       [0-60 range]
  Kashta Phala = 60 − Ishta Phala                     [0-60 range]

  Simple; classical canonical.

  Variant B — Alternate sqrt formula (some commentators)

  Ishta Phala  = sqrt(Uccha Bala × Chesta Bala)
  Kashta Phala = sqrt((60 − Uccha Bala) × (60 − Chesta Bala))

  Alternate; used by some Phaladeepika readings.

  Variant C — Modified incorporating Shadbala total

  Ishta includes Shadbala fraction:
  Ishta Phala = sqrt(Uccha × Chesta) × (Shadbala_total / threshold)

  Modern synthesis; weights by overall strength.

  Variant D — Ernst Wilhelm / Sanjay Rath modern synthesis

  Various modern refinements. Research-tier.

  Variant E — BPHS default + astrologer toggle

  Per uniform convention.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.29 explicitly defines Variant A as Ishta = sqrt(Uccha × Chesta). This is THE canonical formula. Variant B's symmetric Kashta formula is attested but less  
  cited.

  Usage

  - Ishta Phala high + Kashta low → graha brings beneficial results; supports dasa prediction
  - Kashta Phala high → graha produces obstacles; dasa period brings difficulty
  - Feeds into Ayur-Nirnaya (E5b Ayus) and Phala Deepika (dasa phalita interpretation)

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (BPHS canonical sqrt formula)
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Raman published: Variant A

  Q6 — Ishta-Kashta Phala formula:

  - A) BPHS Ch.29 canonical — Ishta = sqrt(Uccha × Chesta) + Kashta = 60 − Ishta. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Alternate symmetric formula — some commentators.
  - C) Modified with Shadbala total weighting — modern synthesis.
  - D) Ernst Wilhelm / Rath refinements — research-tier.
  - E) BPHS default + astrologer toggle — uniform convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E19 Q6 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.29 canonical Ishta-Kashta Phala. Ishta Phala = sqrt(Uccha Bala × Chesta Bala) (0-60 range); Kashta Phala = 60 − Ishta Phala.     
  Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Astrologer-only data per DECISIONS 1.7.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q7 — Cross-source aggregation for E19 Shadbalam (final E19 question)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  Background. Q1-Q6 all locked BPHS canonical formulas + tables. Per uniform variant convention (E1b Q7 / E3 Q6 / E5 Q7 / E5b Q7 / E7 Q6 / E10 Q6), last question      asks whether to expose classical commentary variants as astrologer toggle.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Shadbalam classical source landscape

  - BPHS Ch.27-29 — primary canon (all Q1-Q6 locks from here)
  - Phaladeepika Ch.5 — slightly different formulas for Kala Bala sub-components and Drik Bala
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC synthesis — contemporary interpretive refinements
  - B.V. Raman's Graha and Bhava Balas — classical-faithful treatment; no variant
  - K.N. Rao published Shadbala tables — follows BPHS Santhanam edition

  Options

  Option A — BPHS canonical only, no variants shipped

  Matches E9 KP Q6 single-source approach. Shadbalam as canonical BPHS with no commentary variants. Simplest.

  Option B — BPHS default + Phaladeepika variant astrologer toggle per sub-component

  Uniform variant convention. Where Phaladeepika offers alternate formulas (Paksha Bala for Budhan — but that's locked DECISIONS 1.7; Drik Bala alternate weighting; 
  Nata-Unnata variant), ship both as sibling rules with astrologer toggle.

  Option C — Full F8 aggregation

  Maximum tooling.

  Option D — BPHS + Sanjay Rath synthesis toggle

  Modern SJVC variant.

  Consistency consideration

  - Shadbalam is mathematically dense; even small formula variations produce noticeable numeric differences
  - Astrologers cross-checking against JH expect BPHS canonical (JH uses BPHS)
  - Adding Phaladeepika variant creates "why does my Shadbalam differ from JH?" support burden
  - E9 KP locked Option A (single-source) because KP IS single-source; Shadbalam has classical commentary variation but modern practice converges on BPHS

  Classical software

  - Jagannatha Hora: BPHS canonical only
  - Parashara's Light: BPHS canonical
  - Astrosage: BPHS canonical
  - Raman published: BPHS canonical
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC tools: Sanjay Rath synthesis available

  Q7 — Cross-source aggregation for Shadbalam:

  - A) BPHS canonical only, no variants — matches E9 Q6 single-source pattern + JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + K.N. Rao convergent practice. ← my recommendation      
  (Shadbalam is mathematically precise; avoid cross-checking friction).
  - B) BPHS default + Phaladeepika variant astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention applied.
  - C) Full F8 aggregation — maximum tooling.
  - D) BPHS default + Sanjay Rath synthesis toggle — SJVC modern variant.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E19 Q7 locked: Option A — BPHS canonical only, no variants. Matches E9 Q6 single-source pattern + JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + K.N. Rao convergent practice.      
  Shadbalam is mathematically precise; avoiding variant toggles prevents cross-checking friction.

option A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E23 Panchadha Maitri — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE)                                                                                                               
                             
  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Foundational infrastructure: E23 computes the composite friendship matrix between grahas. Used by E19 Shadbalam (Sthana Bala Saptavargaja dignity), E4a yoga         predicates (planet-in-friend-sign), dasa phalita, and many downstream engines. Astrologer workbench shows 7×7 composite friendship matrix per chart.                                                                                                                                                                                      Step B — Calculation pipeline (E23 inserts after natal chart)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  1. Natal chart computed
  2. E23 Layer 1 — Naisargika Maitri (Natural): Fixed 7×7 matrix per BPHS Ch.3. Lookup only.
  3. E23 Layer 2 — Tatkalika Maitri (Temporal): Computed from chart. For each graha pair, check their position-from-each-other:
    - 2nd/3rd/4th/10th/11th/12th from each other → Friend
    - 1st/5th/6th/7th/8th/9th from each other → Enemy
  4. E23 Layer 3 — Panchadha Maitri (Composite 5-fold): Combine natural + temporal per classical table.
  5. Output: 7×7 composite friendship matrix per chart + per-graha friend/neutral/enemy lists feeding E19

  ---
  Q1 — Natural friendship (Naisargika Maitri) table source

  Background. Fixed classical table of natural friendship relations between the 7 grahas. BPHS Ch.3 v.47-62 specifies. Some commentaries give slight variants.       

  BPHS Ch.3 canonical table

  For each graha: Friends / Enemies / Neutrals

  ┌──────────┬────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
  │  Graha   │          Friends           │          Enemies           │           Neutrals           │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sooriyan │ Chandran, Sevvai, Guru     │ Sukkiran, Sani             │ Budhan                       │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
  │ Chandran │ Sooriyan, Budhan           │ —                          │ Sevvai, Guru, Sukkiran, Sani │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sevvai   │ Sooriyan, Chandran, Guru   │ Budhan                     │ Sukkiran, Sani               │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
  │ Budhan   │ Sooriyan, Sukkiran         │ Chandran                   │ Sevvai, Guru, Sani           │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
  │ Guru     │ Sooriyan, Chandran, Sevvai │ Budhan, Sukkiran           │ Sani                         │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sukkiran │ Budhan, Sani               │ Sooriyan, Chandran         │ Sevvai, Guru                 │
  ├──────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sani     │ Budhan, Sukkiran           │ Sooriyan, Chandran, Sevvai │ Guru                         │
  └──────────┴────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘

  Classical variants

  Variant A — BPHS Ch.3 v.47-62 canonical

  Standard classical table above. Matches all modern Parashari software.

  Variant B — Phaladeepika variant

  Mantreswara's Phaladeepika Ch.1 gives slightly different friendship for Budhan (some versions). Minor; rarely used.

  Variant C — Rangacharya commentary

  Minor adjustments for Rahu/Ketu inclusion.

  Variant D — BPHS default + commentary variant toggle

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.3 table is universally cited. All modern Parashari software + Raman + K.N. Rao use identical table.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: BPHS canonical
  - Parashara's Light: BPHS canonical
  - Astrosage: BPHS canonical
  - Raman published: BPHS canonical

  Q1 — Natural friendship table source:

  - B) Phaladeepika variant — minor differences.
  - C) Rangacharya with Rahu/Ketu extensions — classical commentary.
  - D) BPHS default + commentary toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - E) Other.

                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q2 — Composite 5-fold (Panchadha) categorization                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
  Background. Panchadha Maitri = "five-fold" composite — combines Natural + Temporal friendship layers per BPHS Ch.3 v.63-65.                                                                                                                                                                                                               BPHS Ch.3 classical 5 categories                                                                                                                                   

  ┌──────────────────────────┬──────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │         Category         │ Sanskrit │                                         Natural × Temporal combination                                         │
  ├──────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Adhimitra (great friend) │ अधिमित्र    │ Natural friend + Temporal friend                                                                               │        
  ├──────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Mitra (friend)           │ मित्र      │ (Natural friend + Temporal neutral) OR (Natural neutral + Temporal friend)                                     │
  ├──────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sama (neutral)           │ सम       │ (Natural friend + Temporal enemy) OR (Natural neutral + Temporal neutral) OR (Natural enemy + Temporal friend) │
  ├──────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Shatru (enemy)           │ शत्रु      │ (Natural neutral + Temporal enemy) OR (Natural enemy + Temporal neutral)                                       │
  ├──────────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Adhishatru (great enemy) │ अधिशत्रु    │ Natural enemy + Temporal enemy                                                                                 │        
  └──────────────────────────┴──────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.3 canonical 5-fold

  Standard Parashari. Matches all modern software.

  Option B — Extended 6-fold with "bitter enemy"

  Some commentators add a 6th category between Shatru and Adhishatru for extreme cases (e.g., natural enemy + temporal enemy + debilitated). Rare.

  Option C — 3-fold simplified (friend/neutral/enemy)

  Used when full Shadbala is not computed (quick-dignity usage). Loses classical nuance.

  Option D — 5-fold BPHS default + toggle to 6-fold

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.3 explicitly specifies 5-fold Panchadha. All modern software use this. 6-fold is post-classical synthesis rarely adopted.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: 5-fold canonical
  - Parashara's Light: 5-fold canonical
  - Astrosage: 5-fold canonical
  - Raman published Shadbala: 5-fold canonical
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: 5-fold canonical

  Q2 — Composite 5-fold categorization:

  - A) BPHS Ch.3 canonical 5-fold (Adhimitra / Mitra / Sama / Shatru / Adhishatru) — matches all modern software. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.     
  - B) Extended 6-fold with bitter-enemy — post-classical synthesis.
  - C) 3-fold simplified — loses nuance.
  - D) 5-fold default + 6-fold astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - E) Other.

  Which?
 option a                                                                                                                                                                      
● E23 Q2 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.3 canonical 5-fold Panchadha categorization (Adhimitra / Mitra / Sama / Shatru / Adhishatru). Computed by combining natural      
  friendship (Q1) with temporal friendship (position-based). Matches all modern software + Raman + K.N. Rao + Sanjay Rath. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q3 — Rahu/Ketu friendship treatment                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  Background. BPHS Ch.3 does NOT include Rahu/Ketu in the 7-graha natural friendship table (classical literal is silent on nodes). Modern schools differ on how to     include them.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Variants

  Variant A — No natural friendships for Rahu/Ketu (BPHS literal)

  Nodes have no natural friends/enemies. Tatkalika (temporal) friendship still applies if Rahu/Ketu's positions are used. Sthana Bala Saptavargaja for Rahu/Ketu     
  either skipped entirely OR defaults to Sama (neutral).

  Variant B — Sani-like for Rahu; Sevvai/Guru-like for Ketu (Uttarakalamrita)

  Classical Uttarakalamrita tradition. Rahu inherits Sani's friendships (Friends: Budhan, Sukkiran; Enemies: Sooriyan, Chandran, Sevvai). Ketu inherits Sevvai's OR  
  Guru's friendships (conflicting commentaries).

  Variant C — Nodal-lord inheritance (via natchathiram)

  Rahu's friendships = friendships of the planet ruling Rahu's natchathiram at birth. Chart-specific (not fixed). Used by Sanjay Rath SJVC lineage.

  Variant D — Variant A default + astrologer toggle to Variant B or C

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Similar decisions consistency

  This is parallel to E4a Q5 (Rahu/Ketu drishti) which locked 4-option astrologer toggle (None / 7th only / 5/7/9 Jupiter-like / 3/7/11 Tamil folk). E23 friendship  
  could follow similar flexibility.

  Classical consensus

  - BPHS literal: silent on nodes
  - Modern Parashari practice: split; most software use Variant A as default with optional Variant B
  - Research tradition: Sanjay Rath Variant C

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A default + astrologer toggle to Variant B (Uttarakalamrita)
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A only
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Variant C (nodal-lord inheritance)

  Q3 — Rahu/Ketu friendship treatment:

  - A) No natural friendships for Rahu/Ketu (BPHS literal) + Tatkalika-only participation — matches PL + Astrosage literal reading.
  - B) Sani-like for Rahu; Sevvai-like for Ketu (Uttarakalamrita) — classical commentary.
  - C) Nodal-lord inheritance via natchathiram — Sanjay Rath SJVC.
  - D) Variant A default + astrologer profile toggle to B or C — matches JH convention + uniform variant pattern. Parallels E4a Q5 4-option toggle for node
  treatment. ← my recommendation.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ option D

   (Sani-like Rahu, Sevvai-like Ketu — Variant B) OR Sanjay Rath nodal-lord-inheritance (Variant C). Matches JH convention + uniform variant pattern + parallels E4a

  Step A — User story (context only)

  Astrologer workbench Bhava Bala panel shows 12-bhava strength table — each bhava's 4-component strength breakdown (Bhavadhipathi + Dig + Drishti + Karaka) in rupas
   + percentage + pass/fail threshold. Distinct from Ashtakavargam SAV (E2a) which gives bindu-based house assessment. Bhava Bala answers: "Which houses in the chart
   are classically strong?" from BPHS Ch.31 perspective.

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E20 depends on E19 Shadbalam + E4a Drishti)

  1. Natal chart + Bhava Chalit (Sripati per DECISIONS 1.3) computed
  2. E19 Shadbalam completed (for Bhavadhipathi Bala — lord-strength)
  3. E20 4-component Bhava Bala per house (12 bhavas):
    - Bhavadhipathi Bala: Shadbalam of the bhava-lord (inherited from E19)
    - Bhava Dig Bala: Directional strength of the bhava itself
    - Bhava Drishti Bala: Benefic/malefic aspect-sum on the bhava (uses E4a Q5 V2 graded + special-aspect promotion + Rahu/Ketu 7th-only default)
    - Bhava Karaka Bala: Natural karaka strength per house (Sooriyan karaka for 1st/9th/10th, Chandran for 4th, etc.)
  4. Sum per bhava → Bhava Bala in rupas
  5. Output: 12-bhava strength breakdown for astrologer workbench

  Remaining E20-specific questions (~4)

  - Q1 — Bhava Bala components (BPHS 4 canonical vs extended variants)
  - Q2 — Bhava chart source for computation (Whole Sign or Bhava Chalit/Sripati — given DECISIONS 1.3 locks both)
  - Q3 — Bhava Karaka assignments per house (classical table)
  - Q4 — Cross-source aggregation

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Bhava Bala components

  Background. BPHS Ch.31 specifies components of Bhava Bala. Different sources list 4, 5, or 6 components.

  Classical BPHS 4 components

  1. Bhavadhipathi Bala (भावाधिपति बल) — strength of the bhava-lord (lord's Shadbalam × factor)
  2. Bhava Dig Bala (भाव दिग्बल) — directional strength of the bhava (kendra > panaphara > apoklima for malefic; trikona > other for benefic)
  3. Bhava Drishti Bala (भाव दृष्टिबल) — benefic drishti minus malefic drishti on the bhava cusp
  4. Bhava Karaka Bala (भाव कारक बल) — natural house-karaka's Shadbalam

  Classical sources and variants

  Variant A — BPHS Ch.31 canonical 4 components

  Standard Parashari. All modern software use this.

  Variant B — Extended 5 with Rashi Dig Bala

  Add Rashi Dig Bala (sign-directional) as 5th component. Some Phaladeepika readings.

  Variant C — Extended 6 with Drekkana

  Add Bhava Drekkana Bala (decanate-based). Rare commentary.

  Variant D — Modified Shadbala integration

  Use full Shadbalam percentage instead of Bhavadhipathi + Karaka separately. Non-canonical; simplified.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.31 v.1-15 explicitly specifies 4 components. Modern software uniformly uses these 4.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: 4 canonical
  - Parashara's Light: 4 canonical
  - Astrosage: 4 canonical
  - Raman published: 4 canonical
  - K.N. Rao: 4 canonical

  Q1 — Bhava Bala components:

  - A) BPHS Ch.31 canonical 4 components (Bhavadhipathi + Dig + Drishti + Karaka) — matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both user types. ← my   
  recommendation.
  - B) Extended 5 with Rashi Dig Bala — Phaladeepika variant.
  - C) Extended 6 with Drekkana — rare commentary.
  - D) Modified Shadbala integration — non-canonical simplified.
  - E) BPHS default + astrologer variant toggle — uniform convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Baked for 45s
 A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E20 Q1 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.31 canonical 4 components (Bhavadhipathi + Dig + Drishti + Karaka). Matches all modern software + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both
   user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q2 — Bhava chart source for Bhava Bala computation                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
  Background. DECISIONS 1.3 locked Whole Sign (rasi chart) + Bhava Chalit/Sripati (bhava chart) as parallel charts. For Bhava Bala specifically, which chart is        authoritative?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  The choice matters

  - Whole Sign: each rasi = 1 bhava; bhava lord = rasi lord; simple.
  - Bhava Chalit/Sripati: bhava midpoints shift from rasi midpoints; planets near bhava-sandhi may switch bhavas; more "astronomical."

  Planets near bhava boundaries (e.g., planet at 28° of a rasi when Lagna degree is 3°) will be in different bhavas between the two conventions. Their contribution  
  to Bhava Bala components (Drishti, Karaka) can flip.

  Options

  Option A — Bhava Chalit/Sripati (modern standard)

  Use Sripati bhava midpoints for all Bhava Bala computations. "Actual bhava" per geometric Sripati calculation. Most classical Parashari commentators from Sripati  
  era (11th c) onwards use this.

  Option B — Whole Sign (BPHS literal)

  Use rasi chart. BPHS Ch.31 was written before Sripati formalization; literal BPHS reading is rasi-based. Hellenistic-aligned.

  Option C — Both computed; both shown

  Bhava Bala computed in both charts; astrologer sees both values. Dual-chart approach.

  Option D — Per-component routing

  - Bhavadhipathi + Karaka components → rasi chart (sign-lord dignity based on rasi)
  - Drishti component → bhava chalit (aspects measured to actual bhava cusps)
  - Dig Bala → same either way

  Nuanced; matches some classical commentators.

  Classical consensus

  - BPHS Ch.31 literal: rasi-based (pre-Sripati)
  - Sripati Paddhati (11th c) onward: bhava chalit for bhava computations
  - Modern Parashari practice: Bhava Chalit/Sripati dominant
  - Hellenistic-Vedic synthesis: Whole Sign

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option A (Bhava Chalit/Sripati)
  - Parashara's Light: Option A
  - Astrosage: Option A
  - Raman published Bhava Bala tables: Option A (Sripati)

  Q2 — Bhava chart source:

  - A) Bhava Chalit/Sripati — modern Parashari standard; matches all modern software + Raman tables + post-Sripati classical consensus. Same for both user types. ←  
  my recommendation.
  - B) Whole Sign (BPHS literal) — pre-Sripati literal reading.
  - C) Both computed and shown — dual-chart.
  - D) Per-component routing — rasi for sign-lord; bhava chalit for drishti.
  - E) Bhava Chalit default + astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which? 
   A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E20 Q2 locked: Option A — Bhava Chalit/Sripati as bhava chart source for all Bhava Bala computations. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + post-Sripati Parashari 
  consensus. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q3 — Bhava Karaka assignments per house                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  Background. Bhava Karaka (भाव कारक = natural significator per house) is a classical table. BPHS Ch.12 v.1-13 gives canonical assignments. Each bhava has one or more                                                                                                                                                                      grahas as its natural karaka.                                                                                                                                      

  Classical BPHS Ch.12 canonical table

  ┌───────┬─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┐
  │ Bhava │        Primary karaka(s)        │     What it signifies     │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   1   │ Sooriyan                        │ Body, self, vitality      │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   2   │ Guru                            │ Wealth, family, speech    │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   3   │ Sevvai                          │ Siblings, courage         │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   4   │ Chandran + Sukkiran             │ Home, mother, vehicles    │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   5   │ Guru                            │ Intellect, progeny        │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   6   │ Sevvai + Sani                   │ Health issues, enemies    │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   7   │ Sukkiran                        │ Partner, marriage         │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   8   │ Sani                            │ Longevity, hidden matters │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │   9   │ Guru                            │ Fortune, dharma, father   │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │  10   │ Sooriyan + Budhan + Guru + Sani │ Career (all 4 as karakas) │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │  11   │ Guru                            │ Gains, elder siblings     │
  ├───────┼─────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │  12   │ Sani                            │ Losses, retreats, moksha  │
  └───────┴─────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.12 canonical table

  Standard Parashari. All modern software use this.

  Option B — Jaimini Chara Karakas (movable significators)

  Use Jaimini's chart-specific karakas (AK, AmK, BK, MK, PK, GK, DK from E3 Q1) instead of BPHS natural karakas. Not standard for Bhava Bala.

  Option C — BPHS primary + classical secondary karakas

  Include secondary karakas listed in commentaries (e.g., 4th bhava secondary karaka = Budhan per some sources). Multi-karaka sum.

  Option D — Modified with Parivara Devata karakas

  Include deity-specific karakas (e.g., Ganesha for 1st, Durga for 4th). Non-standard Parashari; devotional tradition.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.12 v.1-13 is universally cited. Modern software uniformly use this table.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: BPHS Ch.12 canonical
  - Parashara's Light: BPHS canonical
  - Astrosage: BPHS canonical
  - Raman published: BPHS canonical
  - K.N. Rao: BPHS canonical

  Q3 — Bhava Karaka assignments:

  - A) BPHS Ch.12 canonical table — matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Jaimini Chara Karakas substitution — non-standard for Bhava Bala.
  - C) BPHS primary + secondary karakas from commentaries — multi-karaka sum.
  - D) Modified with Parivara Devata karakas — devotional tradition.
  - E) BPHS default + astrologer variant toggle — uniform convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?
   A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E20 Q3 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.12 canonical Bhava Karaka table. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both user types.

● E21 Vimshopaka Bala — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE)                                                                                                                

  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Astrologer workbench shows per-graha Vimshopaka Bala score (0-20 range) — multi-varga weighted strength. Distinct from Shadbalam (E19 = 6-component                  positional/temporal/etc.) and Sthana Saptavargaja (E19 Q2 = dignity across 7 vargas weighted equally). Vimshopaka uses classical varga-specific weights summing to   20 points, giving a cleaner single-score-per-graha strength metric.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Step B — Calculation pipeline (E21 depends on E23 Panchadha Maitri for dignity)                                                                                    

  1. Natal chart + divisional charts (D1-D60) computed
  2. E23 Panchadha Maitri completed (for dignity evaluation in each varga)
  3. E21 Vimshopaka computation per graha:
    - For each varga in the scheme: determine graha's dignity (exalted/own/mooltrikona/friend/neutral/enemy/debilitated)
    - Multiply varga's weight × dignity-fraction
    - Sum across all vargas in scheme → Vimshopaka (0-20)
  4. Output per-graha Vimshopaka score + per-varga contribution breakdown

  What's already locked

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Primary Vimshopaka scheme

  Background. BPHS Ch.7 v.30-55 describes three Vimshopaka schemes — each summing to 20 points but across different varga counts. Which is primary for Josi?

  Three classical schemes

  Scheme 1 — Sapta-Vargi Vimshopaka (7 vargas)

  ┌─────────────────┬────────┐
  │      Varga      │ Weight │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D1 Rashi        │   5    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D2 Hora         │   2    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D3 Drekkana     │   3    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D7 Saptamsa     │  2.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D9 Navamsa      │  4.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D12 Dwadashamsa │   2    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D30 Trimsamsa   │   1    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Total           │   20   │
  └─────────────────┴────────┘

  Scheme 2 — Dasa-Vargi Vimshopaka (10 vargas)

  ┌─────────────────┬────────┐
  │      Varga      │ Weight │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D1 Rashi        │   3    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D2 Hora         │  1.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D3 Drekkana     │  1.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D7 Saptamsa     │  1.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D9 Navamsa      │   3    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D10 Dasamsa     │  1.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D12 Dwadashamsa │  1.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D16 Shodasamsa  │  1.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D30 Trimsamsa   │  1.5   │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ D60 Shashtiamsa │   3    │
  ├─────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Total           │   20   │
  └─────────────────┴────────┘

  Scheme 3 — Shodasa-Vargi Vimshopaka (16 vargas)

  Covers all 16 classical vargas. Weights distributed across 16 vargas summing to 20. Most comprehensive; research-tier.

  Options

  Option A — Ship ONLY Sapta-Vargi (most commonly used)

  Simplest; matches Saptavarga from E19 Q2. Loses Dasa/Shodasa depth.

  Option B — Ship ONLY Dasa-Vargi (most-cited in Raman's published charts)

  Dasa-Vargi is B.V. Raman's preferred scheme for published Vimshopaka analysis.

  Option C — Ship all 3 schemes; astrologer picks per chart

  Maximum flexibility. Computation cost 3× but acceptable.

  Option D — Primary = Dasa-Vargi (Raman standard) + astrologer toggle to Sapta/Shodasa

  Default Raman-aligned; power-user options.

  Option E — Dasa-Vargi default for Astrologer; B2C sees simplified Sapta-Vargi

  Split by user type.

  Classical position

  - BPHS Ch.7 describes all 3 — no primary designation
  - B.V. Raman's published Shadbala works: Dasa-Vargi primary
  - K.N. Rao: Dasa-Vargi primary
  - P.V.R. Narasimha Rao (JH): Dasa-Vargi primary
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Shodasa-Vargi primary for deep analysis

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Dasa-Vargi primary + Sapta/Shodasa toggles
  - Parashara's Light: Dasa-Vargi
  - Astrosage: Dasa-Vargi
  - Raman published tables: Dasa-Vargi

  Q1 — Primary Vimshopaka scheme:

  - A) Sapta-Vargi only — simplest; pairs with E19 Q2 Saptavarga.
  - B) Dasa-Vargi only — Raman + K.N. Rao + JH standard.
  - C) All 3 schemes, astrologer picks — maximum flexibility.
  - D) Dasa-Vargi default + astrologer toggle to Sapta/Shodasa — matches JH convention + Raman standard. Uniform variant convention. ← my recommendation.
  - E) Dasa-Vargi for Astrologer; Sapta-Vargi for B2C — split by user type.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

  Q2 — Weight values per varga                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  Background. Each Vimshopaka scheme has specific weights per varga summing to 20. BPHS Ch.7 v.30-55 provides canonical values. Q2 confirms which values.                                                                                                                                                                                   BPHS Ch.7 canonical weights (for all 3 schemes)                                                                                                                    

  Sapta-Vargi (7 vargas):
  D1=5 · D2=2 · D3=3 · D7=2.5 · D9=4.5 · D12=2 · D30=1 → Total 20

  Dasa-Vargi (10 vargas):
  D1=3 · D2=1.5 · D3=1.5 · D7=1.5 · D9=3 · D10=1.5 · D12=1.5 · D16=1.5 · D30=1.5 · D60=3 → Total 20

  Shodasa-Vargi (16 vargas):
  D1=3.5 · D2=1 · D3=1 · D4=0.5 · D7=0.5 · D9=3 · D10=0.5 · D12=0.5 · D16=2 · D20=0.5 · D24=0.5 · D27=0.5 · D30=1 · D40=0.5 · D45=1.5 · D60=4 → Total 20

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.7 canonical weights for all 3 schemes

  Classical canon; matches all modern software. Same weights for B2C and astrologer.

  Option B — Phaladeepika variant weights

  Slight reshuffling of Dasa-Vargi weights per Mantreswara. Rare.

  Option C — Modern synthesis weights

  Research tweaks (more weight to D9 Navamsa reflecting its modern importance). Non-classical.

  Option D — BPHS default + research-variant toggle

  Uniform variant convention.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.7 weights are universal. All modern software use them.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: BPHS canonical
  - Parashara's Light: BPHS canonical
  - Astrosage: BPHS canonical
  - Raman published: BPHS canonical

  Q2 — Vimshopaka weights per varga:

  - A) BPHS Ch.7 canonical for all 3 schemes — matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Phaladeepika variant — rare commentary.
  - C) Modern synthesis — non-classical.
  - D) BPHS default + research toggle — uniform convention.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

  D3=1.5, D7=1.5, D9=3, D10=1.5, D12=1.5, D16=1.5, D30=1.5, D60=3. Shodasa-Vargi per BPHS canonical. Matches all modern software + Raman.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q3 — Dignity-fraction formula                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Background. For each varga in the Vimshopaka scheme, the graha's dignity determines what FRACTION of the varga's weight contributes to the total. BPHS Ch.7 v.55-60   specifies fractions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  Classical BPHS Ch.7 dignity-fraction table

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────┐
  │ Dignity (Panchadha Maitri from E23) │ Fraction of varga weight │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Exaltation (uccha)                  │        1.0 (full)        │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Mooltrikona (mula-trikona)          │           0.75           │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Own sign (swa-kshetra)              │           0.75           │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Great Friend (adhimitra)            │           0.5            │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Friend (mitra)                      │          0.375           │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Neutral (sama)                      │           0.25           │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Enemy (shatru)                      │          0.125           │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Great Enemy (adhishatru)            │          0.0625          │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ Debilitation (neecha)               │           0.0            │
  └─────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┘

  Example

  Graha in own sign in D1 (Rashi) under Dasa-Vargi scheme:
  - D1 weight = 3.0
  - Dignity "own sign" = 0.75 fraction
  - Contribution to Vimshopaka = 3.0 × 0.75 = 2.25 points

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.7 canonical fractions (9 dignity levels)

  Standard. Matches all modern software.

  Option B — Phaladeepika variant (slightly different fractions)

  Mantreswara's Phaladeepika Ch.8 gives 0.5 for own-sign (vs BPHS 0.75). Minor.

  Option C — Simplified 3-level (full / half / none)

  Ignores gradations. Simpler but loses classical nuance.

  Option D — BPHS default + astrologer toggle

  Uniform variant convention.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.7 v.55-60 is the standard. Modern software use these fractions.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: BPHS canonical 9-level
  - Parashara's Light: BPHS canonical
  - Astrosage: BPHS canonical
  - Raman published: BPHS canonical

  Q3 — Dignity-fraction formula:

  - A) BPHS Ch.7 canonical 9 dignity levels — matches all modern software. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Phaladeepika variant — minor differences.
  - C) Simplified 3-level — loses nuance.
  - D) BPHS default + astrologer toggle — uniform convention.
  - E) Other.

  Which?
A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E21 Q3 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.7 canonical 9 dignity-fraction levels (Exaltation 1.0 / Mooltrikona 0.75 / Own sign 0.75 / Great Friend 0.5 / Friend 0.375 /     
  Neutral 0.25 / Enemy 0.125 / Great Enemy 0.0625 / Debilitation 0.0). Matches all modern software + Raman. Same for both user types.

 A

● E22 Avastha Suite — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE)

  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Astrologer workbench Avastha panel shows per-graha functional states across multiple classical avastha schemes simultaneously. Baladi (5-fold position-in-rasi),   
  Jagrat-Swapna-Sushupti (3-fold dignity-based), 9-fold (Deepta/Swastha/Mudita/Shanta/Dinam/Duhkhita/Khala/Kopam/Sobhita), Lajjitadi (6-fold configuration-based),
  and optional 12-fold synthesis. Each graha's avasthas modulate its functional output — a "Kumara-Jagrat-Deepta" Guru behaves differently from a
  "Vriddha-Sushupti-Duhkhita" Guru.

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E22 depends on E23 Panchadha Maitri)

  1. Natal chart + divisional charts computed
  2. E23 Panchadha Maitri completed (for dignity-based avastha schemes)
  3. E22 per-graha avastha computation across schemes:
    - Baladi (5-fold): position-within-rasi (0-6°/6-12°/12-18°/18-24°/24-30°) with gender-adjusted order
    - Jagrat-Swapna-Sushupti (3-fold): dignity-based
    - 9-fold (Deepta/Swastha/etc.): dignity + position + aspect + combustion composite
    - Lajjitadi (6-fold): configuration-based (specific combinations with other grahas)
    - Arohana-Avarohana: declination/orbit-based ascending/descending
  4. Output: per-graha multi-avastha state vector for all 7 grahas (+ Rahu/Ketu per lineage)

  What's already locked

  ┌───────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┬───────────────┐
  │             Decision              │             Value              │      Ref      │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤
  │ Ayanamsa                          │ Lahiri                         │ DECISIONS 1.2 │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Avastha scheme inclusion scope

  Background. E22 PRD + GAP_CLOSURE Section 1.B proposes 5 avastha schemes. Classical literature includes others (12-fold, Arohana-Avarohana, etc.). Which ship MVP? 

  Classical avastha schemes

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┐
  │                                  Scheme                                   │         Source         │      Usage frequency      │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │ Baladi (5-fold) — Bala/Kumara/Yuva/Vriddha/Mrita                          │ BPHS Ch.45             │  Universal — most-cited   │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │ Jagrat-Swapna-Sushupti (3-fold) — dignity-based                           │ BPHS Ch.46             │          Common           │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │ 9-fold (Deepta/Swastha/Mudita/Shanta/Dinam/Duhkhita/Khala/Kopam/Sobhita)  │ BPHS Ch.46 v.15-25     │    Advanced astrologer    │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │ Lajjitadi (6-fold) — Lajjita/Garvita/Kshudhita/Trishita/Muditha/Kshobhita │ BPHS Ch.46 v.30-40     │ Astrologer specialization │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │ Arohana-Avarohana — declination-based ascending/descending                │ Phaladeepika Ch.5      │       Research-tier       │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
  │ 12-fold synthesis                                                         │ Rangacharya commentary │       Rare research       │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — Ship all 5 (Baladi + 3-fold + 9-fold + Lajjitadi + Arohana-Avarohana)

  Comprehensive. Matches GAP_CLOSURE scope + PRD intent.

  Option B — Ship 3 core (Baladi + 3-fold + Lajjitadi)

  Tight scope. Most-used schemes. Skip 9-fold + Arohana.

  Option C — Ship 4 (all except Arohana-Avarohana)

  Research-tier Arohana deferred.

  Option D — Tiered: core 3 always-available + 9-fold astrologer toggle + Arohana research flag

  Matches tiered convention (E8b Q1, E7).

  Option E — All 5 for astrologer; Baladi + 3-fold only for B2C

  Split by user type.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: All 5 (Option A)

                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q2 — Baladi gender-order assignments per graha                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  Background. Baladi 5-fold divides each rasi (30°) into 5 segments of 6° each. The order of the 5 states (Bala / Kumara / Yuva / Vriddha / Mrita) DEPENDS ON GRAHA    GENDER:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  - Male graha order (forward): Bala (0-6°) → Kumara (6-12°) → Yuva (12-18°) → Vriddha (18-24°) → Mrita (24-30°)
  - Female graha order (reverse): Mrita (0-6°) → Vriddha (6-12°) → Yuva (12-18°) → Kumara (18-24°) → Bala (24-30°)

  Gender assignments — classical disagreement

  ┌──────────┬───────────────────────────┬──────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Graha   │        BPHS Ch.45         │ Phaladeepika │        Sign-parity convention         │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sooriyan │           Male            │     Male     │                 Male                  │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Chandran │          Female           │    Female    │                Female                 │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sevvai   │           Male            │     Male     │                 Male                  │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Budhan   │           Male            │   Variable   │ Sign-dependent (odd=male/even=female) │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Guru     │           Male            │     Male     │                 Male                  │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sukkiran │          Female           │    Female    │                Female                 │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────┼──────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sani     │ Female (some say neutral) │   Variable   │            Sign-dependent             │
  └──────────┴───────────────────────────┴──────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────┘

  Budhan and Sani are the disputed ones. BPHS literal gives fixed (Budhan=male, Sani=female). Phaladeepika and Rangacharya commentary say variable per sign
  placement.

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.45 fixed assignments

  Sooriyan/Sevvai/Guru/Budhan = Male. Chandran/Sukkiran/Sani = Female. Fixed regardless of sign.

  Option B — Sign-parity for Budhan and Sani

  Sooriyan/Sevvai/Guru fixed Male. Chandran/Sukkiran fixed Female. Budhan/Sani follow sign-parity (odd-sign placement = male order; even-sign = female).

  Option C — All grahas sign-parity-based

  Pure sign-parity for everyone. Non-classical.

  Option D — BPHS default + astrologer toggle to Phaladeepika sign-parity

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option A (BPHS fixed)
  - Parashara's Light: Option A
  - Astrosage: Option A
  - Raman published: Option A
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Option B (sign-parity for Budhan/Sani)

  Q2 — Baladi gender-order assignments:

  - A) BPHS Ch.45 fixed assignments — Sooriyan/Sevvai/Guru/Budhan male; Chandran/Sukkiran/Sani female. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman.
  - B) Sign-parity for Budhan/Sani — Phaladeepika variant.
  - C) All sign-parity — non-classical.
  - D) BPHS default + sign-parity toggle for Budhan/Sani — uniform variant convention. ← my recommendation (matches BPHS-canonical pattern + Sanjay Rath toggle for  
  SJVC users).
  - E) Other.

  Which?

  Q3 — 9-fold (Deepta/Swastha/etc.) composite formula source                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  Background. 9-fold avastha (BPHS Ch.46 v.15-25) categorizes each graha into one of 9 composite states:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ┌─────────────────────┬──────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                                                                               
  │        State        │ Sanskrit │                Classical trigger                │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Deepta (radiant)    │ दीप्त      │ Exalted                                         │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Swastha (well)      │ स्वस्थ     │ Own sign / Mooltrikona                          │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Mudita (delighted)  │ मुदित      │ Friend's sign                                   │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Shanta (peaceful)   │ शान्त      │ Benefic aspect + own/friend                     │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Dinam (pathetic)    │ दीन       │ Neutral / enemy sign                            │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Duhkhita (sad)      │ दुःखित     │ Enemy sign                                      │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Khala (wicked)      │ खल       │ Debilitation                                    │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kopam (angry)       │ कोप       │ Combustion (astangata)                          │
  ├─────────────────────┼──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sobhita (brilliant) │ शोभित      │ Conjunction with great friend + benefic aspects │
  └─────────────────────┴──────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Each graha classified into 1 state based on dignity + placement + aspects + combustion.

  Classical source variants

  Variant A — BPHS Ch.46 v.15-25 canonical

  Standard Parashari. Matches JH + PL + Raman.

  Variant B — Phaladeepika Ch.4 variant

  Slight threshold differences (e.g., "Khala" extends to enemy sign + retrograde, not just debility). Minor.

  Variant C — Sanjay Rath modification

  Rath's SJVC teaching adds finer composite rules (combustion OR debility for Khala; retrograde modifies state). Modern.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.46 is canonical. All modern software use this table.

  Q3 — 9-fold composite formula source:

  - A) BPHS Ch.46 v.15-25 canonical — matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Phaladeepika variant — minor threshold differences.
  - C) Sanjay Rath SJVC modification — modern synthesis.
  - D) BPHS default + astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

  + Astrosage + Raman. Same for both user types.
