  Q4 — Lajjitadi 6-fold configurations table source                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
  Background. Lajjitadi 6-fold avastha (BPHS Ch.46 v.30-40) classifies each graha based on specific configurations with other grahas.                                                                                                                                                                                                       Classical 6 Lajjitadi states (BPHS Ch.46)                                                                                                                          

  ┌──────────────────────┬──────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │        State         │ Sanskrit │                              Classical trigger                               │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Lajjita (ashamed)    │ लज्जित     │ Graha in 5th house afflicted by 4+ malefics OR specific conjunction patterns │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Garvita (proud)      │ गर्वित     │ Graha in exaltation or mooltrikona + kendra placement                        │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kshudhita (hungry)   │ क्षुधित     │ Graha in enemy sign with malefic conjunction/aspect                          │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Trishita (thirsty)   │ तृषित      │ Graha in watery sign (Kadagam/Viruchigam/Meenam) with malefic affliction     │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Muditha (delighted)  │ मुदित      │ Graha in friend's sign with benefic aspect                                   │
  ├──────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kshobhita (agitated) │ क्षोभित     │ Graha conjunct/aspected by Sooriyan + malefic                                │
  └──────────────────────┴──────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Variants

  Option A — BPHS Ch.46 v.30-40 canonical

  Standard Parashari. All modern software use this.

  Option B — Phaladeepika variant

  Slight threshold differences.

  Option C — Rangacharya synthesis

  Adds additional edge-case handling.

  ---
  Q1 — Maraka house identification

  Background. BPHS Ch.44 v.1-5 defines Marakas. Question: strict classical 2nd+7th only, or extended variants?

  Classical Maraka identification

  Variant A — Strict 2nd + 7th (BPHS Ch.44 canonical)

  - Primary Marakas: 2nd house lord, 7th house lord
  - Secondary: planets occupying 2nd/7th houses; planets conjunct 2nd/7th lords
  - Simple, universally-cited.

  Variant B — Extended with Maraka-adjacent

  Adds 2nd + 7th from the 8th house (considered the house of longevity):
  - 2nd from 8th = 9th
  - 7th from 8th = 2nd
  - Combined with standard 2+7, gives 2/7/9 as Maraka set

  Used by some Phaladeepika readings.

  Variant C — Full Maraka hierarchy (BPHS v.10-15 extended)

  Primary: 2nd + 7th lords
  Secondary: planets in 2nd/7th
  Tertiary: planets in 6th/8th/12th (Dusthana houses — also death-associated)

  Comprehensive; more signals.

  Variant D — Strict + Jaimini Shoola Dasha integration

  Use Maraka identification for Shoola Dasha (Jaimini maraka-timing) integration. Requires E1c Extended Dasa Pack.

  Classical consensus

  - BPHS Ch.44: strict 2nd + 7th primary
  - Phaladeepika Ch.7: same
  - Modern Parashari practice: Variant A universal

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Raman published: Variant A

  Q1 — Maraka house identification:

  - A) Strict 2nd + 7th (BPHS Ch.44 canonical) — primary lords + planets in 2/7 + conjuncts. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Same for both user types. ← my     
  recommendation.
  - B) Extended 2+7+9 — Phaladeepika variant.
  - C) Full Maraka hierarchy with Dusthana — comprehensive signals.
  - D) Variant A + Jaimini Shoola Dasha integration — requires E1c dependency.
  - E) BPHS default + astrologer variant toggle — uniform convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Cogitated for 45s
 A

● E27 Q1 locked: Option A — Strict 2nd + 7th Maraka houses (BPHS Ch.44 canonical). Primary Marakas = 2nd + 7th house lords. Secondary = planets in 2nd/7th + planets 
  conjunct 2nd/7th lords. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Same for both user types.

  ---
  Q2 — Badhaka house rule

  Background. Badhaka (बाधक = "obstructor") is Lagna-type-dependent per BPHS Ch.44 v.15-20.

  Classical Badhaka triad

  ┌────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────┐
  │     Lagna type     │              Lagna signs              │ Badhaka house │
  ├────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤
  │ Movable (Chara)    │ Mesham, Kadagam, Thulam, Magaram      │     11th      │
  ├────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤
  │ Fixed (Sthira)     │ Rishabam, Simmam, Viruchigam, Kumbham │      9th      │
  ├────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤
  │ Dual (Dwiswabhava) │ Mithunam, Kanni, Dhanusu, Meenam      │      7th      │
  └────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.44 canonical triad

  Matches all modern software + Raman + K.N. Rao.

  Option B — Uttarakalamrita extended

  Some Uttarakalamrita commentators add Badhaka-adjacent-signs. Rare.

  Option C — Modern interpretive synthesis

  Non-classical variations.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.44 triad is universal. No competing variant.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: BPHS triad
  - Parashara's Light: BPHS triad
  - Astrosage: BPHS triad
  - Raman published: BPHS triad
  - K.N. Rao: BPHS triad

  Q2 — Badhaka house rule:

  - A) BPHS Ch.44 canonical triad — Movable→11, Fixed→9, Dual→7. Matches all modern software + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.      
  - B) Uttarakalamrita extended — rare commentary.
  - C) Modern synthesis — non-classical.
  - D) BPHS default + astrologer toggle — uniform convention.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ A

● E27 Q2 locked: Option A — BPHS Ch.44 canonical Badhaka triad. Movable Lagna → 11th Badhaka; Fixed Lagna → 9th Badhaka; Dual Lagna → 7th Badhaka. Matches all modern
   software + Raman + K.N. Rao. Same for both user types.

  ---
  Q3 — Badhaka lord activation criteria

  Background. Badhaka HOUSE identified by Q2. Badhaka LORD = ruler of that house. But WHEN does the Badhaka lord's obstruction effect activate?

  Classical activation rules (BPHS Ch.44 v.20-25)

  Variant A — Classical strict

  Badhaka lord considered "activated" when:
  - In Badhaka house itself (self-placement)
  - In 6/8/12 (dusthana)
  - Conjunct or aspected by malefics
  - During its dasha/antardasha period

  Variant B — Strength-based (Shadbala-gated)

  Badhaka lord only considered "strongly obstructive" if its Shadbalam is ABOVE threshold (passes minimum from DECISIONS 1.7). Weak Badhaka lord = diluted
  obstruction.

  Variant C — Always-active

  Badhaka lord considered active regardless of placement. Simplification.

  Variant D — Classical strict + Shadbala-strength weighting

  Strict classical conditions + strength modulation. Hybrid.

  Classical consensus

  BPHS Ch.44 strict conditions are universal. Strength-weighting is modern refinement.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (classical strict) + Shadbala weighting shown separately
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A
  - Astrosage: Variant A
  - Raman published: Variant A

  Q3 — Badhaka lord activation criteria:

  - A) Classical strict (BPHS Ch.44 v.20-25) — self-placement / dusthana / malefic affliction / dasha-period activates. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Raman. Same for
   both user types.
  - B) Strength-based (Shadbala-gated) — only strong Badhaka lords considered strongly obstructive.
  - C) Always-active — simplification.
  - D) Classical strict + Shadbala-strength weighting — hybrid. ← my recommendation (classical rigor + strength nuance via E19 inheritance).
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ D

● E27 Q3 locked: Option D — Classical strict (BPHS Ch.44 v.20-25) + Shadbala-strength weighting via E19 inheritance. Badhaka lord activation: self-placement /       
  dusthana / malefic affliction / dasha-period triggers + Shadbalam strength modulates severity. Hybrid approach — classical rigor + strength nuance. Same for both  
  user types.

  ---
  Q4 — E5b Ayus integration

  Background. Maraka-Badhaka is inherently tied to longevity analysis — Maraka dasha periods within the predicted ayus window are classically when death-timing      
  manifests. How does E27 integrate with E5b Ayus?

  Integration options

  Option A — E27 data feeds E5b dasha-window prediction

  E27 outputs flag "critical Maraka dasha periods" within the E5b ayus window. Astrologer sees combined view: "Within Pindayu 71-year window, Sani dasha period      
  2042-2061 flagged as Maraka-triggered — critical death-period window."

  Option B — E27 standalone; astrologer synthesizes manually

  E27 outputs Maraka-Badhaka analysis separately. Astrologer manually combines with E5b ayus for synthesis.

  Option C — Full integration with critical-dasha-period flagging

  E27 + E5b combined engine produces unified "critical periods timeline" showing: Ayus window + Maraka dasha periods + Badhaka activations + dasha-lord weakness     
  overlays.

  Option D — Ethical-gated integration (astrologer-only; B2C hard refusal)

  All longevity integrations follow E5b Q5 ethical gating. Astrologer workbench sees full integration. B2C AI chat hard-refuses direct Maraka-timing queries (same   
  scripted refusal as E5b).

  Option E — C + D combined

  Full integration available to astrologer; B2C hard refusal inherited.

  Ethical position

  E5b locked hard refusal for B2C. E27 inherits this by default — Maraka-timing questions are more ethically sensitive than general longevity.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option A (feeds into Ayurdaya view; astrologer-only)
  - Parashara's Light: Option B (standalone)
  - Astrologer-focused tools: Option A

  Q4 — E5b Ayus integration:

  - A) E27 feeds E5b dasha-window prediction — integrated view of critical Maraka periods within ayus window.
  - B) E27 standalone, manual synthesis — astrologer combines.
  - C) Full integration with critical-dasha-period flagging — unified timeline engine.
  - D) Ethical-gated integration — all longevity flows hard-refusal for B2C per E5b Q5.
  - E) C + D combined — full integration for astrologer + B2C hard refusal. ← my recommendation (matches E5b ethical gating + provides full astrologer tooling).     
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ A

● E27 Q4 locked: Option A — E27 feeds E5b dasha-window prediction. E27 outputs flag "critical Maraka dasha periods" within the E5b Ayus window. Astrologer workbench 
  sees integrated view: "Within Pindayu X-year window, [graha] dasha period YYYY-YYYY flagged as Maraka-triggered — critical window." Ethical gating inherited from  
  E5b Q5 (B2C hard refusal for direct longevity-timing queries).

A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E24 Janma-Tara 9-Cycle — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE)                                                                                                             
                             
  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Tamil Parashari daily-practice tool. User opens app → AI chat answers "Is today good for starting a project?" — analyzes today's natchathiram counted from user's    natal Chandran natchathiram, classifies into one of 9 Tara categories (Janma/Sampat/Vipat/Kshema/Pratyari/Sadhaka/Vadha/Mitra/Ati-Mitra), reports quality.           Astrologer workbench shows Tara calendar for any date range. Heavily used in Tamil/South-Indian muhurtham.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Step B — Calculation pipeline (E24 simple compute)                                                                                                                 

  1. Natal chart computed → natal Chandran natchathiram recorded
  2. Query natchathiram at target moment (could be today, any date)
  3. E24 Tara computation: count position from natal natchathiram to query natchathiram (1-27)
    - Position mod 9 → Tara category (1-9)
    - Position / 9 → cycle (1st, 2nd, 3rd)
  4. Output: Tara classification + classical significance + cycle indicator

  The 9-Tara cycle

  ┌──────────┬───────────────────────┬──────────┬──────────────────────────────────┐
  │ Position │    Tara (Sanskrit)    │  Tamil   │        Classical meaning         │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   1st    │ Janma Tara            │ ஜன்ம தாரை   │ Natal star — caution             │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   2nd    │ Sampat Tara           │ ஸம்பத் தாரை  │ Wealth, prosperity               │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   3rd    │ Vipat Tara            │ விபத் தாரை   │ Obstacles, dangers               │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   4th    │ Kshema Tara           │ க்ஷேம தாரை   │ Well-being                       │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   5th    │ Pratyari Tara         │ ப்ரத்யரி தாரை │ Enemies, opposition              │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   6th    │ Sadhaka Tara          │ சாதக தாரை   │ Success, achievement             │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   7th    │ Vadha / Naidhana Tara │ வத தாரை    │ Death, destruction — most severe │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   8th    │ Mitra Tara            │ மித்ர தாரை   │ Friends, supporters              │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤
  │   9th    │ Ati-Mitra Tara        │ அதிமித்ர தாரை │ Great friends, supreme support   │
  └──────────┴───────────────────────┴──────────┴──────────────────────────────────┘

  Starting with Q1.

  ---
  Q1 — Janma-Tara classical source

  Background. Multiple classical sources describe the 9-Tara cycle. Minor variations exist.

  Classical sources

  Source A — Muhurta Chintamani Ch.3

  Rama Daivajna (16th c). Canonical Muhurta text. Most widely cited modern reference. Same 9 Tara categories + order.

  Source B — Prasna Marga Ch.4

  Kerala tradition (17th c). Same 9 Taras with minor terminology variations (Vadha ↔ Naidhana).

  Source C — Tamil Vakya Panchangam standard

  Tamil traditional usage. Slightly different daily-practice conventions but same 9-Tara categories.

  Source D — BPHS Ch.40 (transit context)

  Briefly mentions Tara cycle as transit interpretation framework. Less detailed than Muhurta Chintamani.

  Options

  Option A — Muhurta Chintamani Ch.3 canonical

  Standard modern reference. Matches DECISIONS 3.2 nakshatra-percentile source (already locked Muhurta Chintamani for Varjyam/Amrit Kala).

  Option B — Prasna Marga variant

  Kerala tradition.

  Option C — Tamil Vakya convention

  Regional Tamil standard.

  Option D — Multi-source synthesis

  Consistency with earlier locks

  DECISIONS 3.2 locked Muhurta Chintamani as default source for nakshatra-percentile tables with astrologer toggle. Extending same convention to E24 gives
  uniformity.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Muhurta Chintamani
  - Parashara's Light: Muhurta Chintamani
  - Drik Panchang: Muhurta Chintamani
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Muhurta Chintamani-derived

  Q1 — Janma-Tara classical source:

  - A) Muhurta Chintamani Ch.3 canonical — matches JH + PL + Drik + Tamil Vakya + DECISIONS 3.2 locked convention. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.    
  - B) Prasna Marga variant — Kerala tradition.
  - C) Tamil Vakya convention — regional Tamil.
  - D) Multi-source synthesis.
  - E) MC default + astrologer variant toggle — uniform convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Churned for 45s
A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E24 Q1 locked: Option A — Muhurta Chintamani Ch.3 canonical. 9-Tara categories in standard order                                                                   
  (Janma/Sampat/Vipat/Kshema/Pratyari/Sadhaka/Vadha/Mitra/Ati-Mitra). Matches JH + PL + Drik + Tamil Vakya + DECISIONS 3.2 locked convention. Same for both user
  types.                                                                                                                                                                
  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q2 — Cross-cycle severity (1st / 2nd / 3rd cycle treatment)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Background. The 9-Tara cycle repeats 3× across 27 natchathirams. When target natchathiram is 10th from natal (= 1st position in 2nd cycle = Janma Tara of 2nd        cycle), does it carry same severity as 1st cycle Janma Tara?                                                                                                       

  Classical variants

  Variant A — All 3 cycles equal severity

  Each Tara category gets same weight regardless of cycle. 1st cycle Vadha = 2nd cycle Vadha = 3rd cycle Vadha. Simple; matches most modern practice.

  Variant B — Decaying severity (classical gradient)

  1st cycle = full strength (strongest Tara effect)
  2nd cycle = 2/3 strength
  3rd cycle = 1/3 strength

  Matches some Muhurta Chintamani commentary readings — later cycles are "echoes/reflections" of first. Classically nuanced.

  Variant C — Distance-weighted severity

  Severity proportional to inverse distance from natal natchathiram. Position 1 = strongest; position 27 = weakest. Continuous rather than discrete cycles.

  Variant D — Cycle-specific treatment

  1st cycle: personal (body/immediate life)
  2nd cycle: family/social layer
  3rd cycle: karmic/distant

  Not severity gradation but CONTEXT gradation.

  Classical consensus

  - Muhurta Chintamani primary reading: Variant A (all equal) — most-used modern practice
  - Some commentaries: Variant B (decaying)
  - Tamil Vakya daily practice: Variant A

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A
  - Drik Panchang: Variant A
  - Astrosage Tara: Variant A
  - Tamil Vakya panchangam publications: Variant A

  Q2 — Cross-cycle severity:

  - A) All 3 cycles equal severity — matches JH + Drik + Astrosage + Tamil Vakya daily practice. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Decaying severity (1st full / 2nd 2/3 / 3rd 1/3) — classical commentary.
  - C) Distance-weighted continuous — inverse-distance severity.
  - D) Cycle-specific context (personal/social/karmic) — context gradation.
  - E) Variant A default + astrologer toggle to decay — uniform convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ A                                                                                                                                                                                                            
● E24 Q2 locked: Option A — All 3 cycles equal severity. 1st cycle Vadha = 2nd cycle Vadha = 3rd cycle Vadha in classical interpretation. Matches JH + Drik +        
  Astrosage + Tamil Vakya daily practice. Same for both user types.

  Q3 — Interpretation strings per Tara category                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  Background. Each Tara category has classical interpretive meaning. Like DECISIONS 1.9/1.10 BPHS Ch.6 varga purpose metadata, should Josi ship canonical              interpretation strings for UI display?                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                       Proposed canonical strings (Muhurta Chintamani + Prasna Marga synthesis)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  ┌───────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                                               
  │         Tara          │             Meaning              │                 Activity suitability                  │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Janma Tara            │ Natal star — self-focused period │ Caution; avoid new ventures                           │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sampat Tara           │ Prosperity, wealth-attracting    │ Favorable for financial activities, business launches │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Vipat Tara            │ Obstacles, danger-prone          │ Avoid travel, surgery, risky ventures                 │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Kshema Tara           │ Well-being, safety               │ Favorable for health, family activities               │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Pratyari Tara         │ Enemies, opposition              │ Avoid confrontation, competitive activities           │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sadhaka Tara          │ Success, achievement             │ Favorable for starting education, skill-building      │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Vadha / Naidhana Tara │ Death, destruction — most severe │ Strongly avoid major activities, travel, surgery      │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Mitra Tara            │ Friends, supporters              │ Favorable for social activities, networking           │
  ├───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Ati-Mitra Tara        │ Supreme support                  │ Highly favorable for all auspicious activities        │
  └───────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — Ship canonical interpretation strings with Muhurta Chintamani citation

  Each Tara has meaning string + activity-suitability string. Same for both user types. Classical Muhurta Chintamani citation per string.

  Option B — Short keyword per Tara (minimal)

  Just category name + 1-word meaning (e.g., "Vipat Tara: Obstacles"). Loses nuance.

  Option C — No interpretation strings; astrologer/user provides framing

  Category name only. No canonical meaning shipped. Astrologer adds classical context.

  Option D — Multi-source variants per Tara

  Interpretation strings from Muhurta Chintamani + Prasna Marga + Tamil Vakya commentary shown as different options. Dense.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option A (canonical meanings)
  - Drik Panchang: Option A
  - Astrosage Tara: Option A
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams printed: Option A

  Q3 — Interpretation strings per Tara:

  - A) Canonical interpretation strings with Muhurta Chintamani citation — meaning + activity-suitability per Tara. Same for both user types. Matches JH + Drik +    
  Astrosage + Tamil Vakya. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Short keyword per Tara — minimal.
  - C) No interpretation strings — category-only.
  - D) Multi-source variants — dense.
  - E) Other.

A

● E25 Ashtakoota Milan + Manglik — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE)

  Step A — User story (context only)

  User asks AI chat "Is this match compatible?" — provides partner's birth details. Josi computes 8-Koota compatibility score between user's chart and partner's     
  chart, returns 0-36 total score + per-Koota breakdown + Manglik (Kuja Dosha) check + Nadi/Rajju/Papasamya exception rules. Astrologer workbench shows full
  dual-chart compatibility panel. Heavily used across pan-Indian matchmaking tradition — often required before marriage proposals.

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E25 dual-chart input)

  1. Both charts computed (user + partner)
  2. E23 Panchadha Maitri completed (for Graha Maitri koota)
  3. E24 Janma-Tara completed (feeds Tara koota)
  4. E25 8-Koota scoring between charts, per BPHS + Muhurta Chintamani Ch.5:
    - Varna (1pt), Vashya (2pt), Tara (3pt), Yoni (4pt), Graha Maitri (5pt), Gana (6pt), Bhakoot (7pt), Nadi (8pt)
    - Total 0-36 score
  5. E25 Manglik (Kuja Dosha) check for both charts: Sevvai in 1st/2nd/4th/7th/8th/12th from Lagna (+ Chandran variant)
  6. E25 Nadi Dosha exception rules: same-rashi, same-Gotra cancellations
  7. E25 Rajju Dosha + Papasamya + Ganda Moola checks
  8. Output: compatibility summary for both user types

  ---
  Q1 — 8-Koota classical source + point weights

  Background. BPHS + Muhurta Chintamani Ch.5 define the 8-Koota matching system with specific point-weight distribution summing to 36.

  Classical 8-Koota point weights (universal)

  ┌─────┬──────────────┬──────────────┬────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  #  │    Koota     │    Tamil     │ Points │                   What it measures                   │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  1  │ Varna        │ Varna        │   1    │ Social class matching (rasi-based)                   │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  2  │ Vashya       │ Vashya       │   2    │ Control/magnetism (rasi-grouping)                    │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  3  │ Tara         │ Tara         │   3    │ Natchathiram compatibility (9-Tara from E24)         │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  4  │ Yoni         │ Yoni         │   4    │ Sexual compatibility (natchathiram animal symbolism) │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  5  │ Graha Maitri │ Graha Maitri │   5    │ Planetary friendship (Chandran-lords via E23)        │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  6  │ Gana         │ Gana         │   6    │ Temperament (Deva/Manushya/Rakshasa)                 │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  7  │ Bhakoot      │ Bhakoot      │   7    │ Rashi compatibility (positional distance)            │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  8  │ Nadi         │ Nadi         │   8    │ Pulse/health (Adi/Madhya/Antya)                      │
  ├─────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │     │ Total        │              │   36   │                                                      │
  └─────┴──────────────┴──────────────┴────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Classical sources

  Source A — Muhurta Chintamani Ch.5 + BPHS canonical

  Standard 8-Koota with 36-point weighting. Universal Indian matchmaking.

  Source B — Phaladeepika variant

  Slight point-weight adjustment (some commentators). Minor.

  Source C — Regional variants (Tamil/Bengali/Gujarati differences)

  Regional traditions may weight differently.

  Classical consensus

  Muhurta Chintamani 36-point distribution is THE universal standard. All matrimonial software use this.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Muhurta Chintamani 8-Koota
  - Parashara's Light: Muhurta Chintamani
  - Astrosage: Muhurta Chintamani
  - Matrimony-focused tools (Gun Milan): Muhurta Chintamani
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Muhurta Chintamani

  Q1 — 8-Koota classical source + point weights:

  - A) Muhurta Chintamani Ch.5 + BPHS canonical 36-point — Varna 1 + Vashya 2 + Tara 3 + Yoni 4 + Graha Maitri 5 + Gana 6 + Bhakoot 7 + Nadi 8. Matches JH + PL +    
  Astrosage + matrimony tools. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Phaladeepika variant — minor adjustments.
  - C) Regional variants — Tamil/Bengali/Gujarati differences.
  - D) MC default + astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - E) Other.

  ---
  Q2 — Manglik (Kuja Dosha) placement rules

  Background. Manglik dosha is Sevvai (Mars) placement creating marriage-affliction. Classical traditions use different house-count + reference-point combinations.  

  Variants

  Variant A — Classical strict 5 houses from Lagna (BPHS literal)

  Sevvai in 1st, 4th, 7th, 8th, 12th → Manglik. Drops 2nd house.

  Variant B — Standard 6 houses from Lagna (widely used)

  Sevvai in 1st, 2nd, 4th, 7th, 8th, 12th → Manglik. Most common modern matchmaking rule.

  Variant C — Standard 6 + Chandran reference (dual-reference)

  Check Sevvai from BOTH Lagna AND Chandran; Manglik if either reference triggers.

  Variant D — Triple-reference (Lagna + Chandran + Sukkiran)

  More conservative; Sevvai from any of 3 references. Used by some traditional matchmakers.

  Classical position

  - BPHS Ch.44: 1/2/4/7/8/12 from Lagna (Variant B)
  - Phaladeepika: 1/2/4/7/8/12 + Chandran check (Variant C)
  - Modern Indian matchmaking standard: Variant B
  - Strict Tamil Vakya matchmaking: Variant C (dual-reference)

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant B + Chandran check optional toggle
  - Parashara's Light: Variant B
  - Astrosage: Variant B + Chandran
  - Matrimony tools (Gun Milan): Variant C (most conservative — flags more Manglik charts)
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Variant C

  Q2 — Manglik placement rules:

  - A) Strict 5 houses from Lagna (1/4/7/8/12) — BPHS literal minimum.
  - B) Standard 6 houses from Lagna (1/2/4/7/8/12) — widely-used modern rule. Matches BPHS + JH + PL + Astrosage + most modern software.
  - C) 6 houses from Lagna + Chandran check (dual-reference) — Phaladeepika + conservative matrimony tradition + Tamil Vakya. ← my recommendation (most conservative;
   catches more Manglik charts; astrologer can verify).
  - D) Triple-reference (Lagna + Chandran + Sukkiran) — most conservative.
  - E) Variant B default + astrologer toggle to C/D — uniform variant convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ C

● E25 Q2 locked: Option C — Dual-reference Manglik check (Sevvai from Lagna AND Chandran). 6 houses per reference (1/2/4/7/8/12). Manglik if either reference        
  triggers. Matches Phaladeepika + conservative matrimony tradition + Tamil Vakya. Most conservative; catches more Manglik charts for astrologer verification.       

  ---
  Q3 — Manglik cancellation rules scope

  Background. Classical tradition has cancellation rules that nullify Manglik dosha. Which cancellations does Josi implement?

  Classical Manglik cancellations

  ┌─────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┬─────────────┐
  │  #  │                              Cancellation rule                              │         Classical source          │ Widely-used │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┤
  │  1  │ Mutual Manglik — both partners Manglik → dosha cancels                      │ Phaladeepika + Muhurta Chintamani │ ✓ Universal │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┤
  │  2  │ Sevvai in own sign (Mesham/Viruchigam/Magaram) → reduced severity           │ BPHS Ch.44                        │  ✓ Common   │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┤
  │  3  │ Sevvai in exaltation (Magaram 28°) → reduced                                │ BPHS                              │  ✓ Common   │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┤
  │  4  │ Sevvai aspected by benefic (Guru/Sukkiran) → reduced                        │ Phaladeepika                      │  ✓ Common   │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┤
  │  5  │ Sevvai conjunct benefic (Guru/Sukkiran) → reduced                           │ Phaladeepika                      │  ✓ Common   │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┤
  │  6  │ Age-based (post-28) — dosha diminishes after age 28                         │ Modern practice                   │   Partial   │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┤
  │  7  │ Sevvai in Rahu/Ketu rasi → altered severity                                 │ Commentary                        │    Rare     │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┤
  │  8  │ Specific yoga/combination exceptions (e.g., certain Neecha Bhanga variants) │ Rangacharya commentary            │ Specialized │
  └─────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────┴─────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — Strict 5 core cancellations (#1-#5)

  Classical strict — most widely-used matrimony cancellations. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage matchmaking.

  Option B — Core 5 + age-based (#6)

  Adds modern age-28 rule. Matches some matrimony tools.

  Option C — Comprehensive all 8 cancellations

  Full classical + commentary coverage. Most nuanced.

  Option D — Core 5 + commentary-rare (#7/#8) astrologer toggle

  Matches tiered convention — core default + astrologer optional.

  Classical consensus

  Modern matchmaking uses cancellations #1-#5 universally. #6-#8 are more variable.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Core 5
  - Parashara's Light: Core 5
  - Astrosage: Core 5 + age-based optional
  - Matrimony tools (Gun Milan, Kundli Match): Core 5 + age optional
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Core 5

  Q3 — Manglik cancellation rules scope:

  - A) Strict 5 core cancellations (#1-#5) — widely-used; matches JH + PL + Astrosage + Tamil Vakya. Same for both user types.
  - B) Core 5 + age-based (#6) — adds modern age-28 rule.
  - C) Comprehensive all 8 cancellations — full coverage.
  - D) Core 5 + commentary-rare toggle (#7/#8) — tiered convention. ← my recommendation.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ A

  Sevvai, (5) Sevvai conjunct benefic. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + matrimony tools + Tamil Vakya. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q4 — Dashakoota extension scope                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  Background. Ashtakoota is the universal 36-point compatibility. Some classical traditions extend with additional checks (Dashakoota, 10-point supplement). Plus      related marriage-compatibility refinements.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
  Dashakoota supplement (some traditions)

  Beyond Ashtakoota's 8 Kootas, adds checks:
  - Ajna Koota — command-matching
  - Vedha Koota — harmful pair-matching
  - Rajju Koota (cord-matching — birth-star position)
  - Stree-deergha (female longevity indicator)

  Plus commonly-tested additional rules NOT part of Ashtakoota:
  - Rajju Dosha — cord-based incompatibility (separately from Rajju Koota)
  - Vedha Dosha — natchathiram-pair obstruction
  - Papasamya — malefic-burden equalization

  Options

  Option A — Ashtakoota 36 only (PRD default)

  Just the 8 Kootas. Simplest. Matches most matrimony tools.

  Option B — Ashtakoota + Dashakoota extension (10 additional checks)

  Comprehensive 36+ point system. Research-tier.

  Option C — Ashtakoota 36 + selective dosha checks (Rajju/Vedha/Papasamya — NOT full Dashakoota)

  Middle ground. Most Parashari matchmakers include these 3 dosha checks alongside Ashtakoota even without full Dashakoota.

  Option D — Astrologer toggle

  Ashtakoota default; astrologer enables Dashakoota extension per profile.

  Classical position

  - BPHS + Muhurta Chintamani: Ashtakoota 36 is primary
  - Modern matchmaking: Ashtakoota + Manglik + Rajju + Vedha + Papasamya (not full Dashakoota)
  - Research/specialized: Full Dashakoota

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Ashtakoota + Manglik + Rajju/Vedha/Papasamya (Variant C)
  - Parashara's Light: Ashtakoota + Manglik + Rajju
  - Astrosage Gun Milan: Ashtakoota + Manglik + Rajju + Papasamya
  - Matrimony tools: Variant C

  Q4 — Dashakoota extension scope:

  - A) Ashtakoota 36 only (PRD default) — simplest.
  - B) Ashtakoota + full Dashakoota (10 extensions) — comprehensive research-tier.
  - C) Ashtakoota + Rajju/Vedha/Papasamya dosha checks — middle ground; matches JH + PL + Astrosage + matrimony tools. ← my recommendation (matches most modern      
  matchmaking practice).
  - D) Ashtakoota default + Dashakoota astrologer toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ C                                                                                                                                                                      
● E25 Q4 locked: Option C — Ashtakoota 36-point + Rajju Dosha + Vedha Dosha + Papasamya checks. Middle ground between pure 36-point and full Dashakoota              
  research-tier. Matches JH + PL + Astrosage + matrimony tools + Tamil Vakya standard. Same for both user types.

 A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E26 Special Lagnas Suite — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE)                                                                                                           

  Step A — User story (context only)                                                                                                                                    
  Astrologer workbench Special Lagnas panel displays 10+ specialized ascendants computed from birth data. Each Lagna provides a different analytical lens — Bhava      Lagna for life-phase, Horai Lagna for wealth, Ghati Lagna for power, Sri Lagna for prosperity, etc. Classical Parashari practice uses these in context-specific      readings (e.g., career questions consult Horai Lagna + Ghati Lagna alongside natal Lagna).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Step B — Calculation pipeline (E26 simple compute from birth data)                                                                                                 

  1. Natal chart computed
  2. Sunrise time at birth location computed (per DECISIONS 2.3 center-of-disc + refraction)
  3. E26 per-Lagna computation:
    - Time-based Lagnas (Bhava/Horai/Ghati/Vighati): position at birth time from sunrise divided by specific unit
    - Sri Lagna: based on natal Chandran position + specific offset
    - Indu Lagna, Varnada, Nisheka, Pranapada: specific classical formulas
  4. Output: 10+ Special Lagna positions + classical usage strings

 A

● E28 Upaya / Remedies Engine — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE)

  Step A — User story (context only)

  End-user asks AI chat: "My chart shows Manglik dosha — what can I do?" AI surfaces classical remedies: specific mantras (Hanuman Chalisa, Mangal mantra), gemstones
   (red coral per Sevvai), donation recommendations (red cloth, jaggery), fasting days (Tuesdays), temple visits (Murugan temples). Astrologer workbench shows full  
  Upaya recommendations per detected dosha/weak graha. This is a high-user-visible consumer feature — directly answers "why + what to do."

  Ethical framing critical: Remedies are traditional practice, not guaranteed outcomes. Avoid predatory gemstone recommendations. Proper disclaimers.

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E28 consumes all other engines' outputs)

  1. Natal chart + all analysis engines (dosha detection from E4a/E4b, strength from E19/E20, ayus context from E5b)
  2. E28 Upaya mapping:
    - For each detected dosha → map to classical remedies (Mantra + Yantra + Daan + Vrata + Rudraksha + Gemstone + Temple deity)
    - For each weak graha (Shadbalam < threshold from E19) → strength-boosting remedies
    - For each afflicted bhava → bhava-specific remedies
  3. Priority-rank recommendations (which remedies are most-cited classically)
  4. Output: structured Upaya recommendations per user context

  ---
  Q1 — Remedy categories scope

  Background. Classical Upaya spans 9 categories. Which ship MVP?

  The 9 classical Upaya categories

  ┌─────┬─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  #  │          Category           │                            Example                            │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  1  │ Mantra (sound)              │ Bija mantras per graha, stotras, prayers                      │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  2  │ Yantra (geometric)          │ Specific yantras per graha/dosha (engraved on metal)          │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  3  │ Tantra (ritual)             │ Specific ritual practices                                     │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  4  │ Daan (charitable donation)  │ Specific items per graha on specific days                     │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  5  │ Vrata (fasting/observance)  │ Weekday fasts per graha                                       │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  6  │ Rudraksha (sacred beads)    │ Per-graha mukhi count                                         │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  7  │ Gemstone (ratna)            │ Per-graha stone (ruby for Sooriyan, pearl for Chandran, etc.) │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  8  │ Temple deity / Istaladevata │ Regional temple correlations                                  │
  ├─────┼─────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  9  │ Yagna / Homa (fire ritual)  │ Specific fire ceremonies                                      │
  └─────┴─────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Scope options

  Option A — All 9 categories

  Comprehensive. Matches deep classical coverage.

  Option B — Core 5 (Mantra / Daan / Vrata / Gemstone / Temple)

  Most-requested + culturally accessible. Drops Yantra (geometric — less interactive), Tantra (advanced ritual), Rudraksha (commercial), Yagna (requires priest).    

  Option C — Core 7 (all except Tantra + Yagna — the most-ritualistic)

  Excludes rituals requiring priest involvement. Ships remedies user can self-practice.

  Option D — Tiered (core 5 default; 4 advanced research-flag)

  Tiered convention.

  Option E — Ethical-subset (exclude Gemstone to avoid predatory commerce)

  Drop Gemstone + Rudraksha (commercial categories). Classical but predatory-prone in modern practice.

  Classical + modern consensus

  Modern matchmaking/astrology platforms ship core 5-7. Gemstone + Yagna are commercial; ethical/disclaimer-heavy.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: All 9 (reference categories)
  - Parashara's Light: Core 7
  - Astrosage Remedies: All 9 (commerce-tied)
  - Drik Panchang: Core 5 (mantras + temples focus)

  Q1 — Remedy categories scope:

  - A) All 9 classical categories — comprehensive.
  - B) Core 5 (Mantra / Daan / Vrata / Gemstone / Temple) — most-requested accessible remedies.
  - C) Core 7 (exclude Tantra + Yagna — priest-required) — self-practicable.
  - D) Tiered: Core 5 default + 4 advanced astrologer-toggle — matches uniform tiered convention. ← my recommendation.
  - E) Ethical subset (exclude Gemstone + Rudraksha commerce) — non-predatory.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

                                                                                                                                                                       ---
  Q2 — Dosha/weakness-to-remedy mapping source                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  Background. For each detected dosha/weak graha, map to specific remedies across 9 categories. Which classical source drives the mapping?                                                                                                                                                                                                  Classical sources                                                                                                                                                  

  Source A — Purana + Mantra Shastra + Graha Shanti synthesis

  - Puranas (Shiva/Vishnu/Devi Bhagavata) — deity-specific remedies
  - Mantra Shastra — classical bija mantras + stotras per graha
  - Graha Shanti texts — peace-invoking rituals per dosha

  Classical depth. Widely-accepted source landscape.

  Source B — Modern astrologer compilations

  B.V. Raman's Remedial Astrology, K.N. Rao's remedies treatment, Sanjay Rath's SJVC materials. Modern synthesis integrating multiple Puranic sources.

  Source C — Regional tradition compilations

  - Tamil Siddhar tradition — South Indian folk remedies
  - North Indian Vedic — Northern temple-deity correlations
  - Kerala tradition — Prasna Marga-based remedies

  Region-specific.

  Source D — Per-dosha curation

  Pick best-attested source per specific dosha. Manglik → Hanuman tradition; Kaal Sarp → Naga worship; Pitru Dosha → ancestor rituals; etc.

  Option E — Modern astrologer synthesis default + astrologer profile toggle to Purana/regional

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Ethical + quality consideration

  Puranic sources are classical-authoritative but vast (requires curation). Modern synthesis is cleaner but risks commercial bias. Regional variants matter for      
  Josi's Tamil/pan-Indian users.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Option A (classical synthesis)
  - Parashara's Light: Option B (modern compilations)
  - Astrosage Remedies: Option B (commerce-leaning)
  - Drik Panchang: Option C (regional temple correlations)

  Q2 — Remedy mapping source:

  - A) Purana + Mantra Shastra + Graha Shanti classical synthesis — classical depth; matches JH.
  - B) Modern astrologer compilations (Raman + Rao + Rath synthesis) — cleanly organized modern practice.
  - C) Regional tradition compilations (Tamil Siddhar / North Indian / Kerala).
  - D) Per-dosha curation — pick best-attested source per dosha.
  - E) Modern synthesis default + astrologer profile toggle to Purana/regional — uniform variant convention. ← my recommendation (practical modern base + classical  
  extensibility).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Brewed for 51s

❯ A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E28 Q2 locked: Option A — Purana + Mantra Shastra + Graha Shanti classical synthesis. Classical depth; matches JH. Same for both user types.                       

  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q3 — Ethical framing for Upaya recommendations
                                                                                                                                                                       Background. Remedies have real consumer-protection concerns:                                                                                                         - Gemstone recommendations can be predatory (thousands of dollars)                                                                                                   - Some remedies are superstitious (no clinical/empirical basis)                                                                                                      - Users may make costly decisions based on AI-generated advice                                                                                                     

  Ethical framing options

  Option A — Classical "traditional practice" framing + universal disclaimer

  All remedies presented as "traditional Parashari practice" with universal disclaimer: "Remedies are offered per classical tradition and are not guaranteed to      
  produce specific outcomes. Practice them as personal choice, not as medical/financial/life advice."

  Option B — Tiered disclosure by cost

  - Free remedies (mantras, daan below ₹100, vratas, temple visits) — primary; no cost disclosure needed
  - Commercial remedies (gemstones ₹1000+, rudraksha, yagnas) — flagged with:
    - "Consult professional astrologer before purchase"
    - Classical citation required
    - "Price range" disclosure

  Option C — Strict gatekeeping

  B2C sees only free/low-cost remedies (mantras, daan, vratas, temples). Commercial remedies (gemstones, rudraksha, yagnas) astrologer-consultation-only; B2C AI chat
   declines direct recommendations.

  Option D — Predatory-risk checklist

  When AI chat recommends remedy with significant cost, prompts user with:
  - Estimated cost range
  - Alternative low-cost options
  - "Consult professional astrologer" redirect

  Option E — Multi-layer approach

  Option A disclaimer + Option B tiered disclosure + Option D checklist. Most comprehensive ethical framing.

  Regulatory/consumer-protection perspective

  Ethical framing protects Josi from consumer-protection complaints and gem industry commissions-based recommendations.

  Q3 — Ethical framing:

  - A) Classical "traditional practice" framing + universal disclaimer — minimum ethical floor.
  - B) Tiered disclosure by cost — commercial remedies flagged with warnings.
  - C) Strict gatekeeping — B2C sees free remedies only; commercial astrologer-only.
  - D) Predatory-risk checklist — cost disclosure + alternatives + consult redirect.
  - E) Multi-layer (A + B + D combined) — most comprehensive ethical framing. ← my recommendation (matches E5b ethical gating convention + consumer protection).     
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E28 Q3 locked: Option A — Classical "traditional practice" framing + universal disclaimer. Remedies presented as classical tradition, not outcome-guaranteed       
  advice. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q4 — Regional variants (temple correlations, regional remedies)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
  Background. Remedies have strong regional variation especially for temple-deity correlations:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             ┌──────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                                                                 
  │      Region      │                       Sevvai (Mars) remedy tradition                        │
  ├──────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Tamil            │ Murugan temples; specific Navagraha temples (Vaitheeswaran Koil for Sevvai) │
  ├──────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ North Indian     │ Hanuman temples; Mangal Nath rituals                                        │
  ├──────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Malayalam/Kerala │ Ayyappa + specific regional temples                                         │
  ├──────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Telugu/Andhra    │ Tirumala-adjacent tradition + regional temples                              │
  ├──────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Bengali          │ Tarapith + regional Shakti-peetham correlations                             │
  └──────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — Region auto-adapt per DECISIONS 2.1 pattern

  User's region (profile/detected) drives temple-correlation defaults:
  - Tamil users → Tamil temple recommendations
  - Hindi users → North Indian
  - Malayalam → Kerala tradition
  - Etc.

  Matches DECISIONS 2.1 regional auto-adapt for Panchangam.

  Option B — Pan-India default (one universal set)

  Single temple-correlation set (typically North Indian default). Loses regional authenticity.

  Option C — Astrologer-configurable region

  Astrologer picks region per chart.

  Option D — Display multiple regions

  Show Tamil + North Indian + Kerala correlations together. Dense.

  Modern software

  - Drik Panchang: Regional auto-adapt
  - Astrosage: Pan-India default + language-based adjustments
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: Tamil-only
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Pan-India + regional astrologer toggle

  Q4 — Regional variants:

  - A) Region auto-adapt per DECISIONS 2.1 pattern — user region → regional temple correlations. Matches DECISIONS pattern + Drik Panchang. ← my recommendation.     
  - B) Pan-India default — loses regional authenticity.
  - C) Astrologer-configurable region — per-chart.
  - D) Display multiple regions together — dense.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E28 Q4 locked: Option A — Region auto-adapt per DECISIONS 2.1 pattern. User's region drives temple-correlation defaults. Tamil → Tamil temples (Murugan, Navagraha 
  Vaitheeswaran), Hindi → North Indian (Hanuman, Mangal Nath), Malayalam → Kerala, Telugu → Tirumala-tradition, Bengali → Tarapith. Matches DECISIONS 2.1 pattern +
  Drik Panchang convention. Astrologer can override per chart.

  A

● E29 Thirumana Porutham — Pass 1 kick-off (GAP_CLOSURE)

  Step A — User story (context only)

  Tamil-region user asks AI chat about marriage match. Josi shows BOTH Ashtakoota (E25 pan-Indian 8-koota 36-point) AND Thirumana Porutham (Tamil 10-porutham).      
  Thirumana is Tamil-specific — heavily used in Tamil Nadu matrimonial tradition where Ashtakoota alone is considered insufficient. Astrologer workbench shows both  
  compatibility systems side-by-side; B2C Tamil user gets primary Thirumana view.

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E29 dual-chart input, parallel to E25)

  1. Both charts computed (user + partner)
  2. E29 10-Porutham scoring between charts:
    - Dina (Tara Koota equivalent) · Gana · Mahendra · Stree-Deergha · Yoni · Rasi · Rasyathipathi (Graha Maitri equivalent) · Vashya · Rajju · Vedha
  3. Per-porutham pass/fail or score
  4. Integration view with E25 Ashtakoota (both systems shown)
  5. Output: Tamil-tradition compatibility verdict

  The 10 Poruthams (Tamil)

  ┌─────┬────────────────────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  #  │         Tamil          │ Sanskrit equivalent │               Measures               │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  1  │ Dina Porutham          │ Tara Koota          │ Daily compatibility (9-Tara cycle)   │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  2  │ Gana Porutham          │ Gana Koota          │ Temperament (Deva/Manushya/Rakshasa) │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  3  │ Mahendra Porutham      │ —                   │ Longevity-cosmic                     │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  4  │ Stree-Deergha Porutham │ —                   │ Female longevity                     │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  5  │ Yoni Porutham          │ Yoni Koota          │ Sexual compatibility                 │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  6  │ Rasi Porutham          │ —                   │ Sign compatibility                   │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  7  │ Rasyathipathi Porutham │ Graha Maitri Koota  │ Sign-lord friendship                 │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  8  │ Vashya Porutham        │ Vashya Koota        │ Control/magnetism                    │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │  9  │ Rajju Porutham         │ Rajju Dosha overlap │ Cord/health-axis                     │
  ├─────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 10  │ Vedha Porutham         │ Vedha Dosha         │ Obstruction-pair                     │
  └─────┴────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  Q1 — Thirumana Porutham classical source

  Background. Thirumana Porutham is Tamil-tradition. Source variations:

  Source variants

  Source A — Tamil Vakya Panchangam canonical

  Standard Tamil Vakya Panchangam tradition. Universal in Tamil matchmaking. Matches Tamil Nadu government Rashtriya Panchang Tamil edition.

  Source B — Agathiyar Tamil tradition

  Tamil Siddhar lineage; some adjustments to specific poruthams.

  Source C — Northern Tamil (Chettiyar) tradition

  Specific to Chettiar community matchmaking.

  Source D — Pan-Tamil synthesis

  Modern Tamil matrimonial bureaus' synthesis.

  Classical consensus

  Tamil Vakya Panchangam is the universal source. All Tamil matchmaking software + temple-Vedic-pundits use this.

  Modern software

  - Tamil-specific matchmaking apps (Bharat Matrimony, Shaadi Tamil) — Tamil Vakya
  - Astrosage Tamil, Drik Panchang Tamil: Tamil Vakya
  - Vakya Panchangam printed editions — canonical

  Q1 — Thirumana Porutham source:

  - A) Tamil Vakya Panchangam canonical — universal Tamil tradition. Matches all Tamil matchmaking software + Tamil Nadu Rashtriya Panchang. Same for both user      
  types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Agathiyar Tamil Siddhar tradition — niche.
  - C) Northern Tamil (Chettiyar) tradition — community-specific.
  - D) Pan-Tamil synthesis — modern.
  - E) Vakya default + Agathiyar/Chettiyar variant toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

✻ Worked for 48s

❯ A

● E29 Q1 locked: Option A — Tamil Vakya Panchangam canonical. Universal Tamil tradition; matches all Tamil matchmaking software + Tamil Nadu Rashtriya Panchang. Same
   for both user types.

  ---
  Q2 — Scoring system

  Background. Tamil Thirumana Porutham scoring differs from Ashtakoota's 36-point weighted system.

  Scoring variants

  Variant A — Binary pass/fail per porutham (Tamil traditional)

  Each porutham = 0 or 1 (matched or not). Total compatibility = count out of 10. Common threshold: 6/10 for "good match."

  Pros: Matches Tamil tradition cleanly. Cons: Loses gradation.

  Variant B — Weighted scoring (Ashtakoota-style)

  Each porutham assigned classical weight summing to total points. Mahendra + Stree-Deergha higher weight (longevity emphasis).

  Pros: Nuanced. Cons: Non-traditional for Tamil practice.

  Variant C — Binary + critical-flag (Rajju/Vedha as deal-breakers)

  Each porutham binary, but Rajju Porutham and Vedha Porutham are critical — if either fails, match is "incompatible regardless of other matches."

  Pros: Matches strict Tamil tradition. Cons: Strong constraint.

  Variant D — Binary + Mahendra/Stree-Deergha priority + critical Rajju/Vedha

  Multi-tiered: binary scoring + 2 longevity-related poruthams elevated + 2 dosha-poruthams critical. Comprehensive.

  Classical position

  - Tamil Vakya tradition: Variant C (binary + critical Rajju/Vedha) is dominant
  - Modern Tamil matchmaking: Variant C with informal weight emphasis

  Modern software

  - Bharat Matrimony Tamil/Shaadi Tamil: Variant C
  - Astrosage Tamil: Variant A
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams printed: Variant C
  - Drik Panchang Tamil: Variant A

  Q2 — Scoring system:

  - A) Binary pass/fail per porutham (Tamil traditional simple) — count out of 10.
  - B) Weighted scoring (Ashtakoota-style) — non-traditional for Tamil.
  - C) Binary + critical-flag (Rajju/Vedha as deal-breakers) — strict Tamil tradition. Matches Bharat Matrimony Tamil + Tamil Vakya printed editions. ← my
  recommendation.
  - D) Multi-tiered (binary + Mahendra/Stree-Deergha priority + critical Rajju/Vedha) — most comprehensive.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

❯ C

● E29 Q2 locked: Option C — Binary pass/fail per porutham + critical-flag for Rajju and Vedha (deal-breakers if failed). Strict Tamil tradition; matches Bharat      
  Matrimony Tamil + Tamil Vakya printed editions. Same for both user types.

  ---
  Q3 — Integration with E25 Ashtakoota Milan

  Background. User has both E25 Ashtakoota (pan-Indian 8-koota 36-point) AND E29 Thirumana Porutham (Tamil 10-porutham). How are they integrated for display?        

  Integration options

  Option A — Separate panels

  E25 panel + E29 panel shown distinctly. Each system independently visible.

  Option B — Combined synthesis verdict

  AI synthesizes both systems into unified verdict: "Ashtakoota score 28/36 + Thirumana 7/10 with Rajju pass + Vedha pass = HIGHLY COMPATIBLE."

  Option C — Region auto-adapt per DECISIONS 2.1 pattern

  - Tamil-region users (auto-detected): Thirumana Porutham PRIMARY view; Ashtakoota cross-reference
  - Other regions (Hindi, Bengali, etc.): Ashtakoota PRIMARY view; Thirumana not surfaced

  Option D — Both always visible side-by-side

  Both systems shown equally regardless of region.

  Option E — Both for astrologer; region-adapt for B2C

  Astrologer always sees both. B2C region-adapts (Tamil users → Thirumana primary; others → Ashtakoota primary).

  Region-pattern consistency

  DECISIONS 2.1 + E28 Q4 lock regional auto-adapt as standard pattern. Extending to compatibility systems is consistent.

  Modern software

  - Tamil-specific (Bharat Matrimony Tamil): Thirumana primary; Ashtakoota separate
  - Pan-India (Astrosage): Ashtakoota primary; Thirumana under "Tamil module"
  - Drik Panchang Tamil edition: Both visible

  Q3 — E25/E29 integration:

  - A) Separate panels — each system independently visible.
  - B) Combined synthesis verdict — AI weaves both.
  - C) Region auto-adapt — Tamil users → Thirumana primary; others → Ashtakoota primary.
  - D) Both always visible side-by-side.
  - E) Both for astrologer; region-adapt for B2C — split by user type. ← my recommendation (matches DECISIONS 2.1 pattern + astrologer rigor).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ E

● E29 Q3 locked: Option E — Both compatibility systems for astrologer; region-adapt for B2C. Astrologer workbench always shows both Ashtakoota (E25) + Thirumana     
  Porutham (E29) side-by-side. B2C: Tamil users see Thirumana primary; other regions see Ashtakoota primary. Matches DECISIONS 2.1 regional pattern + astrologer     
  rigor.

  ---
  Q4 — Cross-source aggregation for E29 (final E29 question)

  - A) Tamil Vakya canonical only, no variants — matches strength-engine single-source pattern. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Vakya default + Agathiyar/Chettiyar variant toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - C) Full F8 aggregation — maximum tooling.
  - D) Other.

  Which?

❯ A

● E29 Q4 locked: Option A — Tamil Vakya canonical only, no variants. Matches strength-engine single-source pattern.

  ---
  Q1 — Classical source primary

  Background. Multiple classical sources describe Sudarshana Chakra. Which is primary?

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                   Source                   │                              Coverage                              │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Jataka Parijata Ch.11                      │ Most detailed Sudarshana Chakra exposition; widely-cited canonical │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sarvartha Chintamani (Venkatesh Sharma)    │ Dedicated treatment with practical examples                        │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ BPHS references                            │ Brief/scattered; foundational                                      │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Modern (B.V. Raman, K.N. Rao, Sanjay Rath) │ Use Jataka Parijata + commentary synthesis                         │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — Jataka Parijata Ch.11 canonical primary

  Most-detailed classical source. Standard reference. Matches modern Parashari practice (Raman, Rao, Rath all cite JP Ch.11).

  Option B — Sarvartha Chintamani primary

  Practical-examples-heavy; useful for astrologer reference.

  Option C — BPHS-based synthesis

  Foundational; less specific to Sudarshana.

  Option D — Multi-source synthesis with astrologer toggle

  Per uniform variant convention.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: JP Ch.11 canonical
  - Parashara's Light: JP Ch.11
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: JP Ch.11

  Q1 — Sudarshana Chakra classical source:

  - A) Jataka Parijata Ch.11 canonical primary — matches all modern software + Raman + Rao + Rath. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Sarvartha Chintamani primary — practical-examples focus.
  - C) BPHS-based synthesis — foundational only.
  - D) JP Ch.11 default + astrologer variant toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - E) Other.

  Which?

✻ Worked for 34s

❯ A                                                                                                                                                                      
● E18 Q1 locked: Option A — Jataka Parijata Ch.11 canonical primary source. Matches all modern software + Raman + Rao + Rath. Same for both user types.              

  ---                                                                                                                                                                  Q2 — Triple-agreement evaluation thresholds
                                                                                                                                                                       Background. For each bhava-matter, Sudarshana Chakra evaluates across 3 chakras (Lagna/Sooriyan/Chandran). Agreement count determines confidence:                                                                                                                                                                                         ┌───────────┬────────────────────────────────┐                                                                                                                       │ Agreement │    Classical interpretation    │                                                                                                                     
  ├───────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 3/3       │ Certain (matter highly likely) │
  ├───────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 2/3       │ Probable (matter likely)       │
  ├───────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 1/3       │ Partial (matter possible)      │
  ├───────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 0/3       │ Disagreement (matter unclear)  │
  └───────────┴────────────────────────────────┘

  How is "agreement" computed per chakra?

  Variant A — Bhava lord strength comparison

  For each bhava-matter, count chakras where the bhava lord is strong (Shadbalam ≥ threshold) → agreement count.

  Variant B — Per-chakra yoga detection

  For each bhava-matter, count chakras where bhava-relevant yogas (from E4a/E4b) are active → agreement count.

  Variant C — Aspect favorability

  For each bhava, count chakras where benefic aspects to that bhava outweigh malefic → agreement count.

  Variant D — Combined synthesis

  Composite of A+B+C — multi-factor evaluation per chakra.

  Variant E — Astrologer-configurable

  Astrologer picks which evaluation method per query.

  Classical position

  Jataka Parishata Ch.11 mentions "strong bhava lord across chakras" as primary criterion (Variant A). Modern interpretive practice uses Variant D combined
  synthesis.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: Variant A (lord-strength) primary + Variant D synthesis
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: Variant D combined
  - Parashara's Light: Variant A simplified

  Q2 — Agreement evaluation method:

  - A) Variant A — bhava lord strength comparison (Shadbalam-based) — classical primary; matches PL.
  - B) Variant B — per-chakra yoga detection — uses E4a/E4b output.
  - C) Variant C — aspect favorability — benefic-malefic count.
  - D) Variant D — combined synthesis (A+B+C) — most nuanced; matches JH + Sanjay Rath. ← my recommendation.
  - E) Astrologer-configurable per query — flexibility.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ D                                                                                                                                                                      
● E18 Q2 locked: Option D — Combined synthesis (Variant A bhava lord strength + Variant B per-chakra yoga detection + Variant C aspect favorability) for             
  triple-agreement evaluation per bhava. Most nuanced; matches JH + Sanjay Rath. Same for both user types.
                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                
  Q3 — AI tool-use scope (final E18 question)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
  Background. Per DECISIONS 1.9, AI tool-use extension get_sudarshana_reading(chart_id, bhava) returns triple-chakra agreement level + classical interpretation.       Question: API scope.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
  Tool-use scope options

  Option A — Single bhava query only

  get_sudarshana_reading(chart_id: str, bhava: int) -> SudarshanaResult

  Returns one bhava's triple-agreement evaluation.

  Option B — All-12-bhavas summary

  get_sudarshana_summary(chart_id: str) -> dict[int, SudarshanaResult]

  Returns full 12-bhava agreement matrix in one call.

  Option C — Multi-bhava batched query

  get_sudarshana_reading(chart_id: str, bhavas: list[int]) -> dict[int, SudarshanaResult]

  Allows AI chat to query multiple specific bhavas (e.g., 7+10 for "marriage + career"). Most flexible.

  Option D — Bhava + matter-specific

  get_sudarshana_reading(chart_id: str, bhava: int, matter_category: str) -> SudarshanaResult

  Bhava + matter (marriage, career, health, etc.) for context-specific evaluation.

  Option E — All API patterns supported

  A + B + C + D + astrologer-config. Comprehensive API surface.

  Practical AI chat usage

  E11a AI chat consumes Sudarshana Chakra for queries like "How likely is marriage this year?" — needs flexible per-bhava-or-matter querying.

  Q3 — AI tool-use scope:

  - A) Single bhava query only — minimal.
  - B) All-12-bhavas summary — bulk data.
  - C) Multi-bhava batched query — flexible.
  - D) Bhava + matter-specific — context-aware.
  - E) All API patterns supported (A+B+C+D) — comprehensive. ← my recommendation (matches AI tool-use richness need).
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ F                                                                                                                                                                      
● You picked F — "Other." What would you like for the AI tool-use scope? Could be:                                                                                   
  
  - A different API surface combination (e.g., only B + D, skip multi-bhava batched)                                                                                   - A different parameter shape (e.g., chart_id only with auto-determined matters)                                                                                   
  - Specific limit (e.g., only one bhava per query but cached)                                                                                                       
  - Something else entirely

  Tell me what you'd like for E18 Q3.

  South Indian rendering style (square, rasi-fixed) — for Tamil/Telugu/Malayalam/Kannada users

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ Outer ring: Lagna Chakra (1st = Karkatam)      │

  │ MEENAM   │ MESHAM   │ RISHABAM │ MITHUNAM      │

  ├──────────┤                     ├───────────────┤
  │ MAGARAM  │                     │ SIMMAM        │
  │ L7·S10·C1│                     │ L2·S5·C8      │

  │ DHANUSU  │ VIRUCHIGAM│ THULAM   │ KANYA          │
  │ L6·S9·C12│ L5·S8·C11 │ L4·S7·C10│ L3·S6·C9       │
  │          │           │ Saturn☉ex│ Mars           │
