  AI chat sample interaction

  USER: How's my career outlook this year?

  AI (synthesizing Sudarshana Chakra):
  "Strong career indications. Looking at your 10th bhava across the three classical chakras:
  - From your Ascendant: 10th lord Mars is well-placed
  - From your Sun: 10th lord Saturn is exalted
  - From your Moon: 10th lord Venus is in moderate position with Saturn supporting
  All three chakras affirm career success — classically rated CERTAIN.
  Your Saturn dasa periods 2027-2031 align with this — primary career-launch window."

  API usage examples (per Q3 options)

  Option A (single bhava):
  get_sudarshana_reading(chart_id, bhava=10)
  # → {"agreement": "3/3", "verdict": "certain", "per_chakra": {...}}

  Option B (all 12 bhavas):
  get_sudarshana_summary(chart_id)
  # → {1: {...}, 2: {...}, ..., 12: {...}}

  Option C (batched):
  get_sudarshana_reading(chart_id, bhavas=[7, 10])
  # → {7: {...}, 10: {...}}

  Option D (matter-specific):
  get_sudarshana_reading(chart_id, bhava=10, matter="career_change_2027")
  # → context-tuned interpretation including dasa overlay

  Astrologer workbench dasai panel grows from 6 columns (E1a+E1b) to up to 9-14 with Tamil and Jaimini extensions enabled per profile.

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E1c follows E1a/E1b dasa engine pattern)

  Each new dasa = independent engine implementing existing ClassicalEngine Protocol:
  - Tara Dasa: natchathiram-Tara-cycle-based timing
  - Sthira Dasa: fixed sign-based (different sequence than Chara)
  - Shoola Dasa: maraka-timing-focused
  - Drig Dasa: 9th-house dharma-focused
  - Each emits standard temporal_hierarchy shape (5 levels per DECISIONS 1.4)

  Q1 — Dasa-pack scope (which dasas ship MVP)

  Background. Per GAP_CLOSURE, E1c covers Jaimini extensions + Tara Dasa + minor kalpas. Which ship MVP?

  Per GAP_CLOSURE proposed scope

  Tier 1 — Critical (must-ship):
  - Tara Dasa (Tamil Parashari) — CRITICAL for Tamil users
  - Shoola Dasa (Jaimini) — important maraka-timing tool

  Tier 2 — Important (should-ship):
  - Sthira Dasa (Jaimini) — fixed-sign dasa
  - Drig Dasa (Jaimini) — 9th-house dharma focus

  Tier 3 — Nice-to-have:
  - Kendra Dasa, Trikona Dasa, Brahma Dasa, Niryana Shoola Dasa (Jaimini variants)

  Tier 4 — Niche kalpas (research-tier):
  - Dwadashottari (112yr), Panchottari (105yr), Shataabdika (100yr), Chathurseethi-sama (84yr), Dwisaptati-sama (72yr)

  Scope options

  Option A — All tiers (12+ dasas)

  Comprehensive. Heavy MVP scope.

  Option B — Tier 1 + 2 (4 dasas: Tara/Shoola/Sthira/Drig)

  Critical + important. Tighter MVP. Tier 3+4 deferred.

  Option C — Tier 1 only (Tara + Shoola)

  Minimum critical. Defer everything else to E1d.

  Option D — Tiered feature flags (Tier 1 always-on; Tier 2 default-on; Tier 3 astrologer-toggle; Tier 4 research-flag)

  Matches uniform tiered convention (E8b Q1, E22 Q1, etc.).

  Option E — Custom — tell me which specific dasas

  Q1 — Dasa-pack scope:

  - A) All 12+ dasas — comprehensive.
  - B) Tier 1+2 (4 dasas: Tara/Shoola/Sthira/Drig) — critical + important.
  - C) Tier 1 only (Tara + Shoola) — minimum critical.
  - D) Tiered feature flags (T1 always · T2 default · T3 astrologer-toggle · T4 research-flag) — matches uniform tiered convention. ← my recommendation.
  - E) Custom — tell me.

  Which?

  Q2 — Tara Dasa Tamil classical source

  Background. Tara Dasa is Tamil Parashari standard — based on 9-Tara cycle (from E24). Each Tara period mapped to a dasa-period with specific year allocations.     

  Classical Tamil sources

  Source A — Tamil Vakya Panchangam canonical

  Universal Tamil tradition. Year allocations per Tara per Tamil Vakya. Standard for Tamil matchmaking + daily practice.

  Source B — Agathiyar Tamil Siddhar lineage

  Specific Tamil Siddhar text-tradition. Slight allocation variants.

  Source C — Modern Tamil published synthesis

  Modern Tamil jyotish books. Synthesis of Vakya + Siddhar.

  Source D — Pan-Tamil (with North Indian Parashari overlay)

  Combines Tamil Vakya base + influences from North Indian Parashari.

  Classical position

  Tamil Vakya Panchangam is the dominant Tara Dasa source. All Tamil matchmaking + daily-practice software use this.

  Modern software

  - Tamil-specific matchmaking apps: Tamil Vakya
  - Drik Panchang Tamil: Tamil Vakya
  - Astrosage Tamil: Tamil Vakya

  Q2 — Tara Dasa Tamil source:

  - A) Tamil Vakya Panchangam canonical — universal Tamil tradition. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Agathiyar Tamil Siddhar lineage — niche.
  - C) Modern Tamil published synthesis — modern.
  - D) Pan-Tamil + North Indian overlay — combined.
  - E) Vakya default + Siddhar/Modern variant toggle — uniform variant convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

❯ A

● E1c Q2 locked: Option A — Tamil Vakya Panchangam canonical for Tara Dasa. Universal Tamil tradition; matches all Tamil matchmaking + daily-practice software. Same 
  for both user types.

  Q3 — Sthira/Shoola/Drig Dasa Jaimini source

  Background. These three Jaimini sign-based dasas trace to:

  ┌───────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │              Source               │                                   Coverage                                    │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Jaimini Upadesha Sutras           │ Primary Jaimini canon; defines all 4 Jaimini dasas (Chara/Sthira/Shoola/Drig) │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ BPHS Ch.50                        │ Parashari-lineage Jaimini treatment                                           │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Rangacharya Kalpalatha commentary │ Modern interpretive extensions                                                │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sanjay Rath SJVC                  │ Contemporary synthesis                                                        │
  └───────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Options

  Option A — BPHS Ch.50 canonical (consistent with E1b Q7)

  Matches E1b Q7 lock for Chara/Narayana/Kalachakra (BPHS canonical default). Cross-PRD consistency.

  Option B — Jaimini Upadesha Sutras canonical

  Sthira/Shoola/Drig are PRIMARILY JUS-defined. JUS-primary is more classically accurate for these specific dasas.

  Option C — BPHS + JUS synthesis (per-dasa best-source curation)

  Picks JUS or BPHS per dasa based on which source treats it most thoroughly.

  Option D — JUS canonical default + Rangacharya variant astrologer toggle

  Per uniform variant convention. JUS-primary (more accurate for Jaimini dasas) + commentary toggle.

  Classical consensus

  JUS is THE Jaimini canon. BPHS Ch.50 references JUS for these dasas. JUS-primary is the more classically defensible choice.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: JUS-primary + Rangacharya toggle
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC: JUS-primary
  - Parashara's Light: BPHS Ch.50 reading

  Q3 — Sthira/Shoola/Drig source:

  - A) BPHS Ch.50 canonical — consistent with E1b Q7 lock.
  - B) JUS canonical — primary for Jaimini dasas.
  - C) BPHS + JUS per-dasa curation — picks best per dasa.
  - D) JUS canonical default + Rangacharya variant astrologer toggle — matches JH + uniform variant convention. ← my recommendation (more accurate for Jaimini dasas 
  + consistent with E1b Q7 toggle pattern).
  - E) Other.

  Which?

    - Transit Ashtakavargam: SAV + Kaksha Vibhaga at transit moment (per E2a)

    - Chandra Bala: Moon's transit rasi position from natal Moon
    - Lattha: retrograde-back-into-natchathiram rules
    - Graha-Karaka: dasa-lord transit over natal-Karaka triggers
    - Kal Sarpa transit activations

  Background. Vedha = natchathiram-pair obstruction rules in transit. When a planet transits natchathiram X, it can be obstructed by another planet transiting paired   natchathiram Y. Classical sources differ on specific Vedha pairs.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
  Classical sources

  Source A — BPHS Ch.40 canonical (transit chapter)

  Standard Parashari treatment of gochara phala including Vedha. Most-cited.

  Source B — Phaladeepika Ch.26 (Mantreswara extensions)

  Slight variations in some natchathiram-pair definitions.

  Source C — Muhurta Chintamani Ch.4 synthesis

  Integrates BPHS + Phaladeepika.

  Source D — Tamil Vakya panchangam canonical

  Tamil daily-practice application — usually follows Phaladeepika.

  Modern software

  - Jagannatha Hora: BPHS Ch.40
  - Parashara's Light: BPHS Ch.40
  - Astrosage: BPHS Ch.40
  - Tamil Vakya panchangams: BPHS Ch.40 + Phaladeepika synthesis

  Q2 — Vedha source:

  - A) BPHS Ch.40 canonical — matches all modern software. Same for both user types. ← my recommendation.
  - B) Phaladeepika Ch.26 variant — Mantreswara extensions.
  - C) Muhurta Chintamani synthesis — combined.
  - D) Tamil Vakya canonical — Tamil practice.
  - E) BPHS default + variant astrologer toggle — uniform convention.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

A

● E17 Chart Rectification — Pass 1 kick-off (final PRD!)

  Step A — User story (context only)

  User onboarding detects "approximate birth time" (e.g., "around 5-6 AM"). Josi prompts: "Tell me 4-6 major life events with dates — marriage, career change, child 
  birth, parental death, etc. — and I'll estimate your birth time." Engine searches the candidate window, scores each candidate using 4 independent classical+modern 
  techniques, returns top 5 candidate times with confidence + per-method reasoning. Differentiating consumer feature vs competitors who require user to input exact  
  time.

  Step B — Calculation pipeline (E17 = compute-heavy)

  1. User submits: approximate birth date + candidate window (default ±4h) + K life events (date + category)
  2. E17 candidate search: 1-min resolution over window (default ±4h = 480 candidates; coarse-to-fine optimization)
  3. E17 4-method scoring per candidate:
    - Method 1 — Vimshottari Dasa-AD timing (uses E1a): for each event date, identify active MD/AD lords; score significations vs event category
    - Method 2 — Slow-transit natal-house matching (uses E6a/E6b): Jupiter/Saturn/Rahu/Ketu transits to natal houses on event date; score house-matter alignment     
    - Method 3 — Secondary progressions (Western technique): progressed planets at event date; score angular contacts
    - Method 4 — Varga consistency: Lagna lord in D9 + D10 vs reported life pattern; score
  4. Combine scores with configurable weights → composite score
  5. Return top 5 candidates with per-method breakdown
  6. 3 operating modes per PRD: Pure AI / Expert-assisted (astrologer) / Hybrid (AI shortlist + astrologer final pick)

  ---
  Q1 — 4-method scoring weights

  Background. 4 methods produce independent scores per candidate. How are they combined?

  Weighting options

  Option A — Equal weights (25% each)

  Vimshottari + Transit + Progressions + Varga each contribute 25% to composite. Simplest.

  Option B — Parashari-weighted (Vimshottari/Transit/Varga emphasized; Progressions reduced)

  - Vimshottari Dasa: 35%
  - Slow Transit: 30%
  - Varga Consistency: 25%
  - Secondary Progressions: 10% (Western technique — reduced for Parashari-primary chart)

  Aligns with Parashari classical emphasis.

  Option C — User-configurable weights per profile

  Astrologer adjusts weights per their methodology preference.

  Option D — AI-learned weights

  Train weights from historical chart-event pairs. Modern data-driven.

  Option E — Tradition-adaptive

  For Parashari-primary chart: Option B (Vimshottari emphasized). For Western chart: equal-weighted with Progressions higher.

  Modern software

  - Solar Fire Rectification: Configurable weights default Western-equal
  - Sanjay Rath SJVC Rectification: Vimshottari + Transit + Varga weighted heavier
  - Astrosage Rectification: Vimshottari emphasized

  Q1 — 4-method scoring weights:

  - A) Equal weights (25% each) — simplest.
  - B) Parashari-weighted (Vimshottari 35% + Transit 30% + Varga 25% + Progressions 10%) — matches Parashari classical emphasis. ← my recommendation (Josi is        
  Parashari-primary).
  - C) User-configurable per profile — astrologer flexibility.
  - D) AI-learned weights — data-driven.
  - E) Tradition-adaptive — Parashari-weighted for Vedic; equal for Western.
  - F) Other.

  Which?

  ---                                                                                                                                                                  E17 Q2 — Search window default (how wide to scan around reported birth time)
                                                                                                                                                                     
  Rectification needs a time window to sweep. Too narrow misses real birth time; too wide multiplies compute cost + false candidates.

  Classical lineages vary:
  - KP rectification (Krishnamurti): tight ±15–30 min, relies on cuspal sub-lord precision.
  - Parashari/traditional Tamil: ±2–4 hours is common when mother's recollection is "morning" or "around sunset."
  - Jaimini Chara Dasa rectification: often ±4–8 hours when only date is known.
  - Unknown-time charts (adopted children, old records): ±12h or full-day scan.

  Options:

  - A) ±2 hours default — assumes reported time is fairly accurate (hospital record, clock-noted). Fast compute. Fails for "morning/evening" recollections.
  - B) ±4 hours default — matches traditional Tamil astrologer practice for family-recollected births. Balanced compute. ← my recommendation (covers 80% of Indian
  diaspora cases where birth time is "pulgalkaalam" / "maalai" rather than clock-exact).
  - C) ±8 hours default — wide sweep for poorly-remembered times. Heavy compute, more false positives, but catches edge cases.
  - D) Tiered: user picks "known/approximate/unknown" → system maps to ±30min / ±4h / ±12h. Best UX but adds a UI step. B2C default = "approximate" (±4h); astrologer
   can override per case.

  Which?

✻ Baked for 1m 56s
 B

● Q2 locked → B (±4 hours default).

  ---
  E17 Q3 — Operating modes scope (which rectification modes to ship)

  The PRD lists 3 modes. Need to lock which are in-scope for v1 and which are astrologer-only vs B2C-exposed.

  Three modes:
  1. Event-based rectification — user enters 3–10 life events (marriage, job change, parent death, childbirth) with dates; system reverse-fits birth time so
  Vimshottari dasha + transits match those events. Most accurate. Needs user data entry.
  2. Physical/personality-based — user answers questionnaire (body type, temperament, health tendencies); system matches to Lagna/Moon sign indicators. Weaker       
  signal, no dates needed.
  3. Prashna-assisted — astrologer casts a Prashna chart at time of consultation; uses Prashna lagna + querent's cue to cross-check birth time. Astrologer-only      
  workflow.

  Options:

  - A) All 3 modes, all users — maximum feature surface. Risk: B2C users abandon Mode 1 (too many events to enter) and Mode 3 requires Prashna skill.
  - B) Mode 1 + Mode 2 for B2C; all 3 for astrologers. Prashna mode gated to verified astrologers only. ← my recommendation — matches the two-user-type framework;   
  Prashna is genuinely an astrologer skill.
  - C) Mode 1 only (events) — ship the highest-accuracy mode first; defer 2 & 3 to v2.
  - D) Mode 1 + Mode 3 only — skip personality (weak signal). B2C uses events; astrologers add Prashna.

  Which?

❯ B                                                                                                                                                                      
● Q3 locked → B (Modes 1+2 for B2C; all 3 for astrologers, Prashna gated to verified astrologers).                                                                   
  
  ---                                                                                                                                                                  E17 Q4 — Confidence threshold for result acceptance
                                                                                                                                                                     
  Rectification produces a ranked list of candidate birth times with scores. Need to decide when to present a result vs say "inconclusive, need more events."

  Options:

  - A) Top candidate wins (no threshold) — always return the highest-scoring time even if score is weak. Risk: users trust a bad result.
  - B) Absolute threshold ≥70% match score — only return if top candidate passes. Below threshold → "insufficient data, please add more events." ← my recommendation 
  for B2C (protects users from false certainty).
  - C) Relative threshold (top candidate ≥15% above #2) — returns a result only when it's clearly separated from alternatives. Stricter; good for astrologer mode.   
  - D) Tiered: B2C = absolute ≥70%; Astrologer = show top 3 candidates with scores + allow manual pick. Astrologer judges ambiguity themselves.

  Which?

❯ B                                                                                                                                                                      
● Q4 locked → B (absolute threshold ≥70% match score; below → "insufficient data, add more events").                                                                 
  
  ---                                                                                                                                                                  E17 Q5 — Cross-source aggregation (which classical texts inform rectification rules)                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                     
  Rectification combines signals from multiple classical methods. Need to lock the source stack + default vs toggle.

  Options:

  - A) BPHS-only — Vimshottari + Varga + Nisheka (conception-based) per Parashara Ch. 4. Pure Parashari, but ignores Jaimini Chara-dasa signals which many Tamil     
  astrologers use.
  - B) BPHS canonical default + Jaimini/KP as astrologer toggles — B2C gets pure Parashari (Vimshottari + Varga + transits). Astrologer can enable Jaimini Chara or  
  KP cuspal sub-lord as additional scoring dimensions. ← my recommendation (matches cross-cutting pattern 1.x + two-user framework).
  - C) All methods weighted together — BPHS + Jaimini + KP + Tajaka all fire always. Risk: methods disagree, scores muddy.
  - D) User picks tradition upfront — user selects "Parashari" or "KP" at start of rectification; system uses only that tradition's method.

  Which?

❯ B                                                                                                                                                                      
● Q5 locked → B (BPHS canonical default; Jaimini/KP as astrologer toggles).

      138  ### 3.1 Classical rectification techniques
